Rotherham.label: The Emphasised Bible
Rotherham.overlayPolicy: mismatch chapters stored under translationOverlays.Rotherham without altering target verse sequence
Rotherham.emptySourceRowsPolicy: empty Rotherham source rows are governed omissions and are not imported as blank text

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
text.Rotherham: Originally, was, the Word, and, the Word, was, with God; and, the Word, was, God.
text.KJV: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
text.NABRE: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
text.DRB: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and God was the Word.

text.NRSV: He was in the beginning with God.
text.Rotherham: The same, was originally, with God.
text.KJV: The same was in the beginning with God.
text.NABRE: He was in the beginning with God.
text.DRB: This was in the beginning with God.

text.NRSV: All things came into being through him, and without him not one thing came into being. What has come into being
text.Rotherham: All things, through him, came into existence, and, without him, came into existence, not even one thing: that which hath come into existence,
text.KJV: All things were made by him; and without him was not any thing made that was made.
text.NABRE: All things came to be through him, and without him nothing came to be. What came to be
text.DRB: All things were made by him: and without him was made nothing. That which was made,

text.NRSV: in him was life, and the life was the light of all people.
text.Rotherham: in him, was, life, and, the life, was, the light of men.—
text.KJV: In him was life; and the life was the light of men.
text.NABRE: through him was life, and this life was the light of the human race;
text.DRB: in him was life, and the life was the light of men:

text.NRSV: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness did not overcome it.
text.Rotherham: And, the light, in the darkness, shineth; and, the darkness, thereof, laid not hold.
text.KJV: And the light shineth in darkness; and the darkness comprehended it not.
text.NABRE: the light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.
text.DRB: and the light shineth in darkness, and the darkness did not comprehend it.

text.NRSV: There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.
text.Rotherham: There arose a man, sent from God, whose name was, John:
text.KJV: There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.
text.NABRE: A man named John was sent from God.
text.DRB: There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.

text.NRSV: He came as a witness to testify to the light, so that all might believe through him.
text.Rotherham: The same, came, for a witness, That he might bear witness, concerning the light, that, all, might believe, through him.
text.KJV: The same came for a witness, to bear witness of the Light, that all men through him might believe.
text.NABRE: He came for testimony, to testify to the light, so that all might believe through him.
text.DRB: This man came for testimony; to give testimony of the light, that all might believe through him.

text.NRSV: He himself was not the light, but he came to testify to the light.
text.Rotherham: He, was not the light,—but, that he might bear witness concerning the light,
text.KJV: He was not that Light, but was sent to bear witness of that Light.
text.NABRE: He was not the light, but came to testify to the light.
text.DRB: He was not the light, but to give testimony of the light.

text.NRSV: The true light, which enlightens everyone, was coming into the world.
text.Rotherham: It, was—The real light that enlighteneth every man—Coming into the world.
text.KJV: That was the true Light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.
text.NABRE: The true light, which enlightens everyone, was coming into the world.
text.DRB: It was the true light, which lighteneth every man that cometh into this world.

text.NRSV: He was in the world, and the world came into being through him; yet the world did not know him.
text.Rotherham: In the world, he was, and, the world, through him, came into existence, and, the world, knew him not.
text.KJV: He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not.
text.NABRE: He was in the world, and the world came to be through him, but the world did not know him.
text.DRB: He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not.

text.NRSV: He came to what was his own, and his own people did not accept him.
text.Rotherham: Into his own possessions, he came, and, his own people, received him not home.
text.KJV: He came unto his own, and his own received him not.
text.NABRE: He came to what was his own, but his own people did not accept him.
text.DRB: He came into his own, and his own received him not.

text.NRSV: But to all who received him, who believed in his name, he gave power to become children of God,
text.Rotherham: But, as many as did receive him, he gave, unto them, authority, children of God, to become,—unto them who were believing on his name:
text.KJV: But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name:
text.NABRE: But to those who did accept him he gave power to become children of God, to those who believe in his name,
text.DRB: But as many as received him, he gave them power to be made the sons of God, to those that believe in his name.

text.NRSV: who were born, not of blood or of the will of the flesh or of the will of man, but of God.
text.Rotherham: Who—not of bloods, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but—of God, were born.
text.KJV: Which were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.
text.NABRE: who were born not by natural generation nor by human choice nor by a man’s decision but of God.
text.DRB: Who, not of blood, nor of the will of flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God are born.

text.NRSV: And the Word became flesh and lived among us, and we have seen his glory, the glory as of a father’s only son, full of grace and truth.
text.Rotherham: And, the Word, became, flesh, and pitched his tent among us, and we gazed upon his glory,—a glory, as an Only-begotten from his Father. Full of favour and truth.
text.KJV: And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth.
text.NABRE: And the Word became flesh and made his dwelling among us, and we saw his glory, the glory as of the Father’s only Son, full of grace and truth.
text.DRB: And The Word Was Made Flesh, and dwelt in us (and we saw the glory of him, glory as it were of the Only-begotten of the Father) full of grace and verity.

text.NRSV: (John testified to him and cried out, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me ranks ahead of me because he was before me.’”)
text.Rotherham: (John beareth witness concerning him, and hath cried aloud, saying—the same, was he that said—He who, after me, was coming, before me, hath advanced; because, my Chief, was he.)
text.KJV: John bare witness of him, and cried, saying, This was he of whom I spake, He that cometh after me is preferred before me: for he was before me.
text.NABRE: John testified to him and cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘The one who is coming after me ranks ahead of me because he existed before me.’”
text.DRB: John giveth testimony of him, and cryeth saying: This was he of whom I spake, He that shall come after me, is made before me: because he was before me.

text.NRSV: From his fullness we have all received, grace upon grace.
text.Rotherham: Because, out of his fulness, we all, received, even favour over against favour.
text.KJV: And of his fulness have all we received, and grace for grace.
text.NABRE: From his fullness we have all received, grace in place of grace,
text.DRB: And of his fullness we all have received, and grace for grace.

text.NRSV: The law indeed was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Because, the law, through Moses, was given, favour and truth, through Jesus Christ, came into existence.
text.KJV: For the law was given by Moses, but grace and truth came by Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: because while the law was given through Moses, grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: For the law was given by Moyses, grace and verity was made by Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: No one has ever seen God. It is God the only Son, who is close to the Father’s heart, who has made him known.
text.Rotherham: No one, hath seen, God, at any time: An Only Begotten God, The One existing within the bosom of the Father, He, hath interpreted [him].
text.KJV: No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared him.
text.NABRE: No one has ever seen God. The only Son, God, who is at the Father’s side, has revealed him.
text.DRB: God no man hath seen at any time: the only-begotten Son which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared.

text.NRSV: This is the testimony given by John when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the witness of John, when the Jews sent forth unto him, out of Jerusalem, priests and Levites,—that they might question him—Who art, thou?
text.KJV: And this is the record of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, Who art thou?
text.NABRE: And this is the testimony of John. When the Jews from Jerusalem sent priests and Levites [to him] to ask him, “Who are you?”
text.DRB: And this is John's testimony, when the Jews sent from Jerusalem Priests and Levites to him, that they should ask him, who art thou?

text.NRSV: He confessed and did not deny it, but confessed, “I am not the Messiah.”
text.Rotherham: and he confessed, and did not deny,—and he confessed—I, am not, the Christ;
text.KJV: And he confessed, and denied not; but confessed, I am not the Christ.
text.NABRE: he admitted and did not deny it, but admitted, “I am not the Messiah.”
text.DRB: And he confessed, and did not deny: and he confessed, That I am not Christ.

text.NRSV: And they asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you the prophet?” He answered, “No.”
text.Rotherham: and they questioned him—What then? Art, thou, Elijah? and he saith—I am not; The prophet, art, thou? and he answered—No!
text.KJV: And they asked him, What then? Art thou Elias? And he saith, I am not. Art thou that prophet? And he answered, No.
text.NABRE: So they asked him, “What are you then? Are you Elijah?” And he said, “I am not.” “Are you the Prophet?” He answered, “No.”
text.DRB: And they asked him: What then? Art thou Elias? And he said: I am not. Art thou the Prophet? And he answered: No.

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “Who are you? Let us have an answer for those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
text.Rotherham: So they said unto him—Who, art thou? that, an answer, we may give, unto them who sent us,—What sayest thou, concerning thyself?
text.KJV: Then said they unto him, Who art thou? that we may give an answer to them that sent us. What sayest thou of thyself?
text.NABRE: So they said to him, “Who are you, so we can give an answer to those who sent us? What do you have to say for yourself?”
text.DRB: They said therefore unto him: Who art thou, that we may give an answer to them that sent us? what sayest thou of thyself?

text.NRSV: He said, “I am the voice of one crying out in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’” as the prophet Isaiah said.
text.Rotherham: He said—I, am a Voice, of one crying aloud;—In the desert, make ye straight, the way of the Lord: according as said Isaiah the prophet.
text.KJV: He said, I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, Make straight the way of the Lord, as said the prophet Esaias.
text.NABRE: He said: “I am ‘the voice of one crying out in the desert, “Make straight the way of the Lord,”’ as Isaiah the prophet said.”
text.DRB: He said: I am the voice of one crying in the desert, make straight the way of our Lord, as Esaie the Prophet said.

text.NRSV: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.
text.Rotherham: And they had been sent forth from among the Pharisees;
text.KJV: And they which were sent were of the Pharisees.
text.NABRE: Some Pharisees were also sent.
text.DRB: And they that were sent were of the Pharisees.

text.NRSV: They asked him, “Why then are you baptizing if you are neither the Messiah, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?”
text.Rotherham: and they questioned him, and said unto him—Why, then, dost thou immerse,—if, thou, art not, the Christ, nor Elijah, nor, the Prophet?
text.KJV: And they asked him, and said unto him, Why baptizest thou then, if thou be not that Christ, nor Elias, neither that prophet?
text.NABRE: They asked him, “Why then do you baptize if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?”
text.DRB: And they asked him, and said to him: Why then doest thou baptize, if thou be not Christ, nor Elias, nor the Prophet?

text.NRSV: John answered them, “I baptize with water. Among you stands one whom you do not know,
text.Rotherham: John answered them, saying—I, immerse, in water. In the midst of you, standeth one, whom, ye, know not;—
text.KJV: John answered them, saying, I baptize with water: but there standeth one among you, whom ye know not;
text.NABRE: John answered them, “I baptize with water; but there is one among you whom you do not recognize,
text.DRB: John answered them, saying: I baptize in water; but there hath stood in the midst of you whom you know not.

text.NRSV: the one who is coming after me; I am not worthy to untie the thong of his sandal.”
text.Rotherham: after me, coming:—Of whom, I, am not worthy that I should unloose the thong of the sandal.
text.KJV: He it is, who coming after me is preferred before me, whose shoe’s latchet I am not worthy to unloose.
text.NABRE: the one who is coming after me, whose sandal strap I am not worthy to untie.”
text.DRB: The same is he that shall come after me, that is made before me; whose latchet of his shoe I am not worthy to loose.

text.NRSV: This took place in Bethany across the Jordan where John was baptizing.
text.Rotherham: These things, in Bethany, came to pass, beyond the Jordan, where John was, immersing.
text.KJV: These things were done in Bethabara beyond Jordan, where John was baptizing.
text.NABRE: This happened in Bethany across the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
text.DRB: These things were done in Bethania beyond Jordan, where John was baptizing.

text.NRSV: The next day he saw Jesus coming toward him and declared, “Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, he beholdeth Jesus, coming unto him, and saith—See! the Lamb of God, who taketh away the sin of the world.
text.KJV: The next day John seeth Jesus coming unto him, and saith, Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.
text.NABRE: John the Baptist’s Testimony to Jesus. The next day he saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world.
text.DRB: The next day John saw Jesus coming to him, and he saith: Behold the lamb of God, behold him that taketh away the sin of the world.

text.NRSV: This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who ranks ahead of me because he was before me.’
text.Rotherham: This, is he, of whom, I, said—After me, cometh a man, who, before me, hath advanced, because, my Chief, was he.
text.KJV: This is he of whom I said, After me cometh a man which is preferred before me: for he was before me.
text.NABRE: He is the one of whom I said, ‘A man is coming after me who ranks ahead of me because he existed before me.’
text.DRB: This is he of whom I said: After me there cometh a man, which is made before me: because he was before me.

text.NRSV: I myself did not know him; but I came baptizing with water for this reason, that he might be revealed to Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And, I, knew him not, but, that he might be manifested unto Israel, therefore, came, I, in water, immersing.
text.KJV: And I knew him not: but that he should be made manifest to Israel, therefore am I come baptizing with water.
text.NABRE: I did not know him, but the reason why I came baptizing with water was that he might be made known to Israel.”
text.DRB: And I knew him not, but that he may be manifested in Israel, therefore came I baptizing in water.

text.NRSV: And John testified, “I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it remained on him.
text.Rotherham: And John bare witness, saying—I have gazed upon the Spirit, descending like a dove, out of heaven,—and it abode upon him.
text.KJV: And John bare record, saying, I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it abode upon him.
text.NABRE: John testified further, saying, “I saw the Spirit come down like a dove from the sky and remain upon him.
text.DRB: And John gave testimony, saying: That I saw the Spirit descending as a dove from Heaven, and he remained upon him.

text.NRSV: I myself did not know him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, ‘He on whom you see the Spirit descend and remain is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’
text.Rotherham: And, I, knew him not,—but, he that sent me to immerse in water, he, unto me, said—Upon whomsoever thou shalt see the Spirit descending and abiding upon him, the same, is he that immerseth in Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: And I knew him not: but he that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: I did not know him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water told me, ‘On whomever you see the Spirit come down and remain, he is the one who will baptize with the holy Spirit.’
text.DRB: And I knew him not: but he that sent me to baptize in water, he said to me: He upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending and remaining upon him, he it is that baptizeth in the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: And I myself have seen and have testified that this is the Son of God.”
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, have seen, and borne witness—That, this, is, the, Son of God.
text.KJV: And I saw, and bare record that this is the Son of God.
text.NABRE: Now I have seen and testified that he is the Son of God.” The First Disciples.
text.DRB: And I saw; and I gave testimony that this is the Son of God.

text.NRSV: The next day John again was standing with two of his disciples,
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, again, was John standing, and, from among his disciples, two;
text.KJV: Again the next day after John stood, and two of his disciples;
text.NABRE: The next day John was there again with two of his disciples,
text.DRB: The next day again John stood, and two of his Disciples.

text.NRSV: and as he watched Jesus walk by, he exclaimed, “Look, here is the Lamb of God!”
text.Rotherham: and, looking at Jesus walking, he saith—See! the Lamb of God!
text.KJV: And looking upon Jesus as he walked, he saith, Behold the Lamb of God!
text.NABRE: and as he watched Jesus walk by, he said, “Behold, the Lamb of God.”
text.DRB: And beholding Jesus walking, he saith: Behold the Lamb of God.

text.NRSV: The two disciples heard him say this, and they followed Jesus.
text.Rotherham: and the two disciples hearkened unto him speaking, and they followed Jesus.
text.KJV: And the two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
text.NABRE: The two disciples heard what he said and followed Jesus.
text.DRB: And the two Disciples heard him speaking, and they followed Jesus.

text.NRSV: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he said to them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which translated means Teacher), “where are you staying?”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, turning, and looking at them following, saith unto them—What seek ye? And, they, said unto him—Rabbi! which meaneth, when translated, Teacher, Where abidest thou?
text.KJV: Then Jesus turned, and saw them following, and saith unto them, What seek ye? They said unto him, Rabbi, (which is to say, being interpreted, Master,) where dwellest thou?
text.NABRE: Jesus turned and saw them following him and said to them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which translated means Teacher), “where are you staying?”
text.DRB: And Jesus turning, and seeing them following him, saith to them, What seek you? Who said to him: Rabbi (which is called by interpretation, Master) where dwellest thou?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Come and see.” They came and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four o’clock in the afternoon.
text.Rotherham: He saith unto them—Be coming, and ye shall see. They came, therefore, and saw where he abode, and, with him, they abode that day. It was about the tenth, hour.
text.KJV: He saith unto them, Come and see. They came and saw where he dwelt, and abode with him that day: for it was about the tenth hour.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Come, and you will see.” So they went and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about four in the afternoon.
text.DRB: He saith to them: Come and see. They came, and saw where he abode, and they tarried with him that day: and it was about the tenth hour.

text.NRSV: One of the two who heard John speak and followed him was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
text.Rotherham: One of the two that heard from John and followed him, was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
text.KJV: One of the two which heard John speak, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
text.NABRE: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the two who heard John and followed Jesus.
text.DRB: And Andrew the brother of Simon Peter was one of the two that had heard of John, and followed him.

text.NRSV: He first found his brother Simon and said to him, “We have found the Messiah” (which is translated Anointed).
text.Rotherham: The same findeth, first, his own brother Simon, and saith unto him—We have found the Messiah! which is, when translated, Anointed.
text.KJV: He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.
text.NABRE: He first found his own brother Simon and told him, “We have found the Messiah” (which is translated Anointed).
text.DRB: He findeth first his brother Simon, and saith to him: We have found Messias, which is being interpreted, Christ.

text.NRSV: He brought Simon to Jesus, who looked at him and said, “You are Simon son of John. You are to be called Cephas” (which is translated Peter).
text.Rotherham: He led him unto Jesus. Jesus, looking at him, said—Thou, art Simon, the son of John,—thou, shalt be called, Cephas; which is to be translated, Peter.
text.KJV: And he brought him to Jesus. And when Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone.
text.NABRE: Then he brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, “You are Simon the son of John; you will be called Cephas” (which is translated Peter).
text.DRB: And he brought him to Jesus. And Jesus looking upon him, said: Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is interpreted, Peter.

text.NRSV: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, “Follow me.”
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, he desired to go forth into Galilee. And Jesus findeth Philip, and saith unto him—Be following me!
text.KJV: The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee, and findeth Philip, and saith unto him, Follow me.
text.NABRE: The next day he decided to go to Galilee, and he found Philip. And Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
text.DRB: On the morrow he would go forth into Galilee, and he findeth Philippe. And Jesus saith to him: Follow me.

text.NRSV: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
text.Rotherham: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.
text.KJV: Now Philip was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
text.NABRE: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the town of Andrew and Peter.
text.DRB: And Philippe was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.

text.NRSV: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, “We have found him about whom Moses in the law and also the prophets wrote, Jesus son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
text.Rotherham: Philip, findeth, Nathanael, and saith unto him—Him, of whom wrote Moses in the law, and the Prophets, have we found.—Jesus, son of Joseph, him from Nazareth!
text.KJV: Philip findeth Nathanael, and saith unto him, We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, did write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.
text.NABRE: Philip found Nathanael and told him, “We have found the one about whom Moses wrote in the law, and also the prophets, Jesus son of Joseph, from Nazareth.”
text.DRB: Philippe findeth Nathanael, and saith to him: Him whom Moyses in the law, and the Prophets wrote of, we have found, Jesus the son of Joseph, of Nazareth.

text.NRSV: Nathanael said to him, “Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
text.Rotherham: And Nathanael said unto him—Out of Nazareth, can any good thing come? Philip saith unto him—Come, and see!
text.KJV: And Nathanael said unto him, Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth? Philip saith unto him, Come and see.
text.NABRE: But Nathanael said to him, “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
text.DRB: And Nathanael said to him: From Nazareth can there be any good? Philippe saith to him: Come and see.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said of him, “Here is truly an Israelite in whom there is no deceit!”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saw Nathanael coming unto him, and saith concerning him—See! Truly, an Israelite, in whom is no guile.
text.KJV: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and saith of him, Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile!
text.NABRE: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said of him, “Here is a true Israelite. There is no duplicity in him.”
text.DRB: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and he saith of him: Behold an Israelite in very deed, in whom there is no guile.

text.NRSV: Nathanael asked him, “Where did you get to know me?” Jesus answered, “I saw you under the fig tree before Philip called you.”
text.Rotherham: Nathanael saith unto him—Whence, dost thou, know, me? Jesus answered, and said unto him—Before Philip called thee,—when thou wast under the fig-tree, I saw thee.
text.KJV: Nathanael saith unto him, Whence knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said unto him, Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.
text.NABRE: Nathanael said to him, “How do you know me?” Jesus answered and said to him, “Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.”
text.DRB: Nathanael saith to him: How knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said to him: Before that Philippe did call thee, when thou wast under the figtree, I saw thee.

text.NRSV: Nathanael replied, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!”
text.Rotherham: Nathanael answered him—Rabbi! thou, art, the Son of God: Thou, art, King, of Israel.
text.KJV: Nathanael answered and saith unto him, Rabbi, thou art the Son of God; thou art the King of Israel.
text.NABRE: Nathanael answered him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God; you are the King of Israel.”
text.DRB: Nathanael answered him and saith: Rabbi, thou art the Son of God, thou art the King of Israel.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig-tree, believest thou? A greater thing than these, shalt thou see!
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto him, Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to him, “Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than this.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered, and said to him: Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the figtree, thou believest; greater than these things shalt thou see.

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “Very truly, I tell you, you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man.”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto him—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Ye shall see heaven—when set open, and, the messengers of God, ascending and descending unto the Son of Man.
text.KJV: And he saith unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.
text.NABRE: And he said to him, “Amen, amen, I say to you, you will see the sky opened and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”
text.DRB: And he saith to him: Amen Amen I say to you, You shall see the Heaven opened, and the Angels of God ascending and descending, upon the Son of man.

text.NRSV: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
text.Rotherham: And, on the third day, a marriage, took place, in Cana, of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there,—
text.KJV: And the third day there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there:
text.NABRE: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana in Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
text.DRB: And the third day there was a marriage made in Cana of Galilee: and the mother of Jesus was there.

text.NRSV: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited to the wedding.
text.Rotherham: and Jesus also was invited, with his disciples, unto the marriage.
text.KJV: And both Jesus was called, and his disciples, to the marriage.
text.NABRE: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the wedding.
text.DRB: And Jesus also was called, and his Disciples to the marriage.

text.NRSV: When the wine gave out, the mother of Jesus said to him, “They have no wine.”
text.Rotherham: And, wine falling short, the mother of Jesus saith unto him—Wine, have they none!
text.KJV: And when they wanted wine, the mother of Jesus saith unto him, They have no wine.
text.NABRE: When the wine ran short, the mother of Jesus said to him, “They have no wine.”
text.DRB: And the wine failing, the mother of Jesus saith to him: They have no wine.

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to her, “Woman, what concern is that to you and to me? My hour has not yet come.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto her—What part can I take with thee, O woman? Not yet, hath come, mine hour.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.
text.NABRE: [And] Jesus said to her, “Woman, how does your concern affect me? My hour has not yet come.”
text.DRB: And Jesus saith to her: What is to me and thee woman? My hour cometh not yet.

text.NRSV: His mother said to the servants, “Do whatever he tells you.”
text.Rotherham: His mother saith unto them who are ministering—Whatsoever he may say to you, do!
text.KJV: His mother saith unto the servants, Whatsoever he saith unto you, do it.
text.NABRE: His mother said to the servers, “Do whatever he tells you.”
text.DRB: His mother saith to the ministers: Whatsoever he shall say to you, do ye.

text.NRSV: Now standing there were six stone water jars for the Jewish rites of purification, each holding twenty or thirty gallons.
text.Rotherham: Now there were there, six stone water-vessels, placed, according to the purification of the Jews; holding each, two or three measures.
text.KJV: And there were set there six waterpots of stone, after the manner of the purifying of the Jews, containing two or three firkins apiece.
text.NABRE: Now there were six stone water jars there for Jewish ceremonial washings, each holding twenty to thirty gallons.
text.DRB: And there were set there six water-pots of stone, according to the purification of the Jews, holding every one two or three measures.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Fill the jars with water.” And they filled them up to the brim.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto them—Fill the vessels with water. And they filled them up to the brim.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto them, Fill the waterpots with water. And they filled them up to the brim.
text.NABRE: Jesus told them, “Fill the jars with water.” So they filled them to the brim.
text.DRB: Jesus saith to them: Fill the water-pots with water. And they filled them up to the top.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Now draw some out, and take it to the chief steward.” So they took it.
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Draw out now, and be bearing unto the master of the feast. And they bare.
text.KJV: And he saith unto them, Draw out now, and bear unto the governor of the feast. And they bare it.
text.NABRE: Then he told them, “Draw some out now and take it to the headwaiter.” So they took it.
text.DRB: And Jesus saith to them: Draw now, and carry to the chief steward. And they carried it.

text.NRSV: When the steward tasted the water that had become wine, and did not know where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew), the steward called the bridegroom
text.Rotherham: And, when the master of the feast had tasted the water, now made, wine, and knew not whence it was,—though, they who were ministering, knew, even they who had drawn out the water, the master accosteth the bridegroom,
text.KJV: When the ruler of the feast had tasted the water that was made wine, and knew not whence it was: (but the servants which drew the water knew;) the governor of the feast called the bridegroom,
text.NABRE: And when the headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine, without knowing where it came from (although the servers who had drawn the water knew), the headwaiter called the bridegroom
text.DRB: And after the chief steward tasted the water made wine, and knew not whence it was, but the ministers knew that had drawn the water; the chief steward calleth the bridegroom,

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and then the inferior wine after the guests have become drunk. But you have kept the good wine until now.”
text.Rotherham: and saith unto him—Every man, first the good wine, setteth out; and, when they had been well-supplied, the inferior:—Thou, hast kept the good wine until, even now.
text.KJV: And saith unto him, Every man at the beginning doth set forth good wine; and when men have well drunk, then that which is worse: but thou hast kept the good wine until now.
text.NABRE: and said to him, “Everyone serves good wine first, and then when people have drunk freely, an inferior one; but you have kept the good wine until now.”
text.DRB: and saith to him: Every man first setteth the good wine, and when they have well drunk, then that which is worse. But thou hast kept the good wine until now.

text.NRSV: Jesus did this, the first of his signs, in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.
text.Rotherham: This, beginning of his signs, did Jesus, in Cana of Galilee, and manifested his glory; and his disciples believed on him.
text.KJV: This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee, and manifested forth his glory; and his disciples believed on him.
text.NABRE: Jesus did this as the beginning of his signs in Cana in Galilee and so revealed his glory, and his disciples began to believe in him.
text.DRB: This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee: and he manifested his glory, and his Disciples believed in him.

text.NRSV: After this he went down to Capernaum with his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they remained there a few days.
text.Rotherham: After this, he came down into Capernaum,—he, and his mother and brethren, and his disciples; and, there, they abode, not many days.
text.KJV: After this he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, and his brethren, and his disciples: and they continued there not many days.
text.NABRE: After this, he and his mother, [his] brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there only a few days.
text.DRB: After this he went down to Capharnaum himself and his mother, and his brethren, and his Disciples; and there they remained not many days.

text.NRSV: The Passover of the Jews was near, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, near, was the passover of the Jews; and, Jesus, went up unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And the Jews’ passover was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem,
text.NABRE: Cleansing of the Temple. Since the Passover of the Jews was near, Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And the Pasch of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem:

text.NRSV: In the temple he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves, and the money changers seated at their tables.
text.Rotherham: And he found, in the temple, them that were selling oxen and sheep and doves, also the money-changers sitting.
text.KJV: And found in the temple those that sold oxen and sheep and doves, and the changers of money sitting:
text.NABRE: He found in the temple area those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves, as well as the money-changers seated there.
text.DRB: and he found in the Temple them that sold oxen and sheep and doves, and the bankers sitting.

text.NRSV: Making a whip of cords, he drove all of them out of the temple, both the sheep and the cattle. He also poured out the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.
text.Rotherham: And, making a scourge out of rushes, all of them, thrust he forth out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and, the money-changers’ small coins, poured he forth, and, the tables, he overturned;
text.KJV: And when he had made a scourge of small cords, he drove them all out of the temple, and the sheep, and the oxen; and poured out the changers’ money, and overthrew the tables;
text.NABRE: He made a whip out of cords and drove them all out of the temple area, with the sheep and oxen, and spilled the coins of the money-changers and overturned their tables,
text.DRB: And when he had made as it were a whip of little cords, he cast them all out of the temple, the sheep also and the oxen, and the money of the bankers he poured out, and the tables he overthrew.

text.NRSV: He told those who were selling the doves, “Take these things out of here! Stop making my Father’s house a marketplace!”
text.Rotherham: and unto them who were selling, the doves, he said—Take these things hence! Be not making, the house of my Father, a house of merchandise.
text.KJV: And said unto them that sold doves, Take these things hence; make not my Father’s house an house of merchandise.
text.NABRE: and to those who sold doves he said, “Take these out of here, and stop making my Father’s house a marketplace.”
text.DRB: And to them that sold doves, he said: Take away these things hence, and make not the house of my Father, a house of merchandise.

text.NRSV: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will consume me.”
text.Rotherham: His disciples remembered that it was written—The zeal of thy house, eateth me up.
text.KJV: And his disciples remembered that it was written, The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up.
text.NABRE: His disciples recalled the words of scripture, “Zeal for your house will consume me.”
text.DRB: And his Disciples remembered that it is written: The zeal of thy house hath eaten me.

text.NRSV: The Jews then said to him, “What sign can you show us for doing this?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews therefore answered, and said unto him, What sign, dost thou point out to us, in that, these things, thou doest?
text.KJV: Then answered the Jews and said unto him, What sign shewest thou unto us, seeing that thou doest these things?
text.NABRE: At this the Jews answered and said to him, “What sign can you show us for doing this?”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore answered and said to him: What sign doest thou shew us, that thou doest these things?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—Take down this shrine, and, in three days, will I raise it.
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto them, Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to them, “Destroy this temple and in three days I will raise it up.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered and said to them: Dissolve this temple, and in three days I will raise it.

text.NRSV: The Jews then said, “This temple has been under construction for forty-six years, and will you raise it up in three days?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, said—In forty and six years, was this shrine built. And, thou, in three days, wilt raise it!
text.KJV: Then said the Jews, Forty and six years was this temple in building, and wilt thou rear it up in three days?
text.NABRE: The Jews said, “This temple has been under construction for forty-six years, and you will raise it up in three days?”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore said: In forty and six years was this temple built, & wilt thou raise it in three days?

text.NRSV: But he was speaking of the temple of his body.
text.Rotherham: But, he, was speaking concerning the shrine of his body.
text.KJV: But he spake of the temple of his body.
text.NABRE: But he was speaking about the temple of his body.
text.DRB: But he spake of the temple of his body.

text.NRSV: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this; and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he had been raised from among the dead, his disciples remembered, that, this, he had been saying; and they believed in the Scripture, and in the word which Jesus had spoken.
text.KJV: When therefore he was risen from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this unto them; and they believed the scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.
text.NABRE: Therefore, when he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this, and they came to believe the scripture and the word Jesus had spoken.
text.DRB: Therefore when he was risen again from the dead, his Disciples remembered, that he said this, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus did say.

text.NRSV: When he was in Jerusalem during the Passover festival, many believed in his name because they saw the signs that he was doing.
text.Rotherham: Now, when he was in Jerusalem, during the passover, during the feast, many, believed on his name, viewing his signs which he was doing.
text.KJV: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the passover, in the feast day, many believed in his name, when they saw the miracles which he did.
text.NABRE: While he was in Jerusalem for the feast of Passover, many began to believe in his name when they saw the signs he was doing.
text.DRB: And when he was at Jerusalem in the Pasch, upon the festival day, many believed in his name, seeing his signs which he did.

text.NRSV: But Jesus on his part would not entrust himself to them, because he knew all people
text.Rotherham: Jesus himself, however, was not trusting himself unto them, by reason of his understanding them all,
text.KJV: But Jesus did not commit himself unto them, because he knew all men,
text.NABRE: But Jesus would not trust himself to them because he knew them all,
text.DRB: But Jesus did not commit himself unto them, for that he knew all,

text.NRSV: and needed no one to testify about anyone; for he himself knew what was in everyone.
text.Rotherham: and because he had, no need, that anyone should bear witness concerning man; for, he, understood what was in man.
text.KJV: And needed not that any should testify of man: for he knew what was in man.
text.NABRE: and did not need anyone to testify about human nature. He himself understood it well.
text.DRB: and because it was not needful for him that any should give testimony of man; for he knew what was in man.

text.NRSV: Now there was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a leader of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: There was however, a man from among the Pharisees, Nicodemus, his name,—ruler of the Jews.
text.KJV: There was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews:
text.NABRE: Now there was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.
text.DRB: And there was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a Prince of the Jews.

text.NRSV: He came to Jesus by night and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God; for no one can do these signs that you do apart from the presence of God.”
text.Rotherham: The same, came unto him, by night, and said unto him—Rabbi! we know that, from God, thou hast come, a teacher; for, no one, can be doing, these signs, which, thou, art doing, except, God, be with him.
text.KJV: The same came to Jesus by night, and said unto him, Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God: for no man can do these miracles that thou doest, except God be with him.
text.NABRE: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one can do these signs that you are doing unless God is with him.”
text.DRB: This man came to Jesus by night, and said to him: Rabbi, we know that thou art come from God a Master; for no man can do these signs which thou doest, unless God be with him.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “Very truly, I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God without being born from above.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—Verily, verily, I say unto thee: Except one be born from above, he cannot see the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to him, “Amen, amen, I say to you, no one can see the kingdom of God without being born from above.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered, and said to him: Amen, Amen I say to thee, unless a man be born again, he cannot see the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Nicodemus said to him, “How can anyone be born after having grown old? Can one enter a second time into the mother’s womb and be born?”
text.Rotherham: Nicodemus saith unto him—How, can a man be born, when he is, old? Can he, into the womb of his mother, a second time, enter, and be born?
text.KJV: Nicodemus saith unto him, How can a man be born when he is old? can he enter the second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?
text.NABRE: Nicodemus said to him, “How can a person once grown old be born again? Surely he cannot reenter his mother’s womb and be born again, can he?”
text.DRB: Nicodemus said to him: How can a man be born, when he is old? can he enter into his mother's womb again & be born?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Very truly, I tell you, no one can enter the kingdom of God without being born of water and Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—Verily, verily, I say unto thee: Except one be born of water and spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “Amen, amen, I say to you, no one can enter the kingdom of God without being born of water and Spirit.
text.DRB: Jesus answered: Amen, Amen I say to thee, unless a man be born again of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter into the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: What is born of the flesh is flesh, and what is born of the Spirit is spirit.
text.Rotherham: That which hath been born of the flesh, is, flesh, and, that which hath been born of the spirit, is, spirit.
text.KJV: That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
text.NABRE: What is born of flesh is flesh and what is born of spirit is spirit.
text.DRB: That which is born of the flesh, is flesh: & that which is born of the Spirit, is spirit.

text.NRSV: Do not be astonished that I said to you, ‘You must be born from above.’
text.Rotherham: Do not marvel, that I said unto thee: Ye must needs be born from above.
text.KJV: Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again.
text.NABRE: Do not be amazed that I told you, ‘You must be born from above.’
text.DRB: Marvel not, that I said to thee, You must be born again.

text.NRSV: The wind blows where it chooses, and you hear the sound of it, but you do not know where it comes from or where it goes. So it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
text.Rotherham: The spirit, where it pleaseth, doth breathe, and, the sound thereof, thou hearest; but knowest not, whence it cometh and whither it goeth: Thus, is every one who hath been born of the spirit.
text.KJV: The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh, and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.
text.NABRE: The wind blows where it wills, and you can hear the sound it makes, but you do not know where it comes from or where it goes; so it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
text.DRB: The Spirit breatheth where he will; and thou hearest his voice, but thou knowest not whence he cometh and whither he goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.

text.NRSV: Nicodemus said to him, “How can these things be?”
text.Rotherham: Nicodemus answered, and said unto him—How, can these things, come about?
text.KJV: Nicodemus answered and said unto him, How can these things be?
text.NABRE: Nicodemus answered and said to him, “How can this happen?”
text.DRB: Nicodemus answered, & said to him: How can these things be done?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “Are you a teacher of Israel, and yet you do not understand these things?
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—Art, thou, the teacher of Israel, and, these things, knowest not?
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto him, Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things?
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to him, “You are the teacher of Israel and you do not understand this?
text.DRB: Jesus answered, and said to him: Thou art a Master in Israel, and art thou ignorant of these things?

text.NRSV: “Very truly, I tell you, we speak of what we know and testify to what we have seen; yet you do not receive our testimony.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto thee: What we know, we speak, and, of what we have seen, we bear witness, and, our witness, ye receive not.
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that we do know, and testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, we speak of what we know and we testify to what we have seen, but you people do not accept our testimony.
text.DRB: Amen, Amen I say to thee, that we speak that which we know, and that which we have seen we testify, and our testimony you receive not.

text.NRSV: If I have told you about earthly things and you do not believe, how can you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?
text.Rotherham: If, the earthly things, I told you, and ye believe not, How, if I should tell you the heavenly things, will ye believe?
text.KJV: If I have told you earthly things, and ye believe not, how shall ye believe, if I tell you of heavenly things?
text.NABRE: If I tell you about earthly things and you do not believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?
text.DRB: If I have spoken to you earthly things, and you believe not: how if I shall speak to you heavenly things, will you believe?

text.NRSV: No one has ascended into heaven except the one who descended from heaven, the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: And, no one, hath ascended into heaven, save he that, out of heaven, descended,—The Son of Man.
text.KJV: And no man hath ascended up to heaven, but he that came down from heaven, even the Son of man which is in heaven.
text.NABRE: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who has come down from heaven, the Son of Man.
text.DRB: And no man hath ascended into Heaven, but he that descended from Heaven, the Son of man which is in Heaven.

text.NRSV: And just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
text.Rotherham: And, just as, Moses, lifted up the serpent in the desert, so, must, the Son of Man, be lifted up,—
text.KJV: And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up:
text.NABRE: And just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the desert, so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
text.DRB: And as Moyses exalted the serpent in the desert, so must the Son of man be exalted:

text.NRSV: that whoever believes in him may have eternal life.
text.Rotherham: That, whosoever believeth in him, may have life age-abiding.
text.KJV: That whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
text.NABRE: so that everyone who believes in him may have eternal life.”
text.DRB: that every one which believeth in him, perish not, but may have life everlasting.

text.NRSV: “For God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him may not perish but may have eternal life.
text.Rotherham: For God, so loved, the world, that, his Only Begotten Son, he gave,—that, whosoever believeth on him, might not perish, but have life age-abiding.
text.KJV: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
text.NABRE: For God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him might not perish but might have eternal life.
text.DRB: For so God loved the world, that he gave his only-begotten Son; that every one that believeth in him, perish not, but may have life everlasting.

text.NRSV: “Indeed, God did not send the Son into the world to condemn the world, but in order that the world might be saved through him.
text.Rotherham: For God, sent not, his Son into the world, that he might judge the world, but, that the world might be saved through him.
text.KJV: For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved.
text.NABRE: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
text.DRB: For God sent not his Son into the world, to judge the world, but that the world may be saved by him.

text.NRSV: Those who believe in him are not condemned; but those who do not believe are condemned already, because they have not believed in the name of the only Son of God.
text.Rotherham: He that believeth on him, is not to be judged: he that believeth not, already, hath been judged,—because he hath not believed on the name of the Only Begotten Son of God.
text.KJV: He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.
text.NABRE: Whoever believes in him will not be condemned, but whoever does not believe has already been condemned, because he has not believed in the name of the only Son of God.
text.DRB: He that believeth in him, is not judged. But he that doth not believe, is already judged: because he hath not believed in the name of the only-begotten Son of God.

text.NRSV: And this is the judgment, that the light has come into the world, and people loved darkness rather than light because their deeds were evil.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the judgment: That, the light, hath come into the world,—and men loved, rather the darkness than the light, for, wicked, were their, works.
text.KJV: And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.
text.NABRE: And this is the verdict, that the light came into the world, but people preferred darkness to light, because their works were evil.
text.DRB: And this is the judgement: because the light is come into the world, and men have loved the darkness rather than the light: for their works were evil.

text.NRSV: For all who do evil hate the light and do not come to the light, so that their deeds may not be exposed.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever doth practise corrupt things, hateth the light, and cometh not unto the light, lest his works should be reproved;
text.KJV: For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved.
text.NABRE: For everyone who does wicked things hates the light and does not come toward the light, so that his works might not be exposed.
text.DRB: For every one that doeth ill, hateth the light, and cometh not to the light, that his works may not be controlled.

text.NRSV: But those who do what is true come to the light, so that it may be clearly seen that their deeds have been done in God.”
text.Rotherham: But, he that doeth the truth, cometh unto the light, that his works may be, made manifest, that, in God, have they been wrought.
text.KJV: But he that doeth truth cometh to the light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.
text.NABRE: But whoever lives the truth comes to the light, so that his works may be clearly seen as done in God.
text.DRB: But he that doeth verity, cometh to the light, that his works may be made manifest, because they were done in God.

text.NRSV: After this Jesus and his disciples went into the Judean countryside, and he spent some time there with them and baptized.
text.Rotherham: After these things, came Jesus, and his disciples, into the Judaean land; and, there, was he tarrying with them, and immersing.
text.KJV: After these things came Jesus and his disciples into the land of Judea; and there he tarried with them, and baptized.
text.NABRE: Final Witness of the Baptist. After this, Jesus and his disciples went into the region of Judea, where he spent some time with them baptizing.
text.DRB: After these things Jesus came and his Disciples into the country of Jewry; & there he abode with them, and baptized.

text.NRSV: John also was baptizing at Aenon near Salim because water was abundant there; and people kept coming and were being baptized
text.Rotherham: And John also was immersing in Aenon, near to him, because, many waters, were there; and they were coming, and being immersed;—
text.KJV: And John also was baptizing in Ænon near to Salim, because there was much water there: and they came, and were baptized.
text.NABRE: John was also baptizing in Aenon near Salim, because there was an abundance of water there, and people came to be baptized,
text.DRB: And John also was baptizing in Ænon beside Salim; because there was much water there, and they came, and were baptized.

text.NRSV: —John, of course, had not yet been thrown into prison.
text.Rotherham: for, not yet, had John been cast into prison.
text.KJV: For John was not yet cast into prison.
text.NABRE: for John had not yet been imprisoned.
text.DRB: For John was not yet cast into prison.

text.NRSV: Now a discussion about purification arose between John’s disciples and a Jew.
text.Rotherham: There arose, therefore, a questioning, from among the disciples of John, with a Jew,—concerning purification.
text.KJV: Then there arose a question between some of John’s disciples and the Jews about purifying.
text.NABRE: Now a dispute arose between the disciples of John and a Jew about ceremonial washings.
text.DRB: And there rose a question of John's Disciples with the Jews concerning purification.

text.NRSV: They came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, the one who was with you across the Jordan, to whom you testified, here he is baptizing, and all are going to him.”
text.Rotherham: And they came unto John, and said unto him—Rabbi! he who was with thee beyond the Jordan, unto whom, thou, hast borne witness, see! the same, is immersing; and, all, are coming unto him.
text.KJV: And they came unto John, and said unto him, Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold, the same baptizeth, and all men come to him.
text.NABRE: So they came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, the one who was with you across the Jordan, to whom you testified, here he is baptizing and everyone is coming to him.”
text.DRB: And they came to John, and said to him: Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou didst give testimony, behold he baptizeth, and all come to him.

text.NRSV: John answered, “No one can receive anything except what has been given from heaven.
text.Rotherham: John answered, and said—A man can receive, nothing, except it have been given him out of heaven.
text.KJV: John answered and said, A man can receive nothing, except it be given him from heaven.
text.NABRE: John answered and said, “No one can receive anything except what has been given him from heaven.
text.DRB: John answered and said: A man cannot receive any thing, unless it be given him from Heaven,

text.NRSV: You yourselves are my witnesses that I said, ‘I am not the Messiah, but I have been sent ahead of him.’
text.Rotherham: Ye yourselves, unto me, bear witness, that, I, said—I, am not the Christ; but—I am sent before, That One.
text.KJV: Ye yourselves bear me witness, that I said, I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before him.
text.NABRE: You yourselves can testify that I said [that] I am not the Messiah, but that I was sent before him.
text.DRB: Yourselves do bear me witness, that I said, I am not Christ; but that I am sent before him.

text.NRSV: He who has the bride is the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly at the bridegroom’s voice. For this reason my joy has been fulfilled.
text.Rotherham: He that hath the bride, is, bridegroom; but, the friend of the bridegroom, who standeth by and hearkeneth unto him, greatly, rejoiceth, by reason of the voice of the bridegroom. This, my joy, therefore, is fulfilled.
text.KJV: He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice: this my joy therefore is fulfilled.
text.NABRE: The one who has the bride is the bridegroom; the best man, who stands and listens to him, rejoices greatly at the bridegroom’s voice. So this joy of mine has been made complete.
text.DRB: He that hath the bride, is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom that standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth with joy for the voice of the bridegroom. This my joy therefore is filled.

text.NRSV: He must increase, but I must decrease.”
text.Rotherham: That One, it behoveth to increase,—but, me, to decrease.
text.KJV: He must increase, but I must decrease.
text.NABRE: He must increase; I must decrease.” The One from Heaven.
text.DRB: He must increase, and I diminish.

text.NRSV: The one who comes from above is above all; the one who is of the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all.
text.Rotherham: He that, from above, doth come, over all, is: he that is of the earth, of the earth, is, and, of the earth, doth speak: he that, out of heaven, doth come, over all, is,
text.KJV: He that cometh from above is above all: he that is of the earth is earthly, and speaketh of the earth: he that cometh from heaven is above all.
text.NABRE: The one who comes from above is above all. The one who is of the earth is earthly and speaks of earthly things. But the one who comes from heaven [is above all].
text.DRB: He that cometh from above, is above all. He that is of the earth, of the earth he is, and of the earth he speaketh. He that cometh from Heaven, is above all.

text.NRSV: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, yet no one accepts his testimony.
text.Rotherham: What he hath seen and heard, of, the same, he beareth witness,—and, his witness, no one, receiveth:—
text.KJV: And what he hath seen and heard, that he testifieth; and no man receiveth his testimony.
text.NABRE: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
text.DRB: And what he hath seen and heard, that he testifieth: and his testimony no man receiveth.

text.NRSV: Whoever has accepted his testimony has certified this, that God is true.
text.Rotherham: He that hath received his witness, hath set seal—that, God, is, true.
text.KJV: He that hath received his testimony hath set to his seal that God is true.
text.NABRE: Whoever does accept his testimony certifies that God is trustworthy.
text.DRB: He that hath received his testimony, hath signed that God is true.

text.NRSV: He whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for he gives the Spirit without measure.
text.Rotherham: For, he whom God hath sent, the sayings of God, doth speak; for, not by measure, giveth he the Spirit.
text.KJV: For he whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God: for God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him.
text.NABRE: For the one whom God sent speaks the words of God. He does not ration his gift of the Spirit.
text.DRB: For he whom God hath sent, speaketh the words of God. For God doth not give the Spirit by measure.

text.NRSV: The Father loves the Son and has placed all things in his hands.
text.Rotherham: The Father, loveth the Son, and, all things, hath given into his hand.
text.KJV: The Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into his hand.
text.NABRE: The Father loves the Son and has given everything over to him.
text.DRB: The Father loveth the Son: & he hath given all things in his hand.

text.NRSV: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life; whoever disobeys the Son will not see life, but must endure God’s wrath.
text.Rotherham: He that believeth on the Son, hath life age-abiding: whereas, he that yieldeth not unto the Son, shall not see life,—but, the anger of God, awaiteth him.
text.KJV: He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.
text.NABRE: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life, but whoever disobeys the Son will not see life, but the wrath of God remains upon him.
text.DRB: He that believeth in the Son: hath life everlasting: but he that is incredulous to the Son, shall not see life, but the wrath of God remaineth upon him.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard, “Jesus is making and baptizing more disciples than John”
text.Rotherham: When therefore the Lord knew, that the Pharisees had heard—Jesus, more disciples, is making and immersing, than John,—
text.KJV: When therefore the Lord knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John,
text.NABRE: Now when Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John
text.DRB: When Jesus therefore understood that the Pharisees heard that Jesus maketh more Disciples, and baptizeth, than John,

text.NRSV: —although it was not Jesus himself but his disciples who baptized—
text.Rotherham: although indeed, Jesus himself, was not immersing, but his disciples,
text.KJV: (Though Jesus himself baptized not, but his disciples,)
text.NABRE: (although Jesus himself was not baptizing, just his disciples),
text.DRB: (howbeit Jesus did not baptize, but his Disciples)

text.NRSV: he left Judea and started back to Galilee.
text.Rotherham: he left Judaea, and departed again into Galilee.
text.KJV: He left Judea, and departed again into Galilee.
text.NABRE: he left Judea and returned to Galilee.
text.DRB: he left Jewry, and went again into Galilee.

text.NRSV: But he had to go through Samaria.
text.Rotherham: Now he must needs pass through Samaria.
text.KJV: And he must needs go through Samaria.
text.NABRE: The Samaritan Woman. He had to pass through Samaria.
text.DRB: And he had of necessity to pass through Samaria.

text.NRSV: So he came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, near the plot of ground that Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
text.Rotherham: He cometh, therefore, unto a city of Samaria, called Sychar, near the parcel of ground which Jacob gave unto Joseph his son.
text.KJV: Then cometh he to a city of Samaria, which is called Sychar, near to the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son Joseph.
text.NABRE: So he came to a town of Samaria called Sychar, near the plot of land that Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
text.DRB: He cometh therefore into a city of Samaria which is called Sichar; beside the manor that Jacob gave to Joseph his son.

text.NRSV: Jacob’s well was there, and Jesus, tired out by his journey, was sitting by the well. It was about noon.
text.Rotherham: Now Jacob’s fountain was there. Jesus, therefore, having become toil-worn with the journey, was sitting thus, upon the fountain. It was about the sixth hour.
text.KJV: Now Jacob’s well was there. Jesus therefore, being wearied with his journey, sat thus on the well: and it was about the sixth hour.
text.NABRE: Jacob’s well was there. Jesus, tired from his journey, sat down there at the well. It was about noon.
text.DRB: And there was there the fountain of Jacob. Jesus therefore wearied of his journey, sat so upon the fountain. It was about the sixth hour.

text.NRSV: A Samaritan woman came to draw water, and Jesus said to her, “Give me a drink.”
text.Rotherham: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water. Jesus saith unto her—Give me to drink!
text.KJV: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her, Give me to drink.
text.NABRE: A woman of Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, “Give me a drink.”
text.DRB: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water. Jesus said to her: Give me to drink.

text.NRSV: (His disciples had gone to the city to buy food.)
text.Rotherham: for, his disciples, had gone away into the city, that, food, they might buy.
text.KJV: (For his disciples were gone away unto the city to buy meat.)
text.NABRE: His disciples had gone into the town to buy food.
text.DRB: For his Disciples were gone into the city, to buy meats.

text.NRSV: The Samaritan woman said to him, “How is it that you, a Jew, ask a drink of me, a woman of Samaria?” (Jews do not share things in common with Samaritans.)
text.Rotherham: The Samaritan woman, therefore, saith unto him—How dost, thou, being, a Jew, ask to drink, of me, who am, a Samaritan woman? [for, Jews, have no dealings with Samaritans.]
text.KJV: Then saith the woman of Samaria unto him, How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me, which am a woman of Samaria? for the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans.
text.NABRE: The Samaritan woman said to him, “How can you, a Jew, ask me, a Samaritan woman, for a drink?” (For Jews use nothing in common with Samaritans.)
text.DRB: Therefore that Samaritane woman saith to him: How doest thou being a Jew, ask of me to drink, which am a Samaritane woman? For the Jews do not communicate with the Samaritans.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered her, “If you knew the gift of God, and who it is that is saying to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto her—If thou hadst known the free gift of God, and who it is that is saying unto thee, Give me to drink, thou, wouldst have asked him, and he would have given thee living water.
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto her, If thou knewest the gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink; thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to her, “If you knew the gift of God and who is saying to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would have asked him and he would have given you living water.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered, and said to her: If thou didst know the gift of God, and who he is that saith unto thee, Give me to drink; thou perhaps wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.

text.NRSV: The woman said to him, “Sir, you have no bucket, and the well is deep. Where do you get that living water?
text.Rotherham: She saith unto him—Sir! not even a bucket, hast thou,—and, the well, is, deep:—Whence, then, hast thou the living water?
text.KJV: The woman saith unto him, Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: from whence then hast thou that living water?
text.NABRE: [The woman] said to him, “Sir, you do not even have a bucket and the well is deep; where then can you get this living water?
text.DRB: The woman saith to him: Sir, neither hast thou wherein to draw, and the well is deep; whence hast thou the living water?

text.NRSV: Are you greater than our ancestor Jacob, who gave us the well, and with his sons and his flocks drank from it?”
text.Rotherham: Art, thou, greater than, our father Jacob, who gave us the well,—and, himself, out of it drank, and his sons, and his flocks?
text.KJV: Art thou greater than our father Jacob, which gave us the well, and drank thereof himself, and his children, and his cattle?
text.NABRE: Are you greater than our father Jacob, who gave us this well and drank from it himself with his children and his flocks?”
text.DRB: art thou greater than our Father Jacob, who gave us the well, & himself drank of it, and his children, and his cattle?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Everyone who drinks of this water will be thirsty again,
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto her—Whosoever drinketh of this water, will thirst, again;
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto her, Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to her, “Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again;
text.DRB: Jesus answered, and said to her: Every one that drinketh of this water, shall thirst again; but he that shall drink of the water that I will give him, shall not thirst for ever,

text.NRSV: but those who drink of the water that I will give them will never be thirsty. The water that I will give will become in them a spring of water gushing up to eternal life.”
text.Rotherham: But, whosoever shall drink of the water which, I, will give him, in nowise shall thirst, unto times age-abiding,—but, the water which I will give him, shall become, within him, a fountain of water, springing up unto life age-abiding.
text.KJV: But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.
text.NABRE: but whoever drinks the water I shall give will never thirst; the water I shall give will become in him a spring of water welling up to eternal life.”
text.DRB: but the water that I will give him, shall become in him a fountain of water springing up unto life everlasting.

text.NRSV: The woman said to him, “Sir, give me this water, so that I may never be thirsty or have to keep coming here to draw water.”
text.Rotherham: The woman saith unto him—Sir! give me this water, that I thirst not, neither be coming hither to draw.
text.KJV: The woman saith unto him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.
text.NABRE: The woman said to him, “Sir, give me this water, so that I may not be thirsty or have to keep coming here to draw water.”
text.DRB: The woman saith to him: Lord give me this water, that I may not thirst, nor come hither to draw.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Go, call your husband, and come back.”
text.Rotherham: He saith unto her—Go, call thy husband, and come hither!
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto her, Go, call thy husband, and come hither.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to her, “Go call your husband and come back.”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to her: Go, call thy husband, and come hither.

text.NRSV: The woman answered him, “I have no husband.” Jesus said to her, “You are right in saying, ‘I have no husband’;
text.Rotherham: The woman answered, and said [unto him] —I have no husband. Jesus saith unto her—Well, saidst thou, A husband, I have not;
text.KJV: The woman answered and said, I have no husband. Jesus said unto her, Thou hast well said, I have no husband:
text.NABRE: The woman answered and said to him, “I do not have a husband.” Jesus answered her, “You are right in saying, ‘I do not have a husband.’
text.DRB: The woman answered and said: I have no husband. Jesus said to her: Thou hast said well, that I have no husband.

text.NRSV: for you have had five husbands, and the one you have now is not your husband. What you have said is true!”
text.Rotherham: for, five husbands, thou hast had,—and, now, he whom thou hast, is not thy, husband: This true thing, hast thou spoken.
text.KJV: For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.
text.NABRE: For you have had five husbands, and the one you have now is not your husband. What you have said is true.”
text.DRB: For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast, is not thy husband. This thou hast said truly.

text.NRSV: The woman said to him, “Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
text.Rotherham: The woman saith unto him—Sir! I perceive that, a prophet, art, thou:—
text.KJV: The woman saith unto him, Sir, I perceive that thou art a prophet.
text.NABRE: The woman said to him, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
text.DRB: The woman saith to him: Lord, I perceive that thou art a Prophet.

text.NRSV: Our ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you say that the place where people must worship is in Jerusalem.”
text.Rotherham: Our fathers, in this mountain, worshipped; and, ye, say, that, in Jerusalem, is the place, where, to worship, it behoveth.
text.KJV: Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say, that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.
text.NABRE: Our ancestors worshiped on this mountain; but you people say that the place to worship is in Jerusalem.”
text.DRB: Our Fathers adored in this mountain, and you say, that at Jerusalem is the place where men must adore.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Woman, believe me, the hour is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Believe me, woman! There cometh an hour, when, neither in this mountain, nor yet in Jerusalem, shall ye worship the Father.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to her, “Believe me, woman, the hour is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Jesus saith to her: Woman believe me, that the hour shall come, when you shall neither in this mountain, nor in Jerusalem adore the Father.

text.NRSV: You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, for salvation is from the Jews.
text.Rotherham: Ye, worship, that which ye know not; We, worship, that which we know; because, salvation, is, of the Jews.
text.KJV: Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.
text.NABRE: You people worship what you do not understand; we worship what we understand, because salvation is from the Jews.
text.DRB: You adore that you know not: we adore that we know; for salvation is of the Jews.
