text.NRSV: But the hour is coming, and is now here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father seeks such as these to worship him.
text.Rotherham: But there cometh an hour, and, now, is,—when, the real worshippers, shall worship the Father in spirit and truth; for, even the Father, is seeking, such as these, as his worshippers.
text.KJV: But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth: for the Father seeketh such to worship him.
text.NABRE: But the hour is coming, and is now here, when true worshipers will worship the Father in Spirit and truth; and indeed the Father seeks such people to worship him.
text.DRB: But the hour cometh, and now it is, when the true adorers shall adore the Father in spirit and verity. For the Father also seeketh such to adore him.

text.NRSV: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.”
text.Rotherham: God is, spirit; and, they that worship him, in spirit and truth, must needs worship.
text.KJV: God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.
text.NABRE: God is Spirit, and those who worship him must worship in Spirit and truth.”
text.DRB: God is a Spirit, and they that adore him, must adore in spirit and verity.

text.NRSV: The woman said to him, “I know that Messiah is coming” (who is called Christ). “When he comes, he will proclaim all things to us.”
text.Rotherham: The woman saith unto him—I know that, Messiah, cometh, who is called Christ,—Whensoever, he, shall come, he will tell us, everything.
text.KJV: The woman saith unto him, I know that Messias cometh, which is called Christ: when he is come, he will tell us all things.
text.NABRE: The woman said to him, “I know that the Messiah is coming, the one called the Anointed; when he comes, he will tell us everything.”
text.DRB: The woman saith to him: I know that Messias cometh, (which is called Christ) therefore when he cometh, he will shew us all things.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “I am he, the one who is speaking to you.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—I, that speak unto thee, am he.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto her, I that speak unto thee am he.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to her, “I am he, the one who is speaking with you.”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to her: I am he, that speak with thee.

text.NRSV: Just then his disciples came. They were astonished that he was speaking with a woman, but no one said, “What do you want?” or, “Why are you speaking with her?”
text.Rotherham: And, hereupon, came his disciples, and they began to marvel, that, with a woman, he was talking. No one, however, said—What seekest thou? or—Why talkest thou with her?
text.KJV: And upon this came his disciples, and marvelled that he talked with the woman: yet no man said, What seekest thou? or, Why talkest thou with her?
text.NABRE: At that moment his disciples returned, and were amazed that he was talking with a woman, but still no one said, “What are you looking for?” or “Why are you talking with her?”
text.DRB: And incontinent his Disciples came: and they marveled that he talked with a woman. No man for all that said: What seekest thou, or why talkest thou with her?

text.NRSV: Then the woman left her water jar and went back to the city. She said to the people,
text.Rotherham: The woman, therefore, left her water-vessel, and went away into the city, and saith unto the men—
text.KJV: The woman then left her waterpot, and went her way into the city, and saith to the men,
text.NABRE: The woman left her water jar and went into the town and said to the people,
text.DRB: The woman therefore left her water-pot: and she went into the city, and saith to those men:

text.NRSV: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! He cannot be the Messiah, can he?”
text.Rotherham: Come! see a man, that told me all things that ever I did: Can, this, be, the Christ?
text.KJV: Come, see a man, which told me all things that ever I did: is not this the Christ?
text.NABRE: “Come see a man who told me everything I have done. Could he possibly be the Messiah?”
text.DRB: Come, and see a man that hath told me all things whatsoever I have done. Is not he Christ?

text.NRSV: They left the city and were on their way to him.
text.Rotherham: They came forth out of the city, and were coming unto him.
text.KJV: Then they went out of the city, and came unto him.
text.NABRE: They went out of the town and came to him.
text.DRB: They went forth therefore out of the city, and came to him.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile the disciples were urging him, “Rabbi, eat something.”
text.Rotherham: In the meantime, the disciples were requesting him, saying—Rabbi! eat.
text.KJV: In the mean while his disciples prayed him, saying, Master, eat.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile, the disciples urged him, “Rabbi, eat.”
text.DRB: In the mean time the Disciples desired him, saying: Rabbi eat.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “I have food to eat that you do not know about.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto them—I, have, food, to eat, of which, ye, know not.
text.KJV: But he said unto them, I have meat to eat that ye know not of.
text.NABRE: But he said to them, “I have food to eat of which you do not know.”
text.DRB: But he said to them: I have meat to eat which you know not.

text.NRSV: So the disciples said to one another, “Surely no one has brought him something to eat?”
text.Rotherham: The disciples, therefore, began to say, one to another—Hath anyone brought him, to eat?
text.KJV: Therefore said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought him ought to eat?
text.NABRE: So the disciples said to one another, “Could someone have brought him something to eat?”
text.DRB: The Disciples therefore said one to another: Hath any man brought him for to eat?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to complete his work.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto them—My food, is, that I should do the will of him that sent me, and complete, his work.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto them, My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of the one who sent me and to finish his work.
text.DRB: Jesus saith to them: My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, to perfit his work.

text.NRSV: Do you not say, ‘Four months more, then comes the harvest’? But I tell you, look around you, and see how the fields are ripe for harvesting.
text.Rotherham: Are, ye, not saying—Yet, a fourmonth, it is, and, the harvest, cometh? Lo! I say unto you—Lift up your eyes, and gaze at the fields,—that, white, are they unto, harvest. Already,
text.KJV: Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest.
text.NABRE: Do you not say, ‘In four months the harvest will be here’? I tell you, look up and see the fields ripe for the harvest.
text.DRB: Do not you say that yet there are four months, and harvest cometh? Behold I say to you, lift up your eyes, and see the Countries, that they are white already to harvest.

text.NRSV: The reaper is already receiving wages and is gathering fruit for eternal life, so that sower and reaper may rejoice together.
text.Rotherham: he that reapeth, receiveth, a reward, and gathereth fruit unto life age-abiding; that, he that soweth, together may rejoice, with him that reapeth.
text.KJV: And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.
text.NABRE: The reaper is already receiving his payment and gathering crops for eternal life, so that the sower and reaper can rejoice together.
text.DRB: And he that reapeth, receiveth hire, and gathereth fruit unto life everlasting: that both he that soweth, and he that reapeth, may rejoice together.

text.NRSV: For here the saying holds true, ‘One sows and another reaps.’
text.Rotherham: For, herein, doth the saying, hold good,—One, is the sower, and, another, is the reaper.
text.KJV: And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth.
text.NABRE: For here the saying is verified that ‘One sows and another reaps.’
text.DRB: For in this is the saying true: that it is one man that soweth, and it is another that reapeth.

text.NRSV: I sent you to reap that for which you did not labor. Others have labored, and you have entered into their labor.”
text.Rotherham: I, sent you forth, to be reaping that whereon, ye, have not toiled: Others, have toiled, and, ye, into their toil, have entered.
text.KJV: I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.
text.NABRE: I sent you to reap what you have not worked for; others have done the work, and you are sharing the fruits of their work.”
text.DRB: I have sent you to reap that which you laboured not: others have laboured, & you have entered into their labours.

text.NRSV: Many Samaritans from that city believed in him because of the woman’s testimony, “He told me everything I have ever done.”
text.Rotherham: And, out of that city, many, believed on him—of the Samaritans,—by reason of the word of the woman in bearing witness—He told me all things that ever I did.
text.KJV: And many of the Samaritans of that city believed on him for the saying of the woman, which testified, He told me all that ever I did.
text.NABRE: Many of the Samaritans of that town began to believe in him because of the word of the woman who testified, “He told me everything I have done.”
text.DRB: And of that city many believed in him of the Samaritans, for the word of the woman giving testimony, that he told me all things whatsoever I have done.

text.NRSV: So when the Samaritans came to him, they asked him to stay with them; and he stayed there two days.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, the Samaritans came unto him, they went on to request him to abide with them; and he abode there two days.
text.KJV: So when the Samaritans were come unto him, they besought him that he would tarry with them: and he abode there two days.
text.NABRE: When the Samaritans came to him, they invited him to stay with them; and he stayed there two days.
text.DRB: Therefore when the Samaritans were come to him, they desired him that he would tarry there. And he tarried there two days.

text.NRSV: And many more believed because of his word.
text.Rotherham: And, many more, believed by reason of his discourse;
text.KJV: And many more believed because of his own word;
text.NABRE: Many more began to believe in him because of his word,
text.DRB: And many more believed for his own word.

text.NRSV: They said to the woman, “It is no longer because of what you said that we believe, for we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this is truly the Savior of the world.”
text.Rotherham: and, unto the woman, began to say—No longer, by reason of thy talk, do we believe; for, we ourselves, have heard, and know that, This One, is, in truth, the Saviour of the world.
text.KJV: And said unto the woman, Now we believe, not because of thy saying: for we have heard him ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world.
text.NABRE: and they said to the woman, “We no longer believe because of your word; for we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this is truly the savior of the world.”
text.DRB: And they said to the woman, That now not for thy saying do we believe; for ourselves have heard, and do know that this is the Saviour of the world indeed.

text.NRSV: When the two days were over, he went from that place to Galilee
text.Rotherham: But, after the two days, he went forth from thence, into Galilee.
text.KJV: Now after two days he departed thence, and went into Galilee.
text.NABRE: Return to Galilee. After the two days, he left there for Galilee.
text.DRB: And after two days he departed thence; and went into Galilee.

text.NRSV: (for Jesus himself had testified that a prophet has no honor in the prophet’s own country).
text.Rotherham: For, Jesus himself, bare witness, that, a prophet, in his own fatherland, hath not, honour.
text.KJV: For Jesus himself testified, that a prophet hath no honour in his own country.
text.NABRE: For Jesus himself testified that a prophet has no honor in his native place.
text.DRB: For Jesus himself gave testimony that a Prophet hath not honour in his own country:

text.NRSV: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him, since they had seen all that he had done in Jerusalem at the festival; for they too had gone to the festival.
text.Rotherham: So, when he came into Galilee, the Galilaeans welcomed him, having seen, all things, whatsoever he had done in Jerusalem during the feast; for, they also, went unto the feast.
text.KJV: Then when he was come into Galilee, the Galileans received him, having seen all the things that he did at Jerusalem at the feast: for they also went unto the feast.
text.NABRE: When he came into Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him, since they had seen all he had done in Jerusalem at the feast; for they themselves had gone to the feast. Second Sign at Cana.
text.DRB: Therefore when he was come into Galilee, the Galilæans received him, whereas they had seen all things that he had done at Jerusalem in the festival day: for themselves also came to the festival day.

text.NRSV: Then he came again to Cana in Galilee where he had changed the water into wine. Now there was a royal official whose son lay ill in Capernaum.
text.Rotherham: So he came again into Cana of Galilee, where he had made the water, wine. And there was a certain courtier, whose son was sick, in Capernaum.
text.KJV: So Jesus came again into Cana of Galilee, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman, whose son was sick at Capernaum.
text.NABRE: Then he returned to Cana in Galilee, where he had made the water wine. Now there was a royal official whose son was ill in Capernaum.
text.DRB: He came again therefore into Cana of Galilee, where he made water wine. And there was a certain lord whose son was sick at Capharnaum.

text.NRSV: When he heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went and begged him to come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.
text.Rotherham: The same, hearing that Jesus had come out of Judaea into Galilee, came away unto him, and began requesting him, that he would come down and heal his son; for he was on the point of dying.
text.KJV: When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judea into Galilee, he went unto him, and besought him that he would come down, and heal his son: for he was at the point of death.
text.NABRE: When he heard that Jesus had arrived in Galilee from Judea, he went to him and asked him to come down and heal his son, who was near death.
text.DRB: He having heard that Jesus came from Jewry into Galilee, went to him, and desired him that he would come down and heal his son, For he began to die.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “Unless you see signs and wonders you will not believe.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus therefore said unto him—Except, signs and wonders, ye see, in nowise will ye believe.
text.KJV: Then said Jesus unto him, Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “Unless you people see signs and wonders, you will not believe.”
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to him: Unless you see signs and wonders, you believe not.

text.NRSV: The official said to him, “Sir, come down before my little boy dies.”
text.Rotherham: The courtier saith unto him—Sir! come down, ere my child die!
text.KJV: The nobleman saith unto him, Sir, come down ere my child die.
text.NABRE: The royal official said to him, “Sir, come down before my child dies.”
text.DRB: The lord saith to him: Lord, come down before that my son die.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Go; your son will live.” The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and started on his way.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—Be going thy way: thy son, liveth! And the man believed in the word which Jesus spake to him, and at once went his way.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto him, Go thy way; thy son liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “You may go; your son will live.” The man believed what Jesus said to him and left.
text.DRB: Jesus saith to him: Go, thy son liveth. The man believed the word that Jesus said to him, and went.

text.NRSV: As he was going down, his slaves met him and told him that his child was alive.
text.Rotherham: And, when he was even now going down, his servants, met him, saying, that, his boy, was living.
text.KJV: And as he was now going down, his servants met him, and told him, saying, Thy son liveth.
text.NABRE: While he was on his way back, his slaves met him and told him that his boy would live.
text.DRB: And as he was now going down, his servants met him: and they brought word, saying, That his son lived.

text.NRSV: So he asked them the hour when he began to recover, and they said to him, “Yesterday at one in the afternoon the fever left him.”
text.Rotherham: So he enquired the hour, from them, when he began, to amend. They said, therefore, unto him—Yesterday, at the seventh hour, the fever left him.
text.KJV: Then enquired he of them the hour when he began to amend. And they said unto him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.
text.NABRE: He asked them when he began to recover. They told him, “The fever left him yesterday, about one in the afternoon.”
text.DRB: He asked therefore of them the hour, wherein he was amended. And they said to him, That yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.

text.NRSV: The father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, “Your son will live.” So he himself believed, along with his whole household.
text.Rotherham: So the father took note that it was, in that hour, wherein Jesus said unto him—Thy son, liveth! and he believed, he, and all his house.
text.KJV: So the father knew that it was at the same hour, in the which Jesus said unto him, Thy son liveth: and himself believed, and his whole house.
text.NABRE: The father realized that just at that time Jesus had said to him, “Your son will live,” and he and his whole household came to believe.
text.DRB: The father therefore knew that it was in the same hour wherein Jesus said to him: Thy son liveth, and himself believed and his whole house.

text.NRSV: Now this was the second sign that Jesus did after coming from Judea to Galilee.
text.Rotherham: Now, this again, as, a second sign, Jesus did, after coming out of Judaea into Galilee.
text.KJV: This is again the second miracle that Jesus did, when he was come out of Judea into Galilee.
text.NABRE: [Now] this was the second sign Jesus did when he came to Galilee from Judea.
text.DRB: This again the second sign did Jesus, when he was come from Jewry into Galilee.

text.KJV: After this there was a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: After this, there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: After this there was a festival of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: After these things there was a festival day of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: After these things, was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up unto Jerusalem.

text.KJV: Now there is at Jerusalem by the sheep market a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda, having five porches.
text.NABRE: Now there is in Jerusalem at the Sheep [Gate] a pool called in Hebrew Bethesda, with five porticoes.
text.NRSV: Now in Jerusalem by the Sheep Gate there is a pool, called in Hebrew Beth-zatha, which has five porticoes.
text.DRB: And there is at Jerusalem upon Probatica a pond which in Hebrew is surnamed Bethsaida, having five porches.
text.Rotherham: Now there is in Jerusalem, at the Sheep-gate, a pool, which is called in Hebrew Bethzatha,—having, five porches.

text.KJV: In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk, of blind, halt, withered, waiting for the moving of the water.
text.NABRE: In these lay a large number of ill, blind, lame, and crippled.
text.NRSV: In these lay many invalids—blind, lame, and paralyzed.
text.DRB: In these lay a great multitude of sick persons, of blind, lame, withered, expecting the stirring of the water.
text.Rotherham: In these, was lying a throng of them who were sick,—blind, lame, withered.

text.KJV: For an angel went down at a certain season into the pool, and troubled the water: whosoever then first after the troubling of the water stepped in was made whole of whatsoever disease he had.
text.DRB: And an Angel of our Lord descended at a certain time into the pond; and the water was stirred. And he that had gone down first into the pond after the stirring of the water, was made whole of whatsoever infirmity he was holden.

text.KJV: And a certain man was there, which had an infirmity thirty and eight years.
text.NABRE: One man was there who had been ill for thirty-eight years.
text.NRSV: One man was there who had been ill for thirty-eight years.
text.DRB: And there was a certain man there that had been eight and thirty years in his infirmity.
text.Rotherham: And there was a certain man there, who, for thirty-eight years, had continued in his sickness.

text.KJV: When Jesus saw him lie, and knew that he had been now a long time in that case, he saith unto him, Wilt thou be made whole?
text.NABRE: When Jesus saw him lying there and knew that he had been ill for a long time, he said to him, “Do you want to be well?”
text.NRSV: When Jesus saw him lying there and knew that he had been there a long time, he said to him, “Do you want to be made well?”
text.DRB: Him when Jesus had seen lying, & knew that he had now a long time, he saith to him: Wilt thou be made whole?
text.Rotherham: Jesus, seeing, this one, lying, and taking note that now, a long time, had he continued, saith unto him—Desirest thou to be made, well?

text.KJV: The impotent man answered him, Sir, I have no man, when the water is troubled, to put me into the pool: but while I am coming, another steppeth down before me.
text.NABRE: The sick man answered him, “Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up; while I am on my way, someone else gets down there before me.”
text.NRSV: The sick man answered him, “Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up; and while I am making my way, someone else steps down ahead of me.”
text.DRB: The sick man answered him: Lord, I have no man, when the water is troubled, to put me into the pond. For whiles I come, another goeth down before me.
text.Rotherham: The sick man answered him—Sir! I have, no man, that, as soon as the water hath been troubled, might thrust me into the pool; but, while, I, am coming, another, before me, goeth down.

text.KJV: Jesus saith unto him, Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “Rise, take up your mat, and walk.”
text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Stand up, take your mat and walk.”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to him: Arise, take up thy bed, and walk.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—Rise! take up thy couch, and be walking.

text.KJV: And immediately the man was made whole, and took up his bed, and walked: and on the same day was the sabbath.
text.NABRE: Immediately the man became well, took up his mat, and walked. Now that day was a sabbath.
text.NRSV: At once the man was made well, and he took up his mat and began to walk. Now that day was a sabbath.
text.DRB: And forthwith he was made whole: and he took up his bed, and walked. And it was the Sabboth that day.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, the man was made well and took up his couch, and began to walk. Now it was Sabbath, on that day.

text.KJV: The Jews therefore said unto him that was cured, It is the sabbath day: it is not lawful for thee to carry thy bed.
text.NABRE: So the Jews said to the man who was cured, “It is the sabbath, and it is not lawful for you to carry your mat.”
text.NRSV: So the Jews said to the man who had been cured, “It is the sabbath; it is not lawful for you to carry your mat.”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore said to him that was healed: It is the Sabboth, thou mayst not take up thy bed.
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, were saying, unto him who had been cured—It is, Sabbath; and it is not allowable for thee to take up thy couch:—

text.KJV: He answered them, He that made me whole, the same said unto me, Take up thy bed, and walk.
text.NABRE: He answered them, “The man who made me well told me, ‘Take up your mat and walk.’”
text.NRSV: But he answered them, “The man who made me well said to me, ‘Take up your mat and walk.’”
text.DRB: He answered them: He that made me whole, he said to me, take up thy bed, and walk.
text.Rotherham: who, however, answered them—He that made me well, he, unto me, said—Take up thy couch, and be walking.

text.KJV: Then asked they him, What man is that which said unto thee, Take up thy bed, and walk?
text.NABRE: They asked him, “Who is the man who told you, ‘Take it up and walk’?”
text.NRSV: They asked him, “Who is the man who said to you, ‘Take it up and walk’?”
text.DRB: They asked him therefore, what is that man that said to thee, take up thy bed, and walk?
text.Rotherham: They questioned him—Who is the man that said unto thee, Take up [thy couch], and be walking?

text.KJV: And he that was healed wist not who it was: for Jesus had conveyed himself away, a multitude being in that place.
text.NABRE: The man who was healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had slipped away, since there was a crowd there.
text.NRSV: Now the man who had been healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had disappeared in the crowd that was there.
text.DRB: But he that was made whole, knew not who it was. For Jesus shrunk aside from the multitude standing in the place.
text.Rotherham: But, the healed one, knew not, who, it was; for, Jesus, had turned aside, a multitude, being in the place.

text.KJV: Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple, and said unto him, Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.
text.NABRE: After this Jesus found him in the temple area and said to him, “Look, you are well; do not sin any more, so that nothing worse may happen to you.”
text.NRSV: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, “See, you have been made well! Do not sin any more, so that nothing worse happens to you.”
text.DRB: Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple, & said to him: Behold thou art made whole; sin no more, lest some worse thing chance to thee.
text.Rotherham: After these things, Jesus findeth him, in the temple, and said unto him—See! thou hast become, well:—No more, be committing sin, lest, some worse thing, do thee befall.

text.KJV: The man departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus, which had made him whole.
text.NABRE: The man went and told the Jews that Jesus was the one who had made him well.
text.NRSV: The man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
text.DRB: That man went his way, and told the Jews that it was Jesus that made him whole.
text.Rotherham: The man went away, and told the Jews, that it was, Jesus, who had made him well.

text.KJV: And therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus, and sought to slay him, because he had done these things on the sabbath day.
text.NABRE: Therefore, the Jews began to persecute Jesus because he did this on a sabbath.
text.NRSV: Therefore the Jews started persecuting Jesus, because he was doing such things on the sabbath.
text.DRB: Thereupon the Jews persecuted Jesus, because he did these things on the Sabboth.
text.Rotherham: And, on this account, were the Jews persecuting Jesus, because, these things, he had been doing, on Sabbath.

text.KJV: But Jesus answered them, My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.
text.NABRE: But Jesus answered them, “My Father is at work until now, so I am at work.”
text.NRSV: But Jesus answered them, “My Father is still working, and I also am working.”
text.DRB: But Jesus answered them: My Father worketh until now; and I do work.
text.Rotherham: But, he, answered them—My Father, until even now, is working; and, I, am working.

text.KJV: Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him, because he not only had broken the sabbath, but said also that God was his Father, making himself equal with God.
text.NABRE: For this reason the Jews tried all the more to kill him, because he not only broke the sabbath but he also called God his own father, making himself equal to God.
text.NRSV: For this reason the Jews were seeking all the more to kill him, because he was not only breaking the sabbath, but was also calling God his own Father, thereby making himself equal to God.
text.DRB: Thereupon therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him: because he did not only break the Sabboth, but also he said God was his Father, making himself equal to God.
text.Rotherham: On this account, therefore, the rather, were the Jews seeking to slay him,—because, not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but was calling God, his own Father, making, himself, equal, with, God.

text.KJV: Then answered Jesus and said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise.
text.NABRE: The Work of the Son. Jesus answered and said to them, “Amen, amen, I say to you, a son cannot do anything on his own, but only what he sees his father doing; for what he does, his son will do also.
text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, the Son can do nothing on his own, but only what he sees the Father doing; for whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore answered, and said to them: Amen, amen, I say to you, the Son cannot do any thing of himself, but that which he seeth the Father doing. For what things soever he doeth, these the Son also doeth in like manner.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, answered, and went on to say unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you: The Son cannot be doing, of himself, a single thing,—save anything he may see, the Father, doing; for, whatsoever, he, may be doing, these things, the Son also, in like manner, doeth.

text.KJV: For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth: and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may marvel.
text.NABRE: For the Father loves his Son and shows him everything that he himself does, and he will show him greater works than these, so that you may be amazed.
text.NRSV: The Father loves the Son and shows him all that he himself is doing; and he will show him greater works than these, so that you will be astonished.
text.DRB: For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth, and greater works than these will he shew him, that you may marvel.
text.Rotherham: For, the Father, dearly loveth the Son, and, all things, pointeth out to him, which, himself, is doing; and, greater works than these, will he point out to him, that, ye, may marvel.

text.KJV: For as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them; even so the Son quickeneth whom he will.
text.NABRE: For just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so also does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
text.NRSV: Indeed, just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, so also the Son gives life to whomever he wishes.
text.DRB: For as the Father doth raise the dead and quickeneth: so the Son also quickeneth whom he will.
text.Rotherham: For, just as the Father waketh up the dead, and quickeneth them, in like manner, the Son also, whom he pleaseth, doth quicken.

text.KJV: For the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son:
text.NABRE: Nor does the Father judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to his Son,
text.NRSV: The Father judges no one but has given all judgment to the Son,
text.DRB: For neither doth the Father judge any man: but all judgement he hath given to the Son,
text.Rotherham: For neither doth, the Father, judge anyone,—but, all the judging, hath given unto the Son:

text.KJV: That all men should honour the Son, even as they honour the Father. He that honoureth not the Son honoureth not the Father which hath sent him.
text.NABRE: so that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.
text.NRSV: so that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Anyone who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.
text.DRB: that all may honour the Son, as they do honour the Father. He that honoureth not the Son, doth not honour the Father, who sent him.
text.Rotherham: That, all, may honour the Son, just as they honour the Father: he that doth not honour the Son, doth not, in fact, honour the Father who sent him.

text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and will not come to condemnation, but has passed from death to life.
text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, anyone who hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life, and does not come under judgment, but has passed from death to life.
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to you, that he which heareth my word, and believeth him that sent me, hath life everlasting: and he cometh not into judgement, but shall pass from death into life.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you: He that heareth, my word, and believeth in him that sent me, hath life age-abiding, and, into judgment, cometh not, but hath passed over, out of death into life.

text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, the hour is coming and is now here when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.
text.NRSV: “Very truly, I tell you, the hour is coming, and is now here, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to you, that the hour cometh, and now it is when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God, and they that have heard, shall live.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you: There cometh an hour, and now is, when, the dead, shall hearken unto the voice of the Son of God, and, they who have hearkened, shall live.

text.KJV: For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself;
text.NABRE: For just as the Father has life in himself, so also he gave to his Son the possession of life in himself.
text.NRSV: For just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son also to have life in himself;
text.DRB: For as the Father hath life in himself; so he hath given to the Son also to have life in himself:
text.Rotherham: For, just as, the Father, hath life, in himself, in like manner, unto the Son also, hath he given, life, to have, in himself;

text.KJV: And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.
text.NABRE: And he gave him power to exercise judgment, because he is the Son of Man.
text.NRSV: and he has given him authority to execute judgment, because he is the Son of Man.
text.DRB: and he hath given him power to do judgement also because he is the Son of man.
text.Rotherham: And, authority, hath he given him, to be executing, judgment,—because, Son of Man, is he!

text.KJV: Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice,
text.NABRE: Do not be amazed at this, because the hour is coming in which all who are in the tombs will hear his voice
text.NRSV: Do not be astonished at this; for the hour is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice
text.DRB: Marvel not at this, because the hour cometh wherein all that are in the graves, shall hear his voice,
text.Rotherham: Do not be marvelling at this: because there come an hour, in which, all they in the tombs, shall hearken unto his voice,

text.KJV: And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.
text.NABRE: and will come out, those who have done good deeds to the resurrection of life, but those who have done wicked deeds to the resurrection of condemnation.
text.NRSV: and will come out—those who have done good, to the resurrection of life, and those who have done evil, to the resurrection of condemnation.
text.DRB: and they that have done good things, shall come forth into the resurrection of life: but they that have done evil into the resurrection of judgement.
text.Rotherham: and shall come forth,—they who, the good things, have done, unto a resurrection, of life; but, they who the corrupt things, have practised, unto a resurrection, of judgment.

text.KJV: I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me.
text.NABRE: “I cannot do anything on my own; I judge as I hear, and my judgment is just, because I do not seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
text.NRSV: “I can do nothing on my own. As I hear, I judge; and my judgment is just, because I seek to do not my own will but the will of him who sent me.
text.DRB: I cannot of myself do any thing. As I hear, so I judge: and my judgement is just, because I seek not my will, but the will of him that sent me.
text.Rotherham: I, cannot be doing, of myself, a single thing,—Just as I hear, I judge; and, my judging, is, just,—because I seek not my own will, but the will of him that sent me.

text.KJV: If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.
text.NABRE: Witnesses to Jesus. “If I testify on my own behalf, my testimony cannot be verified.
text.NRSV: “If I testify about myself, my testimony is not true.
text.DRB: If I give testimony of myself, my testimony is not true.
text.Rotherham: If, I, bear witness, concerning myself, my witness, is not, true.

text.KJV: There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.
text.NABRE: But there is another who testifies on my behalf, and I know that the testimony he gives on my behalf is true.
text.NRSV: There is another who testifies on my behalf, and I know that his testimony to me is true.
text.DRB: There is another that giveth testimony of me: and I know that the testimony is true which he giveth of me.
text.Rotherham: Another, it is, who beareth witness concerning me,—and I know that, true, is the witness which he witnesseth concerning me.

text.KJV: Ye sent unto John, and he bare witness unto the truth.
text.NABRE: You sent emissaries to John, and he testified to the truth.
text.NRSV: You sent messengers to John, and he testified to the truth.
text.DRB: You sent to John; and he gave testimony to the truth,
text.Rotherham: Ye, have sent forth unto John,—and he hath borne witness to the truth:—

text.KJV: But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say, that ye might be saved.
text.NABRE: I do not accept testimony from a human being, but I say this so that you may be saved.
text.NRSV: Not that I accept such human testimony, but I say these things so that you may be saved.
text.DRB: But I receive not testimony of man: but I say these things that you may be saved.
text.Rotherham: I, however, not from man, my witness, receive; but, these things, I say, that, ye, may be saved:—

text.KJV: He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.
text.NABRE: He was a burning and shining lamp, and for a while you were content to rejoice in his light.
text.NRSV: He was a burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
text.DRB: He was the lamp burning and shining. And you would for a time rejoice in his light.
text.Rotherham: He, was the burning and shining lamp, and, ye, were willing to exult, for an hour, in his light.

text.KJV: But I have greater witness than that of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me.
text.NABRE: But I have testimony greater than John’s. The works that the Father gave me to accomplish, these works that I perform testify on my behalf that the Father has sent me.
text.NRSV: But I have a testimony greater than John’s. The works that the Father has given me to complete, the very works that I am doing, testify on my behalf that the Father has sent me.
text.DRB: But I have a greater testimony than John. For the works which the Father hath given me to perfit them: the very works themselves which I do, give testimony of me, that the Father hath sent me.
text.Rotherham: But, I, have, as my witness, something greater than John’s; for, the works which the Father hath given me, that I should complete them,—the works themselves which I am doing, bear witness concerning me, that, the Father, hath sent me.

text.KJV: And the Father himself, which hath sent me, hath borne witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his shape.
text.NABRE: Moreover, the Father who sent me has testified on my behalf. But you have never heard his voice nor seen his form,
text.NRSV: And the Father who sent me has himself testified on my behalf. You have never heard his voice or seen his form,
text.DRB: And the Father that sent me, himself hath given testimony of me: neither have you heard his voice at any time, nor seen his shape,
text.Rotherham: And so, the Father who sent me, he, hath borne witness concerning me; Neither, a sound of him, at any time, have ye heard, nor, a form of him, have ye seen;

text.KJV: And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.
text.NABRE: and you do not have his word remaining in you, because you do not believe in the one whom he has sent.
text.NRSV: and you do not have his word abiding in you, because you do not believe him whom he has sent.
text.DRB: and his word you have not remaining in you: because whom he hath sent, him you believe not.
text.Rotherham: And, his word, ye have not, within you abiding—because, as touching him whom, he, sent, in him, ye, believe not.

text.KJV: Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which testify of me.
text.NABRE: You search the scriptures, because you think you have eternal life through them; even they testify on my behalf.
text.NRSV: “You search the scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and it is they that testify on my behalf.
text.DRB: Search the scriptures, for you think in them to have life everlasting: & the same are they that give testimony of me:
text.Rotherham: Ye search the Scriptures, because, ye, think, by them, to have, life age-abiding; and, those [Scriptures], are they which bear witness concerning me:

text.KJV: And ye will not come to me, that ye might have life.
text.NABRE: But you do not want to come to me to have life.
text.NRSV: Yet you refuse to come to me to have life.
text.DRB: and you will not come to me that you may have life.
text.Rotherham: And yet ye choose not to come unto me, that, life, ye may have.

text.KJV: I receive not honour from men.
text.NABRE: Unbelief of Jesus’ Hearers. “I do not accept human praise;
text.NRSV: I do not accept glory from human beings.
text.DRB: Glory of men I receive not.
text.Rotherham: Glory from men, am I not receiving;—

text.KJV: But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you.
text.NABRE: moreover, I know that you do not have the love of God in you.
text.NRSV: But I know that you do not have the love of God in you.
text.DRB: But I have known you, that the love of God you have not in you.
text.Rotherham: But I have taken knowledge of you, that, the love of God, ye have not, in yourselves:—

text.KJV: I am come in my Father’s name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.
text.NABRE: I came in the name of my Father, but you do not accept me; yet if another comes in his own name, you will accept him.
text.NRSV: I have come in my Father’s name, and you do not accept me; if another comes in his own name, you will accept him.
text.DRB: I am come in the name of my Father, and you receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him you will receive.
text.Rotherham: I, have come, in the name of my Father,—and ye are not receiving me: If, another, shall come in his own name, him, ye will receive!

text.KJV: How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that cometh from God only?
text.NABRE: How can you believe, when you accept praise from one another and do not seek the praise that comes from the only God?
text.NRSV: How can you believe when you accept glory from one another and do not seek the glory that comes from the one who alone is God?
text.DRB: How can you believe, that receive glory one of another: and the glory which is of God only, you seek not?
text.Rotherham: How can, ye, believe,—Glory from one another, receiving; and, the glory which is from the only God, are not seeking?

text.KJV: Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust.
text.NABRE: Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father: the one who will accuse you is Moses, in whom you have placed your hope.
text.NRSV: Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father; your accuser is Moses, on whom you have set your hope.
text.DRB: Think not that I will accuse you to the Father. There is that accuseth you, Moyses, in whom you trust.
text.Rotherham: Do not think that, I, will accuse you unto the Father: There, is, who accuseth you, even, Moses,—on whom, ye, have set your hope.

text.KJV: For had ye believed Moses, ye would have believed me: for he wrote of me.
text.NABRE: For if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me, because he wrote about me.
text.NRSV: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, for he wrote about me.
text.DRB: For if you did believe Moyses: you would perhaps believe me also. For of me he hath written.
text.Rotherham: For, if ye had been believing, in Moses, ye would, in that case, have been believing, in me: for, concerning me, he wrote.

text.KJV: But if ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe my words?
text.NABRE: But if you do not believe his writings, how will you believe my words?”
text.NRSV: But if you do not believe what he wrote, how will you believe what I say?”
text.DRB: And if you do not believe his writings: how will you believe my words.
text.Rotherham: But, if, in his writings, ye are not believing, how, in my sayings, will ye believe?

text.NRSV: After this Jesus went to the other side of the Sea of Galilee, also called the Sea of Tiberias.
text.Rotherham: After these things, Jesus went away across the sea of Galilee, that is, of Tiberias;
text.KJV: After these things Jesus went over the sea of Galilee, which is the sea of Tiberias.
text.NABRE: After this, Jesus went across the Sea of Galilee [of Tiberias].
text.DRB: After these things Jesus went beyond the sea of Galilee, which is of Tiberias.

text.NRSV: A large crowd kept following him, because they saw the signs that he was doing for the sick.
text.Rotherham: and there was following him a great multitude, because they had been viewing the signs which he did upon such as were sick.
text.KJV: And a great multitude followed him, because they saw his miracles which he did on them that were diseased.
text.NABRE: A large crowd followed him, because they saw the signs he was performing on the sick.
text.DRB: and a great multitude followed him, because they saw the signs which he did upon those that were sick.

text.NRSV: Jesus went up the mountain and sat down there with his disciples.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus had gone up into the mountain, and, there, was sitting with his disciples.
text.KJV: And Jesus went up into a mountain, and there he sat with his disciples.
text.NABRE: Jesus went up on the mountain, and there he sat down with his disciples.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore went up into the mountain, and there he sat with his Disciples.

text.NRSV: Now the Passover, the festival of the Jews, was near.
text.Rotherham: Now the passover was near, the feast of the Jews.
text.KJV: And the passover, a feast of the Jews, was nigh.
text.NABRE: The Jewish feast of Passover was near.
text.DRB: And the Pasch was at hand, the festival day of the Jews.

text.NRSV: When he looked up and saw a large crowd coming toward him, Jesus said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread for these people to eat?”
text.Rotherham: So then Jesus, lifting up his eyes, and beholding that a great multitude was coming unto him, saith unto Philip—Whence are we to buy loaves, that these may eat?
text.KJV: When Jesus then lifted up his eyes, and saw a great company come unto him, he saith unto Philip, Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?
text.NABRE: When Jesus raised his eyes and saw that a large crowd was coming to him, he said to Philip, “Where can we buy enough food for them to eat?”
text.DRB: When Jesus therefore had lifted up his eyes, and saw that a very great multitude cometh to him, he saith to Philippe: Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?

text.NRSV: He said this to test him, for he himself knew what he was going to do.
text.Rotherham: But, this, he was saying, to test him; for, he himself, knew, what he was about to do.
text.KJV: And this he said to prove him: for he himself knew what he would do.
text.NABRE: He said this to test him, because he himself knew what he was going to do.
text.DRB: And this he said, tempting him. For himself knew what he would do?

text.NRSV: Philip answered him, “Six months’ wages would not buy enough bread for each of them to get a little.”
text.Rotherham: Philip answered him—Two hundred denaries-worth of loaves, are not sufficient for them, that, each one, may take, a little.
text.KJV: Philip answered him, Two hundred pennyworth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may take a little.
text.NABRE: Philip answered him, “Two hundred days’ wages worth of food would not be enough for each of them to have a little [bit].”
text.DRB: Philippe answered him: Two hundred penny worth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every man may take a little piece.

text.NRSV: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,
text.Rotherham: One from among his disciples, Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, saith unto him—
text.KJV: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, saith unto him,
text.NABRE: One of his disciples, Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, said to him,
text.DRB: One of his Disciples, Andrew the brother of Simon Peter, saith to him:

text.NRSV: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish. But what are they among so many people?”
text.Rotherham: There is a little lad here, that hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes,—but, these,—what are they, for such numbers?
text.KJV: There is a lad here, which hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes: but what are they among so many?
text.NABRE: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish; but what good are these for so many?”
text.DRB: There is a boy here that hath five barley loaves, & two fishes; but what are these among so many?

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “Make the people sit down.” Now there was a great deal of grass in the place; so they sat down, about five thousand in all.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said—Make the people recline. Now there was much grass in the place. So the men reclined, to the number of about five thousand.
text.KJV: And Jesus said, Make the men sit down. Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
text.NABRE: Jesus said, “Have the people recline.” Now there was a great deal of grass in that place. So the men reclined, about five thousand in number.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore saith: Make the men to sit down. And there was much grass in the place. The men therefore sat down, in number about five thousand.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus took the loaves, and when he had given thanks, he distributed them to those who were seated; so also the fish, as much as they wanted.
text.Rotherham: Jesus therefore took the loaves, and, giving thanks, went on distributing unto them that reclined; in like manner, of the small fishes also: as much as they were wishing.
text.KJV: And Jesus took the loaves; and when he had given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to them that were set down; and likewise of the fishes as much as they would.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus took the loaves, gave thanks, and distributed them to those who were reclining, and also as much of the fish as they wanted.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore took the loaves; and when he had given thanks, he distributed to them that sat. In like manner also of the fishes as much as they would.

text.NRSV: When they were satisfied, he told his disciples, “Gather up the fragments left over, so that nothing may be lost.”
text.Rotherham: And, when they were well-filled, he saith unto his disciples—Gather up the broken pieces left over, that nothing be lost.
text.KJV: When they were filled, he said unto his disciples, Gather up the fragments that remain, that nothing be lost.
text.NABRE: When they had had their fill, he said to his disciples, “Gather the fragments left over, so that nothing will be wasted.”
text.DRB: And after they were filled, he said to his Disciples: Gather the fragments that are remaining, lest they be lost.

text.NRSV: So they gathered them up, and from the fragments of the five barley loaves, left by those who had eaten, they filled twelve baskets.
text.Rotherham: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets, with broken pieces out of the five barley loaves,—which were left over by them who had eaten.
text.KJV: Therefore they gathered them together, and filled twelve baskets with the fragments of the five barley loaves, which remained over and above unto them that had eaten.
text.NABRE: So they collected them, and filled twelve wicker baskets with fragments from the five barley loaves that had been more than they could eat.
text.DRB: They gathered therefore, and filled twelve baskets with fragments of the five barley loaves, which remained to them that had eaten.

text.NRSV: When the people saw the sign that he had done, they began to say, “This is indeed the prophet who is to come into the world.”
text.Rotherham: The people, therefore, seeing what signs he wrought, began to say—This, is, of a truth, the prophet who was to come into the world.
text.KJV: Then those men, when they had seen the miracle that Jesus did, said, This is of a truth that prophet that should come into the world.
text.NABRE: When the people saw the sign he had done, they said, “This is truly the Prophet, the one who is to come into the world.”
text.DRB: Those men therefore when they had seen what a sign Jesus had done, said, That this is the Prophet indeed that is to come into the world.

text.NRSV: When Jesus realized that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, he withdrew again to the mountain by himself.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, getting to know that they were about to come, and seize him, that they might make him king, retired again into the mountain, himself, alone.
text.KJV: When Jesus therefore perceived that they would come and take him by force, to make him a king, he departed again into a mountain himself alone.
text.NABRE: Since Jesus knew that they were going to come and carry him off to make him king, he withdrew again to the mountain alone. Walking on the Water.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore when he knew that they would come to take him, and make him king, he fled again into the mountain himself alone.

text.NRSV: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,
text.Rotherham: But, when evening came, his disciples went down unto the sea;
text.KJV: And when even was now come, his disciples went down unto the sea,
text.NABRE: When it was evening, his disciples went down to the sea,
text.DRB: And when even was come, his Disciples went down to the sea.

text.NRSV: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
text.Rotherham: and, entering into a boat, were going across the sea into Capernaum. And, dark, already, had it become, and, not yet, had Jesus reached them;
text.KJV: And entered into a ship, and went over the sea toward Capernaum. And it was now dark, and Jesus was not come to them.
text.NABRE: embarked in a boat, and went across the sea to Capernaum. It had already grown dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
text.DRB: And when they were gone up into the ship, they came beyond the sea into Capharnaum: And now it was dark and Jesus was not come unto them.

text.NRSV: The sea became rough because a strong wind was blowing.
text.Rotherham: moreover the sea, by reason of a great wind that blew, was rising high.
text.KJV: And the sea arose by reason of a great wind that blew.
text.NABRE: The sea was stirred up because a strong wind was blowing.
text.DRB: And the sea arose, by reason of a great wind that blew.

text.NRSV: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and coming near the boat, and they were terrified.
text.Rotherham: Having therefore rowed about twenty-five or thirty furlongs, they observe Jesus, walking upon the sea, and, near the boat, coming; and they were affrighted.
text.KJV: So when they had rowed about five and twenty or thirty furlongs, they see Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing nigh unto the ship: and they were afraid.
text.NABRE: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and coming near the boat, and they began to be afraid.
text.DRB: When they had rowed therefore about five and twenty or thirty furlongs, they see Jesus walking upon the sea, and to draw nigh to the ship, and they feared.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “It is I; do not be afraid.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, saith unto them—It is, I: be not affrighted!
text.KJV: But he saith unto them, It is I; be not afraid.
text.NABRE: But he said to them, “It is I. Do not be afraid.”
text.DRB: But he said to them: It is I, fear not.

text.NRSV: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat reached the land toward which they were going.
text.Rotherham: They were willing, therefore, to receive him into the boat; and, straightway, the boat was at land, whither they had been slowly going.
text.KJV: Then they willingly received him into the ship: and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went.
text.NABRE: They wanted to take him into the boat, but the boat immediately arrived at the shore to which they were heading.
text.DRB: They would therefore have taken him into the ship: and forthwith the ship was at the land to which they went.

text.NRSV: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the other side of the sea saw that there had been only one boat there. They also saw that Jesus had not got into the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone away alone.
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, the multitude that was standing on the other side of the sea, saw that there was not, another small-boat, there, save one,—and that Jesus entered not, along with his disciples, unto the boat, but that, alone, his disciples departed:—
text.KJV: The day following, when the people which stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was none other boat there, save that one whereinto his disciples were entered, and that Jesus went not with his disciples into the boat, but that his disciples were gone away alone;
text.NABRE: The Bread of Life Discourse. The next day, the crowd that remained across the sea saw that there had been only one boat there, and that Jesus had not gone along with his disciples in the boat, but only his disciples had left.
text.DRB: The next day, the multitude that stood beyond the sea, saw that there was no other boat there but one, and that Jesus had not entered into the boat with his Disciples, but that his Disciples only were departed.

text.NRSV: Then some boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
text.Rotherham: howbeit there came boats out of Tiberias, near the place where they did eat the bread, after the Lord had given thanks:—
text.KJV: (Howbeit there came other boats from Tiberias nigh unto the place where they did eat bread, after that the Lord had given thanks:)
text.NABRE: Other boats came from Tiberias near the place where they had eaten the bread when the Lord gave thanks.
text.DRB: But other boats came in from Tiberias beside the place where they had eaten the bread, our Lord giving thanks.

text.NRSV: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they themselves got into the boats and went to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, the multitude saw, that, Jesus, was not there, nor yet his disciples, they themselves, got into the small boats, and came unto Capernaum, seeking Jesus;
text.KJV: When the people therefore saw that Jesus was not there, neither his disciples, they also took shipping, and came to Capernaum, seeking for Jesus.
text.NABRE: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they themselves got into boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
text.DRB: When therefore the multitude saw that Jesus was not there, nor his Disciples, they went up into the boats, & came to Capharnaum seeking Jesus.

text.NRSV: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
text.Rotherham: and, finding him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him—Rabbi! when, camest thou, hither?
text.KJV: And when they had found him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him, Rabbi, when camest thou hither?
text.NABRE: And when they found him across the sea they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
text.DRB: And when they had found him beyond the sea, they said to him: Rabbi, when camest thou hither?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Very truly, I tell you, you are looking for me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate your fill of the loaves.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them, and said—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Ye seek me, not because ye saw signs, but because ye did eat of the loaves and were filled.
text.KJV: Jesus answered them and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them and said, “Amen, amen, I say to you, you are looking for me not because you saw signs but because you ate the loaves and were filled.
text.DRB: Jesus answered them, and said: Amen, amen I say to you, you seek me not because you have seen signs, but because you did eat of the loaves, and were filled.

text.NRSV: Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For it is on him that God the Father has set his seal.”
text.Rotherham: Be working, not for the food that perisheth, but for the food that endureth unto life age-abiding,—which, the Son of Man, unto you, will give; for upon, the same, hath the Father, even God, set his seal.
text.KJV: Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.
text.NABRE: Do not work for food that perishes but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For on him the Father, God, has set his seal.”
text.DRB: Work not the meat that perisheth, but that endureth unto life everlasting, which the Son of man will give you. For him the Father, God, hath signed.

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “What must we do to perform the works of God?”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—What are we to do, that we may be working the works of God?
text.KJV: Then said they unto him, What shall we do, that we might work the works of God?
text.NABRE: So they said to him, “What can we do to accomplish the works of God?”
text.DRB: They said therefore unto him: What shall we do that we may work the works of God?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—This, is the work of God: that ye believe on him whom, he, hath sent forth.
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in the one he sent.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered, and said to them: This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he hath sent.

text.NRSV: So they said to him, “What sign are you going to give us then, so that we may see it and believe you? What work are you performing?
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—What, then, art, thou, doing, by way of sign, that we may see, and believe in thee: what art thou working?
text.KJV: They said therefore unto him, What sign shewest thou then, that we may see, and believe thee? what dost thou work?
text.NABRE: So they said to him, “What sign can you do, that we may see and believe in you? What can you do?
text.DRB: They said therefore to him: What sign therefore doest thou, that we may see, and may believe thee? what workest thou?

text.NRSV: Our ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
text.Rotherham: Our fathers, did eat, the manna, in the desert,—just as it is written: Bread out of heaven, he gave them to eat.
text.KJV: Our fathers did eat manna in the desert; as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat.
text.NABRE: Our ancestors ate manna in the desert, as it is written: ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
text.DRB: Our Fathers did eat Manna in the desert as it is written, Bread from Heaven he gave them to eat.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but it is my Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Not Moses, gave you the bread out of heaven; but, my Father, giveth you the real bread out of heaven.
text.KJV: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Moses gave you not that bread from heaven; but my Father giveth you the true bread from heaven.
text.NABRE: So Jesus said to them, “Amen, amen, I say to you, it was not Moses who gave the bread from heaven; my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to them: Amen, amen I say to you, Moyses gave you not the bread from Heaven, but my Father giveth you the true bread from Heaven.

text.NRSV: For the bread of God is that which comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
text.Rotherham: For, the bread of God, is that which is coming down out of heaven, and giving, life, unto the world.
text.KJV: For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven, and giveth life unto the world.
text.NABRE: For the bread of God is that which comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
text.DRB: For the bread of God it is that descended from Heaven, and giveth life to the world.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Sir, give us this bread always.”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—Sir! Evermore, give us this bread.
text.KJV: Then said they unto him, Lord, evermore give us this bread.
text.NABRE: So they said to him, “Sir, give us this bread always.”
text.DRB: They said therefore unto him: Lord, give us always this bread.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never be hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—I, am the bread of life: he that cometh unto me, in nowise shall hunger, and, he that believeth on me, in nowise shall thirst, any more.
text.KJV: And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life; whoever comes to me will never hunger, and whoever believes in me will never thirst.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them: I am the bread of life, he that cometh to me, shall not hunger; and he that believeth in me, shall never thirst.

text.NRSV: But I said to you that you have seen me and yet do not believe.
text.Rotherham: But I told you—Ye have even seen [me], and yet do not believe.
text.KJV: But I said unto you, That ye also have seen me, and believe not.
text.NABRE: But I told you that although you have seen [me], you do not believe.
text.DRB: But I said to you that both you have seen me and you believe not.

text.NRSV: Everything that the Father gives me will come to me, and anyone who comes to me I will never drive away;
text.Rotherham: All that which the Father is giving me, unto me, will have come, and, him that cometh unto me, in nowise will I cast out,—
text.KJV: All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.
text.NABRE: Everything that the Father gives me will come to me, and I will not reject anyone who comes to me,
text.DRB: All that the Father giveth me, shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will not cast forth.

text.NRSV: for I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: Because I have come down from heaven,—Not that I should be doing my own will, but the will of him that sent me.
text.KJV: For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me.
text.NABRE: because I came down from heaven not to do my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
text.DRB: Because I descended from Heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me.

text.NRSV: And this is the will of him who sent me, that I should lose nothing of all that he has given me, but raise it up on the last day.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the will of him that sent me, That, of all that which he hath given me, I should lose nothing, but should raise it up at the last day.
text.KJV: And this is the Father’s will which hath sent me, that of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up again at the last day.
text.NABRE: And this is the will of the one who sent me, that I should not lose anything of what he gave me, but that I should raise it [on] the last day.
text.DRB: For this is the will of him that sent me, the Father: that all that he hath given me I leese not thereof, but raise it in the last day.

text.NRSV: This is indeed the will of my Father, that all who see the Son and believe in him may have eternal life; and I will raise them up on the last day.”
text.Rotherham: For, this, is the will of my Father, That, every one that vieweth the Son, and believeth on him, should have life age-abiding, and, I, should raise him up, at the last day.
text.KJV: And this is the will of him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son, and believeth on him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day.
text.NABRE: For this is the will of my Father, that everyone who sees the Son and believes in him may have eternal life, and I shall raise him [on] the last day.”
text.DRB: And this is the will of my Father that sent me; that every one that seeth the Son, and believeth in him, have life everlasting, & I will raise him in the last day.

text.NRSV: Then the Jews began to complain about him because he said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, began to murmur concerning him, because he said—I, am the bread that came down out of heaven;
text.KJV: The Jews then murmured at him, because he said, I am the bread which came down from heaven.
text.NABRE: The Jews murmured about him because he said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven,”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore murmured at him, because he had said, I am the bread which descended from Heaven;

text.NRSV: They were saying, “Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How can he now say, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”
text.Rotherham: and were saying—Is not, this, Jesus, the son of Joseph,—of whom, we, know the father and the mother! How is it then, that he, now, saith: Out of heaven, have I come down?
text.KJV: And they said, Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? how is it then that he saith, I came down from heaven?
text.NABRE: and they said, “Is this not Jesus, the son of Joseph? Do we not know his father and mother? Then how can he say, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”
text.DRB: and they said: Is not this Jesus the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then saith he, That I descended from Heaven?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Do not complain among yourselves.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—Be not murmuring, one with another:
text.KJV: Jesus therefore answered and said unto them, Murmur not among yourselves.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to them, “Stop murmuring among yourselves.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore answered and said to them: Murmur not one to another:

text.NRSV: No one can come to me unless drawn by the Father who sent me; and I will raise that person up on the last day.
text.Rotherham: No one, can come unto me, except, the Father who sent me, draw him,—and, I, will raise him up, in the last day.
text.KJV: No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him: and I will raise him up at the last day.
text.NABRE: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draw him, and I will raise him on the last day.
text.DRB: no man can come to me, unless the Father that sent me, draw him, and I will raise him up in the last day.

text.NRSV: It is written in the prophets, ‘And they shall all be taught by God.’ Everyone who has heard and learned from the Father comes to me.
text.Rotherham: It is written in the prophets,—And they shall be, all, the instructed of God: Every one who hath heard of the Father, and learned, cometh unto me.
text.KJV: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Every man therefore that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me.
text.NABRE: It is written in the prophets: ‘They shall all be taught by God.’ Everyone who listens to my Father and learns from him comes to me.
text.DRB: It is written in the Prophets: And all shall be docible of God. Every one that hath heard of the Father, & hath learned, cometh to me.

text.NRSV: Not that anyone has seen the Father except the one who is from God; he has seen the Father.
text.Rotherham: Not that any one hath seen, the Father, save he who is from God,—this one, hath seen the Father.
text.KJV: Not that any man hath seen the Father, save he which is of God, he hath seen the Father.
text.NABRE: Not that anyone has seen the Father except the one who is from God; he has seen the Father.
text.DRB: Not that any man hath seen the Father, but he which is of God; this hath seen the Father.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, whoever believes has eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you: He that believeth, hath life age-abiding.
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me hath everlasting life.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, whoever believes has eternal life.
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to you: He that believeth in me, hath life everlasting.

text.NRSV: I am the bread of life.
text.Rotherham: I am the bread of life:—
text.KJV: I am that bread of life.
text.NABRE: I am the bread of life.
text.DRB: I am the bread of life.

text.NRSV: Your ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
text.Rotherham: Your fathers, did eat, in the desert, the manna,—and died:
text.KJV: Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, and are dead.
text.NABRE: Your ancestors ate the manna in the desert, but they died;
text.DRB: Your fathers did eat Manna in the desert; and they died.

text.NRSV: This is the bread that comes down from heaven, so that one may eat of it and not die.
text.Rotherham: This, is the bread which, out of heaven, cometh down, that one, thereof, may eat,—and not die.
text.KJV: This is the bread which cometh down from heaven, that a man may eat thereof, and not die.
text.NABRE: this is the bread that comes down from heaven so that one may eat it and not die.
text.DRB: This is the bread that descended from Heaven: that if any man eat of it, he die not.

text.NRSV: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats of this bread will live forever; and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”
text.Rotherham: I, am the living bread, which, out of heaven, came down: If one eat of this bread, he shall live unto times age-abiding; and, the bread, moreover, which, I, will give, is, my flesh—for the world’s life.
text.KJV: I am the living bread which came down from heaven: if any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever: and the bread that I will give is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.
text.NABRE: I am the living bread that came down from heaven; whoever eats this bread will live forever; and the bread that I will give is my flesh for the life of the world.”
text.DRB: I am the living bread, that came down from Heaven.

text.NRSV: The Jews then disputed among themselves, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, began to strive one with another, saying—How can this one, unto us, give his flesh to eat?
text.KJV: The Jews therefore strove among themselves, saying, How can this man give us his flesh to eat?
text.NABRE: The Jews quarreled among themselves, saying, “How can this man give us [his] flesh to eat?”
text.DRB: If any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever: and the bread which I will give, is my flesh for the life of the world.

text.NRSV: So Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you—Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of Man, and drink his blood, ye have not life within yourselves.
text.KJV: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink his blood, ye have no life in you.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Amen, amen, I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you do not have life within you.
text.DRB: The Jews therefore strove among themselves, saying: How can this man give us his flesh to eat?

text.NRSV: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood have eternal life, and I will raise them up on the last day;
