text.Rotherham: He that feedeth upon my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath life age-abiding, and, I, will raise him up at the last day;
text.KJV: Whoso eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.
text.NABRE: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him on the last day.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to them: Amen, amen I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink his blood, you shall not have life in you.

text.NRSV: for my flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
text.Rotherham: For, my flesh, is, true, food, and, my blood, is, true, drink:
text.KJV: For my flesh is meat indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.
text.NABRE: For my flesh is true food, and my blood is true drink.
text.DRB: He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath life everlasting; and I will raise him up in the last day.

text.NRSV: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood abide in me, and I in them.
text.Rotherham: He that feedeth upon my flesh, and drinketh my blood, in me, abideth, and, I, in him.
text.KJV: He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, dwelleth in me, and I in him.
text.NABRE: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in him.
text.DRB: For my flesh, is meat indeed: and my blood is drink indeed.

text.NRSV: Just as the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me will live because of me.
text.Rotherham: Just as the living Father sent me,—and I live by reason of the Father, he also that feedeth upon me, even he, shall live by reason of me.
text.KJV: As the living Father hath sent me, and I live by the Father: so he that eateth me, even he shall live by me.
text.NABRE: Just as the living Father sent me and I have life because of the Father, so also the one who feeds on me will have life because of me.
text.DRB: He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, abideth in me, and I in him.

text.NRSV: This is the bread that came down from heaven, not like that which your ancestors ate, and they died. But the one who eats this bread will live forever.”
text.Rotherham: This, is the bread, which, out of heaven, came down:—Not just as your fathers did eat—and died! He that feedeth upon this bread, shall live unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: This is that bread which came down from heaven: not as your fathers did eat manna, and are dead: he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever.
text.NABRE: This is the bread that came down from heaven. Unlike your ancestors who ate and still died, whoever eats this bread will live forever.”
text.DRB: As the living Father hath sent me, and I live by the Father: and he that eateth me, the same also shall live by me.

text.NRSV: He said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue at Capernaum.
text.Rotherham: These things, said he, as, in a synagogue, he was teaching, in Capernaum.
text.KJV: These things said he in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.
text.NABRE: These things he said while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum. The Words of Eternal Life.
text.DRB: This is the bread that came down from Heaven. Not as your fathers did eat Manna, and died. He that eateth this bread, shall live for ever.

text.NRSV: When many of his disciples heard it, they said, “This teaching is difficult; who can accept it?”
text.Rotherham: Many, of his disciples, therefore, when they heard, said—Hard, is this discourse,—Who can, thereunto, hearken?
text.KJV: Many therefore of his disciples, when they had heard this, said, This is an hard saying; who can hear it?
text.NABRE: Then many of his disciples who were listening said, “This saying is hard; who can accept it?”
text.DRB: These things he said teaching in the Synagogue, in Capharnaum.

text.NRSV: But Jesus, being aware that his disciples were complaining about it, said to them, “Does this offend you?
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, knowing within himself that his disciples were murmuring concerning this, said unto them—Doth, this, cause, you, to stumble?
text.KJV: When Jesus knew in himself that his disciples murmured at it, he said unto them, Doth this offend you?
text.NABRE: Since Jesus knew that his disciples were murmuring about this, he said to them, “Does this shock you?
text.DRB: Many therefore of his Disciples hearing it, said: This saying is hard, and who can hear it?

text.NRSV: Then what if you were to see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
text.Rotherham: [What] then, if ye should view the Son of Man ascending where he was before? …
text.KJV: What and if ye shall see the Son of man ascend up where he was before?
text.NABRE: What if you were to see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
text.DRB: But Jesus knowing with himself that his Disciples murmured at this, he said to them: Doth this scandalize you?

text.NRSV: It is the spirit that gives life; the flesh is useless. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
text.Rotherham: The spirit, it is, that giveth life,—the flesh, profiteth, nothing: The declarations which, I, have spoken unto you, are, spirit, and, are, life.
text.KJV: It is the spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing: the words that I speak unto you, they are spirit, and they are life.
text.NABRE: It is the spirit that gives life, while the flesh is of no avail. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
text.DRB: If then you shall see the Son of man ascend where he was before?

text.NRSV: But among you there are some who do not believe.” For Jesus knew from the first who were the ones that did not believe, and who was the one that would betray him.
text.Rotherham: But there are some from among you, who do not believe. For Jesus knew from the beginning, who they were that did not believe, and who it was would deliver him up;—
text.KJV: But there are some of you that believe not. For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that believed not, and who should betray him.
text.NABRE: But there are some of you who do not believe.” Jesus knew from the beginning the ones who would not believe and the one who would betray him.
text.DRB: It is the spirit that quickeneth, the flesh profiteth nothing. The words that I have spoken to you, be spirit and life.

text.NRSV: And he said, “For this reason I have told you that no one can come to me unless it is granted by the Father.”
text.Rotherham: and he went on to say—For this cause, have I said unto you: No one, can come unto me, except it have been given him, of the Father.
text.KJV: And he said, Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto me, except it were given unto him of my Father.
text.NABRE: And he said, “For this reason I have told you that no one can come to me unless it is granted him by my Father.”
text.DRB: But there be certain of you that believe not. For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that did not believe, and who he was that would betray him.

text.NRSV: Because of this many of his disciples turned back and no longer went about with him.
text.Rotherham: Because of this, many from among his disciples, went away back, and, no longer, with him, were walking.
text.KJV: From that time many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.
text.NABRE: As a result of this, many [of] his disciples returned to their former way of life and no longer accompanied him.
text.DRB: And he said: Therefore did I say to you, that no man can come to me, unless it be given him of my Father.

text.NRSV: So Jesus asked the twelve, “Do you also wish to go away?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto the twelve—Are, ye also, wishing to withdraw?
text.KJV: Then said Jesus unto the twelve, Will ye also go away?
text.NABRE: Jesus then said to the Twelve, “Do you also want to leave?”
text.DRB: After this many of his Disciples went back: and now they walked not with him.

text.NRSV: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom can we go? You have the words of eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter answered him—Lord! unto whom, shall we go? Declarations of life age-abiding, thou hast;
text.KJV: Then Simon Peter answered him, Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.
text.NABRE: Simon Peter answered him, “Master, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to the Twelve: What, will you also depart?

text.NRSV: We have come to believe and know that you are the Holy One of God.”
text.Rotherham: And, we, have believed, and come to know,—that, thou, art the Holy One of God.
text.KJV: And we believe and are sure that thou art that Christ, the Son of the living God.
text.NABRE: We have come to believe and are convinced that you are the Holy One of God.”
text.DRB: Simon Peter therefore answered him: Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Did I not choose you, the twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—Did not, I, make choice, of you, the twelve? And yet, from among you, one, is, an adversary.
text.KJV: Jesus answered them, Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “Did I not choose you twelve? Yet is not one of you a devil?”
text.DRB: And we believe and have known that thou art Christ the Son of God.

text.NRSV: He was speaking of Judas son of Simon Iscariot, for he, though one of the twelve, was going to betray him.
text.Rotherham: Now he was speaking of Judas, son of Simon Iscariot: for, the same, was about to deliver him up,—one of the twelve.
text.KJV: He spake of Judas Iscariot the son of Simon: for he it was that should betray him, being one of the twelve.
text.NABRE: He was referring to Judas, son of Simon the Iscariot; it was he who would betray him, one of the Twelve.
text.DRB: Jesus answered them: Have not I chosen you the Twelve; and of you one is a devil?
And he meant Judas Iscariot, Simon's son: for this same was to betray him, whereas he was one of the Twelve.

text.NRSV: After this Jesus went about in Galilee. He did not wish to go about in Judea because the Jews were looking for an opportunity to kill him.
text.Rotherham: And, after these things, Jesus was walking in Galilee; for he was not wishing, in Judaea, to be walking, because the Jews were seeking to slay him.
text.KJV: After these things Jesus walked in Galilee: for he would not walk in Jewry, because the Jews sought to kill him.
text.NABRE: After this, Jesus moved about within Galilee; but he did not wish to travel in Judea, because the Jews were trying to kill him.
text.DRB: After these things Jesus walked into Galilee, for he would not walk into Jewry: because the Jews sought to kill him.

text.NRSV: Now the Jewish festival of Booths was near.
text.Rotherham: But the feast of the Jews was near, that of the pitching of tents.
text.KJV: Now the Jews’ feast of tabernacles was at hand.
text.NABRE: But the Jewish feast of Tabernacles was near.
text.DRB: And the festival day of the Jews, Scenopegia, was at hand.

text.NRSV: So his brothers said to him, “Leave here and go to Judea so that your disciples also may see the works you are doing;
text.Rotherham: His brethren, therefore, said unto him—Remove hence, and withdraw into Judaea, that, thy disciples also, may view thy works which thou art doing.
text.KJV: His brethren therefore said unto him, Depart hence, and go into Judea, that thy disciples also may see the works that thou doest.
text.NABRE: So his brothers said to him, “Leave here and go to Judea, so that your disciples also may see the works you are doing.
text.DRB: And his brethren said to him: Pass from hence, and go into Jewry; that thy Disciples also may see thy works which thou doest.

text.NRSV: for no one who wants to be widely known acts in secret. If you do these things, show yourself to the world.”
text.Rotherham: For, no one, anything in secret, doeth, and yet is seeking, himself, to be well-known. If, these things, thou art doing, manifest thyself unto the world.
text.KJV: For there is no man that doeth any thing in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly. If thou do these things, shew thyself to the world.
text.NABRE: No one works in secret if he wants to be known publicly. If you do these things, manifest yourself to the world.”
text.DRB: For no man doeth any thing in secret, and seeketh himself to be in public. If thou do these things, manifest thyself to the world.

text.NRSV: (For not even his brothers believed in him.)
text.Rotherham: For, not even his brethren, were believing on him.
text.KJV: For neither did his brethren believe in him.
text.NABRE: For his brothers did not believe in him.
text.DRB: For neither did his brethren believe in him.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “My time has not yet come, but your time is always here.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, saith unto them—My season, not yet, is here; but, your season, at anytime, is ready:
text.KJV: Then Jesus said unto them, My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready.
text.NABRE: So Jesus said to them, “My time is not yet here, but the time is always right for you.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore saith to them: My time is not yet come; but your time is always ready.

text.NRSV: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I testify against it that its works are evil.
text.Rotherham: The world cannot hate you; but, me, it hateth, because, I, bear witness concerning it, that, the works thereof, are, wicked.
text.KJV: The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil.
text.NABRE: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me, because I testify to it that its works are evil.
text.DRB: The world cannot hate you, but me it hateth: because I give testimony of it, that the works thereof are evil.

text.NRSV: Go to the festival yourselves. I am not going to this festival, for my time has not yet fully come.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, go up unto the feast: I, not yet, am coming up unto this feast, because, my season, not yet, hath fully come.
text.KJV: Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.
text.NABRE: You go up to the feast. I am not going up to this feast, because my time has not yet been fulfilled.”
text.DRB: Go you up to this festival day: I go not up to this festival day; because my time is not yet accomplished.

text.NRSV: After saying this, he remained in Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, having said unto them, he was still abiding in Galilee.
text.KJV: When he had said these words unto them, he abode still in Galilee.
text.NABRE: After he had said this, he stayed on in Galilee.
text.DRB: When he had said these things, himself tarried in Galilee.

text.NRSV: But after his brothers had gone to the festival, then he also went, not publicly but as it were in secret.
text.Rotherham: But, when his brethren had gone up unto the feast, then, he also, went up,—not openly, but as it were in secret.
text.KJV: But when his brethren were gone up, then went he also up unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.
text.NABRE: But when his brothers had gone up to the feast, he himself also went up, not openly but [as it were] in secret.
text.DRB: But after his brethren were gone up, then he also went up to the festival day, not openly, but as it were in secret.

text.NRSV: The Jews were looking for him at the festival and saying, “Where is he?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, were seeking him in the feast, and were saying—Where is that one?
text.KJV: Then the Jews sought him at the feast, and said, Where is he?
text.NABRE: The Jews were looking for him at the feast and saying, “Where is he?”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore sought him in the festival day, and said: Where is he?

text.NRSV: And there was considerable complaining about him among the crowds. While some were saying, “He is a good man,” others were saying, “No, he is deceiving the crowd.”
text.Rotherham: and there was great, murmuring, about him among the multitudes: some, indeed, were saying—Good, is he! [but], others, were saying—Not so! but he is leading the multitude astray.
text.KJV: And there was much murmuring among the people concerning him: for some said, He is a good man: others said, Nay; but he deceiveth the people.
text.NABRE: And there was considerable murmuring about him in the crowds. Some said, “He is a good man,” [while] others said, “No; on the contrary, he misleads the crowd.”
text.DRB: And there was much murmuring in the multitude of him. For certain said: That he is good. And others said: No, but he seduceth the multitudes.

text.NRSV: Yet no one would speak openly about him for fear of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: No one, nevertheless, was speaking, openly, about him, because of their fear of the Jews.
text.KJV: Howbeit no man spake openly of him for fear of the Jews.
text.NABRE: Still, no one spoke openly about him because they were afraid of the Jews. The First Dialogue.
text.DRB: Yet no man spake openly of him for fear of the Jews.

text.NRSV: About the middle of the festival Jesus went up into the temple and began to teach.
text.Rotherham: Presently, however, the feast being at its height, Jesus went up unto the temple and was teaching.
text.KJV: Now about the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple, and taught.
text.NABRE: When the feast was already half over, Jesus went up into the temple area and began to teach.
text.DRB: And when the festivity was now half done, Jesus went up into the temple, and taught.

text.NRSV: The Jews were astonished at it, saying, “How does this man have such learning, when he has never been taught?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, began to marvel, saying—How, doth this one know, letters, not having learned?
text.KJV: And the Jews marvelled, saying, How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?
text.NABRE: The Jews were amazed and said, “How does he know scripture without having studied?”
text.DRB: And the Jews marveled, saying: How doth this man know letters, whereas he hath not learned?

text.NRSV: Then Jesus answered them, “My teaching is not mine but his who sent me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, answered them, and said—My teaching, is not mine, but his who sent me.
text.KJV: Jesus answered them, and said, My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them and said, “My teaching is not my own but is from the one who sent me.
text.DRB: Jesus answered them, and said: My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me.

text.NRSV: Anyone who resolves to do the will of God will know whether the teaching is from God or whether I am speaking on my own.
text.Rotherham: If any one intend, his will, to do, he shall get to know concerning the teaching—whether it is, of God, or, I, from myself, am speaking,
text.KJV: If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself.
text.NABRE: Whoever chooses to do his will shall know whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
text.DRB: If any man will do the will of him, he shall understand of the doctrine whether it be of God, or I speak of myself.

text.NRSV: Those who speak on their own seek their own glory; but the one who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and there is nothing false in him.
text.Rotherham: He that, from himself, doth speak, his own glory, is seeking: he that seeketh the glory of him that sent him, the same, is, true, and, injustice, in him, is there none.
text.KJV: He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.
text.NABRE: Whoever speaks on his own seeks his own glory, but whoever seeks the glory of the one who sent him is truthful, and there is no wrong in him.
text.DRB: He that speaketh of himself, seeketh his own glory. But he that seeketh the glory of him that sent him, he is true, and injustice in him there is not.

text.NRSV: “Did not Moses give you the law? Yet none of you keeps the law. Why are you looking for an opportunity to kill me?”
text.Rotherham: Did not, Moses, give you the law? And yet, not one of you, doeth the law!—Why, seek ye, to slay, me?
text.KJV: Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?
text.NABRE: Did not Moses give you the law? Yet none of you keeps the law. Why are you trying to kill me?”
text.DRB: Did not Moyses give you the law, and none of you doeth the law?

text.NRSV: The crowd answered, “You have a demon! Who is trying to kill you?”
text.Rotherham: The multitude answered—A demon, thou hast: Who, is seeking, to slay, thee?
text.KJV: The people answered and said, Thou hast a devil: who goeth about to kill thee?
text.NABRE: The crowd answered, “You are possessed! Who is trying to kill you?”
text.DRB: Why seek you to kill me? The multitude answered, and said, Thou hast a devil, who seeketh to kill thee?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “I performed one work, and all of you are astonished.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—One work, I did, and ye, all, are marvelling:
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto them, I have done one work, and ye all marvel.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to them, “I performed one work and all of you are amazed
text.DRB: Jesus answered and said to them: One work I have done; and you do all marvel.

text.NRSV: Moses gave you circumcision (it is, of course, not from Moses, but from the patriarchs), and you circumcise a man on the sabbath.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, Moses, hath given you, circumcision,—not that, of Moses, it is, but of the fathers;—and, on Sabbath, ye circumcise a man.
text.KJV: Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man.
text.NABRE: because of it. Moses gave you circumcision—not that it came from Moses but rather from the patriarchs—and you circumcise a man on the sabbath.
text.DRB: Therefore Moyses gave you circumcision: not that it is of Moyses, but of the fathers, and in the Sabboth you circumcise a man.

text.NRSV: If a man receives circumcision on the sabbath in order that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me because I healed a man’s whole body on the sabbath?
text.Rotherham: If, circumcision, a man receiveth, on Sabbath, that the law of Moses may not be broken, with me, are ye bitter as gall, because, a whole man, I made, well, on Sabbath?
text.KJV: If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?
text.NABRE: If a man can receive circumcision on a sabbath so that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me because I made a whole person well on a sabbath?
text.DRB: If a man receive circumcision in the Sabboth, that the law of Moyses be not broken; are you angry at me because I have healed a man wholly in the Sabboth?

text.NRSV: Do not judge by appearances, but judge with right judgment.”
text.Rotherham: Be not judging according to appearance, but, just judgment, be judging,
text.KJV: Judge not according to the appearance, but judge righteous judgment.
text.NABRE: Stop judging by appearances, but judge justly.”
text.DRB: Judge not according to the face, but judge just judgement.

text.NRSV: Now some of the people of Jerusalem were saying, “Is not this the man whom they are trying to kill?
text.Rotherham: So then, some of them of Jerusalem were saying—Is not, this, he whom they are seeking to kill?
text.KJV: Then said some of them of Jerusalem, Is not this he, whom they seek to kill?
text.NABRE: So some of the inhabitants of Jerusalem said, “Is he not the one they are trying to kill?
text.DRB: Certain therefore of Jerusalem said: Is not this he whom they seek to kill?

text.NRSV: And here he is, speaking openly, but they say nothing to him! Can it be that the authorities really know that this is the Messiah?
text.Rotherham: And yet, see! with freedom of speech, he is talking, and, nothing, unto him, do they say:—Have the rulers perhaps come to know, of a truth, that, this, is, the Christ?
text.KJV: But, lo, he speaketh boldly, and they say nothing unto him. Do the rulers know indeed that this is the very Christ?
text.NABRE: And look, he is speaking openly and they say nothing to him. Could the authorities have realized that he is the Messiah?
text.DRB: And behold, he speaketh openly, and they say nothing to him. Have the Princes known indeed that this is Christ?

text.NRSV: Yet we know where this man is from; but when the Messiah comes, no one will know where he is from.”
text.Rotherham: But, as for this one, we know whence he is; The Christ, however, whensoever he shall come, no one, getteth to know whence he is.
text.KJV: Howbeit we know this man whence he is: but when Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence he is.
text.NABRE: But we know where he is from. When the Messiah comes, no one will know where he is from.”
text.DRB: But this man we know whence he is. But when Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence he is.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus cried out as he was teaching in the temple, “You know me, and you know where I am from. I have not come on my own. But the one who sent me is true, and you do not know him.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, cried aloud in the temple, teaching, and saying—Both me, ye know, and ye know whence I am,—and yet, of myself, have I not come, but he is real, who sent me, whom, ye, know not.
text.KJV: Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught, saying, Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye know not.
text.NABRE: So Jesus cried out in the temple area as he was teaching and said, “You know me and also know where I am from. Yet I did not come on my own, but the one who sent me, whom you do not know, is true.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore cried in the temple teaching and saying: Both me you do know, and whence I am you know. And of myself I am not come, but he is true that sent me, whom you know not.

text.NRSV: I know him, because I am from him, and he sent me.”
text.Rotherham: I, know him, because, from him, I am, and, he, sent me forth.
text.KJV: But I know him: for I am from him, and he hath sent me.
text.NABRE: I know him, because I am from him, and he sent me.”
text.DRB: I know him, because I am of him, and he sent me.

text.NRSV: Then they tried to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him, because his hour had not yet come.
text.Rotherham: They were seeking therefore to seize him; and yet no one laid on him his hand, because, not yet, had come his hour.
text.KJV: Then they sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him, because his hour was not yet come.
text.NABRE: So they tried to arrest him, but no one laid a hand upon him, because his hour had not yet come.
text.DRB: They sought therefore to apprehend him: and no man laid hands upon him, because his hour was not yet come.

text.NRSV: Yet many in the crowd believed in him and were saying, “When the Messiah comes, will he do more signs than this man has done?”
text.Rotherham: But, from amongst the multitude, many, believed on him, and were saying—The Christ, whensoever he cometh, greater signs, will he do, than those which, this one, did?
text.KJV: And many of the people believed on him, and said, When Christ cometh, will he do more miracles than these which this man hath done?
text.NABRE: But many of the crowd began to believe in him, and said, “When the Messiah comes, will he perform more signs than this man has done?” Officers Sent to Arrest Jesus.
text.DRB: But of the multitude many believed in him, and said: Christ, when he cometh shall he do more signs than these which this man doeth?

text.NRSV: The Pharisees heard the crowd muttering such things about him, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent temple police to arrest him.
text.Rotherham: The Pharisees hearkened unto the multitude murmuring concerning him these things; and the High-priests and the Pharisees sent forth officers, that they might seize him.
text.KJV: The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.
text.NABRE: The Pharisees heard the crowd murmuring about him to this effect, and the chief priests and the Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
text.DRB: The Pharisees heard the multitude murmuring these things touching him: and the Princes and Pharisees sent Ministers to apprehend him.

text.NRSV: Jesus then said, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I am going to him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said—Yet a little time, I am, with you, and withdraw unto him that sent me:
text.KJV: Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me.
text.NABRE: So Jesus said, “I will be with you only a little while longer, and then I will go to the one who sent me.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to them: Yet a little time I am with you: and I go to him that sent me.

text.NRSV: You will search for me, but you will not find me; and where I am, you cannot come.”
text.Rotherham: Ye shall seek me, and not find me, and, where, I, am, ye, cannot come.
text.KJV: Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.
text.NABRE: You will look for me but not find [me], and where I am you cannot come.”
text.DRB: You seek me, and shall not find: and where I am, you cannot come.

text.NRSV: The Jews said to one another, “Where does this man intend to go that we will not find him? Does he intend to go to the Dispersion among the Greeks and teach the Greeks?
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, said unto themselves—Whither, is, this one, about to go, that we shall not find him? Unto the dispersion of the Greeks, is he about to go, and teach the Greeks?
text.KJV: Then said the Jews among themselves, Whither will he go, that we shall not find him? will he go unto the dispersed among the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?
text.NABRE: So the Jews said to one another, “Where is he going that we will not find him? Surely he is not going to the dispersion among the Greeks to teach the Greeks, is he?
text.DRB: The Jews therefore said among themselves, whither will this man go, that we shall not find him? will he go into the dispersion of the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?

text.NRSV: What does he mean by saying, ‘You will search for me and you will not find me’ and ‘Where I am, you cannot come’?”
text.Rotherham: What, is this which he said: Ye shall seek me and not find me, and, where, I, am, ye, cannot come?
text.KJV: What manner of saying is this that he said, Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come?
text.NABRE: What is the meaning of his saying, ‘You will look for me and not find [me], and where I am you cannot come’?” Rivers of Living Water.
text.DRB: What is this saying that he hath said: You shall seek me, and shall not find; and where I am, you cannot come.

text.NRSV: On the last day of the festival, the great day, while Jesus was standing there, he cried out, “Let anyone who is thirsty come to me,
text.Rotherham: Now, on the last—the great—day of the feast, Jesus was standing, and he cried aloud, saying—If any man thirst, let him come unto me and drink:
text.KJV: In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.
text.NABRE: On the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood up and exclaimed, “Let anyone who thirsts come to me and drink.
text.DRB: And in the last, the great day of the festivity Jesus stood and cried, saying: If any man thirst, let him come to me, and drink.

text.NRSV: and let the one who believes in me drink. As the scripture has said, ‘Out of the believer’s heart shall flow rivers of living water.’”
text.Rotherham: He that believeth on me—just as said the Scripture,—River, from within him, shall flow, of living water.
text.KJV: He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.
text.NABRE: Whoever believes in me, as scripture says: ‘Rivers of living water will flow from within him.’”
text.DRB: He that believeth in me, as the scripture saith, Out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.

text.NRSV: Now he said this about the Spirit, which believers in him were to receive; for as yet there was no Spirit, because Jesus was not yet glorified.
text.Rotherham: Now, this, spake he concerning the Spirit which they who believed on him were about to receive; for, not yet, was there Spirit, because, Jesus, not yet, was glorified!
text.KJV: (But this spake he of the Spirit, which they that believe on him should receive: for the Holy Ghost was not yet given; because that Jesus was not yet glorified.)
text.NABRE: He said this in reference to the Spirit that those who came to believe in him were to receive. There was, of course, no Spirit yet, because Jesus had not yet been glorified. Discussion About the Origins of the Messiah.
text.DRB: (And this he said of the Spirit that they should receive which believed in him. For as yet the Spirit was not given: because Jesus was not yet glorified.)

text.NRSV: When they heard these words, some in the crowd said, “This is really the prophet.”
text.Rotherham: Some from amongst the multitude, therefore, having hearkened unto these words, were saying—This, is, in truth, the prophet.
text.KJV: Many of the people therefore, when they heard this saying, said, Of a truth this is the Prophet.
text.NABRE: Some in the crowd who heard these words said, “This is truly the Prophet.”
text.DRB: Of that multitude therefore, when they had heard these words of his, some said: This is the Prophet indeed.

text.NRSV: Others said, “This is the Messiah.” But some asked, “Surely the Messiah does not come from Galilee, does he?
text.Rotherham: Others, were saying—This, is, the Christ. But, others, were saying—Out of Galilee, is, the Christ, to come?
text.KJV: Others said, This is the Christ. But some said, Shall Christ come out of Galilee?
text.NABRE: Others said, “This is the Messiah.” But others said, “The Messiah will not come from Galilee, will he?
text.DRB: Others said: This is Christ. But certain said: Why, doth Christ come from Galilee?

text.NRSV: Has not the scripture said that the Messiah is descended from David and comes from Bethlehem, the village where David lived?”
text.Rotherham: Hath not the Scripture said: Of the seed of David, and from Bethlehem, the village where David was, cometh the Christ?
text.KJV: Hath not the scripture said, That Christ cometh of the seed of David, and out of the town of Bethlehem, where David was?
text.NABRE: Does not scripture say that the Messiah will be of David’s family and come from Bethlehem, the village where David lived?”
text.DRB: Doth not the scripture say: That of the seed of David, and from Bethlehem the town where David was, Christ doth come?

text.NRSV: So there was a division in the crowd because of him.
text.Rotherham: A division, therefore, arose in the multitude, because of him.
text.KJV: So there was a division among the people because of him.
text.NABRE: So a division occurred in the crowd because of him.
text.DRB: Therefore there arose dissension in the multitude for him.

text.NRSV: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him.
text.Rotherham: Certain, indeed, from among them, were wishing to seize him; but, no one, thrust upon him his hands.
text.KJV: And some of them would have taken him; but no man laid hands on him.
text.NABRE: Some of them even wanted to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him.
text.DRB: And certain of them would have apprehended him: but no man laid hands upon him.

text.NRSV: Then the temple police went back to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, “Why did you not arrest him?”
text.Rotherham: So then the officers came unto the High-priests and Pharisees, and, they, said unto them—For what cause, have ye not brought him?
text.KJV: Then came the officers to the chief priests and Pharisees; and they said unto them, Why have ye not brought him?
text.NABRE: So the guards went to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, “Why did you not bring him?”
text.DRB: The ministers therefore came to the chief Priests and the Pharisees. And they said to them: Why have you not brought him?

text.NRSV: The police answered, “Never has anyone spoken like this!”
text.Rotherham: The officers answered—Never, man spake thus!
text.KJV: The officers answered, Never man spake like this man.
text.NABRE: The guards answered, “Never before has anyone spoken like this one.”
text.DRB: The Ministers answered: Never did there man so speak, as this man.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees replied, “Surely you have not been deceived too, have you?
text.Rotherham: The Pharisees, therefore, answered them—Surely, ye also, have not been led astray?
text.KJV: Then answered them the Pharisees, Are ye also deceived?
text.NABRE: So the Pharisees answered them, “Have you also been deceived?
text.DRB: The Pharisees therefore answered them: Why, are you also seduced?

text.NRSV: Has any one of the authorities or of the Pharisees believed in him?
text.Rotherham: Surely, none of the rulers, hath believed in him, nor of the Pharisees?
text.KJV: Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed on him?
text.NABRE: Have any of the authorities or the Pharisees believed in him?
text.DRB: Hath any of the Princes believed in him, or of the Pharisees?

text.NRSV: But this crowd, which does not know the law—they are accursed.”
text.Rotherham: But, this multitude, that take no note of the law, are, laid under a curse.
text.KJV: But this people who knoweth not the law are cursed.
text.NABRE: But this crowd, which does not know the law, is accursed.”
text.DRB: but this multitude that knoweth not the law, are accursed.

text.NRSV: Nicodemus, who had gone to Jesus before, and who was one of them, asked,
text.Rotherham: Nicodemus saith unto them—he that came unto him formerly, being, one, from among them—
text.KJV: Nicodemus saith unto them, (he that came to Jesus by night, being one of them,)
text.NABRE: Nicodemus, one of their members who had come to him earlier, said to them,
text.DRB: Nicodemus said to them, he that came to him by night, who was one of them:

text.NRSV: “Our law does not judge people without first giving them a hearing to find out what they are doing, does it?”
text.Rotherham: Surely, our law, doth not judge the man, unless it hear first from him, and get to know what he is doing?
text.KJV: Doth our law judge any man, before it hear him, and know what he doeth?
text.NABRE: “Does our law condemn a person before it first hears him and finds out what he is doing?”
text.DRB: Doth our law judge a man, unless it first hear him, and know what he doeth?

text.NRSV: They replied, “Surely you are not also from Galilee, are you? Search and you will see that no prophet is to arise from Galilee.” [
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—Surely, thou also, art not, of Galilee? Search and see! That, out of Galilee, a prophet is not to arise.
text.KJV: They answered and said unto him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look: for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.
text.NABRE: They answered and said to him, “You are not from Galilee also, are you? Look and see that no prophet arises from Galilee.”
text.DRB: They answered & said to him: Why, art thou also a Galilæan? Search, & see that from Galilee a Prophet riseth not.

text.NRSV: Then each of them went home,
text.Rotherham: [[And they went, each unto his own house;
text.KJV: And every man went unto his own house.
text.NABRE: [ Then each went to his own house,
text.DRB: And every man returned to his house.

text.NRSV: while Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
text.Rotherham: but, Jesus, went unto the Mount of Olives.
text.KJV: Jesus went unto the mount of Olives.
text.NABRE: while Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
text.DRB: And Jesus went into the Mount-olivet:

text.NRSV: Early in the morning he came again to the temple. All the people came to him and he sat down and began to teach them.
text.Rotherham: And, early in the morning, again, came he into the temple, and all the people were coming unto him; and, sitting down, he began to teach them.
text.KJV: And early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came unto him; and he sat down, and taught them.
text.NABRE: But early in the morning he arrived again in the temple area, and all the people started coming to him, and he sat down and taught them.
text.DRB: and early in the morning again he came into the temple, and the people came to him, and sitting he taught them.

text.NRSV: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in adultery; and making her stand before all of them,
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and the Pharisees bring a woman, caught, in adultery; and, setting her in the midst,
text.KJV: And the scribes and Pharisees brought unto him a woman taken in adultery; and when they had set her in the midst,
text.NABRE: Then the scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in adultery and made her stand in the middle.
text.DRB: And the Scribes and Pharisees bring a woman taken in adultery; and they did set her in the midst,

text.NRSV: they said to him, “Teacher, this woman was caught in the very act of committing adultery.
text.Rotherham: they say unto him—Teacher! this woman, hath been caught in the very act of committing adultery!
text.KJV: They say unto him, Master, this woman was taken in adultery, in the very act.
text.NABRE: They said to him, “Teacher, this woman was caught in the very act of committing adultery.
text.DRB: And said to him: Master, this woman was even now taken in adultery.

text.NRSV: Now in the law Moses commanded us to stone such women. Now what do you say?”
text.Rotherham: Now, in the law, Moses, [unto us] gave command to stone, such as these. What, then, dost, thou, say?
text.KJV: Now Moses in the law commanded us, that such should be stoned: but what sayest thou?
text.NABRE: Now in the law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. So what do you say?”
text.DRB: And in the law Moyses commanded us to stone such. What sayest thou therefore?

text.NRSV: They said this to test him, so that they might have some charge to bring against him. Jesus bent down and wrote with his finger on the ground.
text.Rotherham: [This, however, they were saying, by way of testing him,—that they might have whereof to accuse him.] But, Jesus, stooping down, with his finger, wrote in the ground.
text.KJV: This they said, tempting him, that they might have to accuse him. But Jesus stooped down, and with his finger wrote on the ground, as though he heard them not.
text.NABRE: They said this to test him, so that they could have some charge to bring against him. Jesus bent down and began to write on the ground with his finger.
text.DRB: And this they said tempting him, that they might accuse him. But Jesus bowing himself down, with his finger wrote in the earth.

text.NRSV: When they kept on questioning him, he straightened up and said to them, “Let anyone among you who is without sin be the first to throw a stone at her.”
text.Rotherham: When, however, they still continued questioning him, he lifted himself up and said [unto them] —He of you, that is without sin, let him first cast at her a stone;
text.KJV: So when they continued asking him, he lifted up himself, and said unto them, He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.
text.NABRE: But when they continued asking him, he straightened up and said to them, “Let the one among you who is without sin be the first to throw a stone at her.”
text.DRB: When they therefore continued asking him, he lifted up himself, and said to them: He that is without sin of you, let him first throw the stone at her.

text.NRSV: And once again he bent down and wrote on the ground.
text.Rotherham: and, again stooping down, he wrote in the ground.
text.KJV: And again he stooped down, and wrote on the ground.
text.NABRE: Again he bent down and wrote on the ground.
text.DRB: And again bowing himself, he wrote in the earth.

text.NRSV: When they heard it, they went away, one by one, beginning with the elders; and Jesus was left alone with the woman standing before him.
text.Rotherham: And, they who heard, began to go out, one by one, beginning from the elders,—and he was left, alone; the woman also, being, in the midst.
text.KJV: And they which heard it, being convicted by their own conscience, went out one by one, beginning at the eldest, even unto the last: and Jesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.
text.NABRE: And in response, they went away one by one, beginning with the elders. So he was left alone with the woman before him.
text.DRB: And they hearing, went out one by one, beginning at the seniors: and Jesus alone remained, and the woman standing in the midst.

text.NRSV: Jesus straightened up and said to her, “Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?”
text.Rotherham: And lifting himself up, Jesus said unto her—Woman, where are they? Hath, no one, condemned thee?
text.KJV: When Jesus had lifted up himself, and saw none but the woman, he said unto her, Woman, where are those thine accusers? hath no man condemned thee?
text.NABRE: Then Jesus straightened up and said to her, “Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?”
text.DRB: And Jesus lifting up himself, said to her: Woman, where are they that accused thee? Hath no man condemned thee?

text.NRSV: She said, “No one, sir.” And Jesus said, “Neither do I condemn you. Go your way, and from now on do not sin again.”]
text.Rotherham: And, she, said—No one, Sir! And Jesus said—Neither do, I, condemn thee,—be going thy way: Henceforth, be sinning, no more.]]
text.KJV: She said, No man, Lord. And Jesus said unto her, Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.
text.NABRE: She replied, “No one, sir.” Then Jesus said, “Neither do I condemn you. Go, [and] from now on do not sin any more.”] The Light of the World.
text.DRB: Who said: No man, Lord. And Jesus said: Neither will I condemn thee. Go, and now sin no more.

text.NRSV: Again Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness but will have the light of life.”
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, unto them, spake Jesus, saying—I, am the light of the world: He that followeth me, shall in nowise walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.
text.KJV: Then spake Jesus again unto them, saying, I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.
text.NABRE: Jesus spoke to them again, saying, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.”
text.DRB: Again therefore Jesus spake to them, saying: I am the light of the world: he that followeth me, walketh not in darkness, but shall have the light of life.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees said to him, “You are testifying on your own behalf; your testimony is not valid.”
text.Rotherham: The Pharisees, therefore, said unto him—Thou, concerning thyself, bearest witness: thy witness, is not true.
text.KJV: The Pharisees therefore said unto him, Thou bearest record of thyself; thy record is not true.
text.NABRE: So the Pharisees said to him, “You testify on your own behalf, so your testimony cannot be verified.”
text.DRB: The Pharisees therefore said to him: Thou givest testimony of thyself; thy testimony is not true.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Even if I testify on my own behalf, my testimony is valid because I know where I have come from and where I am going, but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—Even though, I, bear witness concerning myself, true, is my witness, because I know whence I came, and whither I go; but, ye, know not whence I come, and whither I go.
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto them, Though I bear record of myself, yet my record is true: for I know whence I came, and whither I go; but ye cannot tell whence I come, and whither I go.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to them, “Even if I do testify on my own behalf, my testimony can be verified, because I know where I came from and where I am going. But you do not know where I come from or where I am going.
text.DRB: Jesus answered, and said to them: Although I do give testimony of myself, my testimony is true: because I know whence I came, & whither I go: but you know not whence I come, or whither I go.

text.NRSV: You judge by human standards; I judge no one.
text.Rotherham: Ye, according to the flesh, do judge: I, am judging no one.
text.KJV: Ye judge after the flesh; I judge no man.
text.NABRE: You judge by appearances, but I do not judge anyone.
text.DRB: You judge according to the flesh: I do not judge any man.

text.NRSV: Yet even if I do judge, my judgment is valid; for it is not I alone who judge, but I and the Father who sent me.
text.Rotherham: And, even if, I, am judging, my judging, is, genuine,—because, alone, am I not, but, I, and the Father who sent me;
text.KJV: And yet if I judge, my judgment is true: for I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me.
text.NABRE: And even if I should judge, my judgment is valid, because I am not alone, but it is I and the Father who sent me.
text.DRB: And if I do judge, my judgement is true: because I am not alone, but I and he that sent me, the Father.

text.NRSV: In your law it is written that the testimony of two witnesses is valid.
text.Rotherham: And, in your own law, it is written, that, two men’s witness, is, true:
text.KJV: It is also written in your law, that the testimony of two men is true.
text.NABRE: Even in your law it is written that the testimony of two men can be verified.
text.DRB: And in your law it is written, that the testimony of two men is true.

text.NRSV: I testify on my own behalf, and the Father who sent me testifies on my behalf.”
text.Rotherham: I, am the one bearing witness of myself, and the Father who sent me is bearing witness concerning me.
text.KJV: I am one that bear witness of myself, and the Father that sent me beareth witness of me.
text.NABRE: I testify on my behalf and so does the Father who sent me.”
text.DRB: I am he that give testimony of myself: and he that sent me, the Father, giveth testimony of me.

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”
text.Rotherham: They were saying unto him, therefore—Where, is thy father? Jesus answered—Neither, me, do ye know, nor yet my Father: If, me, ye had known, my Father also, had ye known.
text.KJV: Then said they unto him, Where is thy Father? Jesus answered, Ye neither know me, nor my Father: if ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also.
text.NABRE: So they said to him, “Where is your father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”
text.DRB: They said therefore to him: Where is thy Father? Jesus answered, Neither me do you know, nor my Father. If you did know me, perhaps you might know my Father also.

text.NRSV: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the treasury of the temple, but no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.
text.Rotherham: These sayings, spake he in the treasury, teaching in the temple, and yet, no one, seized him, because, not yet, had come his hour.
text.KJV: These words spake Jesus in the treasury, as he taught in the temple: and no man laid hands on him; for his hour was not yet come.
text.NABRE: He spoke these words while teaching in the treasury in the temple area. But no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come. Jesus, the Father’s Ambassador.
text.DRB: These words Jesus spake in the Treasury, teaching in the temple, and no man apprehended him, because his hour was not yet come.

text.NRSV: Again he said to them, “I am going away, and you will search for me, but you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come.”
text.Rotherham: He said unto them again, therefore—I, go, and ye shall seek me,—and yet, in your sin, shall ye die: Whither, I, go, ye, cannot come.
text.KJV: Then said Jesus again unto them, I go my way, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your sins: whither I go, ye cannot come.
text.NABRE: He said to them again, “I am going away and you will look for me, but you will die in your sin. Where I am going you cannot come.”
text.DRB: Again therefore Jesus said to them: I go, and you shall seek me, and shall die in your sin. Whither I go, you cannot come.

text.NRSV: Then the Jews said, “Is he going to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come’?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, were saying—Can it be that he will kill himself, that he saith, Whither, I, go, ye, cannot come?
text.KJV: Then said the Jews, Will he kill himself? because he saith, Whither I go, ye cannot come.
text.NABRE: So the Jews said, “He is not going to kill himself, is he, because he said, ‘Where I am going you cannot come’?”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore said: Why, will he kill himself, because he saith: Whither I go, you cannot come?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “You are from below, I am from above; you are of this world, I am not of this world.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—Ye, are, of the realms below, I, am, of the realms above: Ye, are, of this world, I, am not of this world.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Ye are from beneath; I am from above: ye are of this world; I am not of this world.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “You belong to what is below, I belong to what is above. You belong to this world, but I do not belong to this world.
text.DRB: And he said to them: You are from beneath, I am from above. You are of this world, I am not of this world.

text.NRSV: I told you that you would die in your sins, for you will die in your sins unless you believe that I am he.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore said I unto you, Ye shall die in your sins: For, if ye believe not that, I, am he, ye shall die in your sins.
text.KJV: I said therefore unto you, that ye shall die in your sins: for if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins.
text.NABRE: That is why I told you that you will die in your sins. For if you do not believe that I AM, you will die in your sins.”
text.DRB: Therefore I said to you that you shall die in your sins. For if you believe not that I am he, you shall die in your sin.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “Why do I speak to you at all?
text.Rotherham: They were saying unto him, therefore—Who art, thou? Jesus said unto them—First and foremost, even what I speak unto you.
text.KJV: Then said they unto him, Who art thou? And Jesus saith unto them, Even the same that I said unto you from the beginning.
text.NABRE: So they said to him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “What I told you from the beginning.
text.DRB: They said therefore to him: Who art thou? Jesus said to them: The Beginning who also speak to you.

text.NRSV: I have much to say about you and much to condemn; but the one who sent me is true, and I declare to the world what I have heard from him.”
text.Rotherham: Many things, have I, concerning you, to speak, and to judge; but, he who sent me, is, true, and, I, what things I heard from him, the same, speak I unto the world.
text.KJV: I have many things to say and to judge of you: but he that sent me is true; and I speak to the world those things which I have heard of him.
text.NABRE: I have much to say about you in condemnation. But the one who sent me is true, and what I heard from him I tell the world.”
text.DRB: Many things I have to speak and judge of you. But he that sent me, is true: and what I have heard of him, these things I speak in the world.

text.NRSV: They did not understand that he was speaking to them about the Father.
text.Rotherham: They noted not that, as touching the Father, unto them, he was speaking.
text.KJV: They understood not that he spake to them of the Father.
text.NABRE: They did not realize that he was speaking to them of the Father.
text.DRB: And they knew not that he said to them that his Father was God.

text.NRSV: So Jesus said, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will realize that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own, but I speak these things as the Father instructed me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said—Whensoever ye shall lift up the Son of Man, then, shall ye know, that, I, am he, and, of myself, am doing, nothing; but, just as the Father taught me, the same things, am I speaking.
text.KJV: Then said Jesus unto them, When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am he, and that I do nothing of myself; but as my Father hath taught me, I speak these things.
text.NABRE: So Jesus said [to them], “When you lift up the Son of Man, then you will realize that I AM, and that I do nothing on my own, but I say only what the Father taught me.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to them: When you shall have exalted the Son of man, then you shall know that I am he, and of myself I do nothing, but as the Father hath taught me, these things I speak:

text.NRSV: And the one who sent me is with me; he has not left me alone, for I always do what is pleasing to him.”
text.Rotherham: And, he that sent me, is, with me: He hath not left me, alone. Because, I, the things that please him, ever do.
text.KJV: And he that sent me is with me: the Father hath not left me alone; for I do always those things that please him.
text.NABRE: The one who sent me is with me. He has not left me alone, because I always do what is pleasing to him.”
text.DRB: and he that sent me, is with me: and he hath not left me alone, because the things that please him I do always.

text.NRSV: As he was saying these things, many believed in him.
text.Rotherham: As he was speaking these things, many, believed on him.
text.KJV: As he spake these words, many believed on him.
text.NABRE: Because he spoke this way, many came to believe in him. Jesus and Abraham.
text.DRB: When he spake these things, many believed in him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to the Jews who had believed in him, “If you continue in my word, you are truly my disciples;
text.Rotherham: Jesus was saying, therefore, unto the Jews who had believed on him—If, ye, abide in my word, of a truth, my disciples, ye are;
text.KJV: Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on him, If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed;
text.NABRE: Jesus then said to those Jews who believed in him, “If you remain in my word, you will truly be my disciples,
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to them that believed him, the Jews: If you abide in my word, you shall be my Disciples indeed.

text.NRSV: and you will know the truth, and the truth will make you free.”
text.Rotherham: And ye shall know the truth, and, the truth, shall make you free.
text.KJV: And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.
text.NABRE: and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
text.DRB: And you shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.

text.NRSV: They answered him, “We are descendants of Abraham and have never been slaves to anyone. What do you mean by saying, ‘You will be made free’?”
text.Rotherham: They made answer unto him—Seed of Abraham, are we, and, unto no one, have been brought into bondage, at any time: How sayest, thou, Free, shall ye be made?
text.KJV: They answered him, We be Abraham’s seed, and were never in bondage to any man: how sayest thou, Ye shall be made free?
text.NABRE: They answered him, “We are descendants of Abraham and have never been enslaved to anyone. How can you say, ‘You will become free’?”
text.DRB: They answered him: We are the seed of Abraham, and we never served any man: how sayest thou, You shall be free?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Very truly, I tell you, everyone who commits sin is a slave to sin.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them, Verily, verily, I say unto you: Every one who committeth sin, is, a slave, [of sin:]
text.KJV: Jesus answered them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “Amen, amen, I say to you, everyone who commits sin is a slave of sin.
text.DRB: Jesus answered them: Amen, amen I say to you, that every one which committeth sin, is the servant of sin.

text.NRSV: The slave does not have a permanent place in the household; the son has a place there forever.
text.Rotherham: Now, the slave, doth not abide in the house, evermore. The Son, abideth, evermore.
text.KJV: And the servant abideth not in the house for ever: but the Son abideth ever.
text.NABRE: A slave does not remain in a household forever, but a son always remains.
text.DRB: And the servant abideth not in the house for ever: the son abideth for ever.

text.NRSV: So if the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.
text.Rotherham: If then, the Son, shall make you free, really free, shall ye be.
text.KJV: If the Son therefore shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.
text.NABRE: So if a son frees you, then you will truly be free.
text.DRB: If therefore the Son make you free, you shall be free indeed.

text.NRSV: I know that you are descendants of Abraham; yet you look for an opportunity to kill me, because there is no place in you for my word.
text.Rotherham: I know that ye are, seed of Abraham; but ye are seeking to kill me, because, my word, findeth no place in you.
text.KJV: I know that ye are Abraham’s seed; but ye seek to kill me, because my word hath no place in you.
text.NABRE: I know that you are descendants of Abraham. But you are trying to kill me, because my word has no room among you.
text.DRB: I know that you are the children of Abraham: but you seek to kill me, because my word taketh not in you.

text.NRSV: I declare what I have seen in the Father’s presence; as for you, you should do what you have heard from the Father.”
text.Rotherham: What things, I, have seen with the Father, I am speaking; Ye, also, then, what things ye have heard from your father, are doing.—
text.KJV: I speak that which I have seen with my Father: and ye do that which ye have seen with your father.
text.NABRE: I tell you what I have seen in the Father’s presence; then do what you have heard from the Father.”
text.DRB: Ispeak that which I have seen with my Father: and you do the things that you have seen with your father.

text.NRSV: They answered him, “Abraham is our father.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would be doing what Abraham did,
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—Our father, is, Abraham. Jesus saith unto them—If, children of Abraham, ye are, the works of Abraham, are ye doing.
text.KJV: They answered and said unto him, Abraham is our father. Jesus saith unto them, If ye were Abraham’s children, ye would do the works of Abraham.
text.NABRE: They answered and said to him, “Our father is Abraham.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would be doing the works of Abraham.
text.DRB: They answered, and said to him: Our father is Abraham. Jesus saith to them: If you be the children of Abraham, do the works of Abraham.

text.NRSV: but now you are trying to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. This is not what Abraham did.
text.Rotherham: But, now, ye are seeking, to kill me,—A man who, the truth, unto you, hath spoken, which I have heard from God: this, Abraham, did not.
text.KJV: But now ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I have heard of God: this did not Abraham.
text.NABRE: But now you are trying to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God; Abraham did not do this.
text.DRB: But now, you seek to kill me, a man that have spoken the truth to you, which I have heard of God. This did not Abraham.

text.NRSV: You are indeed doing what your father does.” They said to him, “We are not illegitimate children; we have one father, God himself.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, are doing the works of your father. They said unto him—We, of fornication, were not born: One father, have we—God.
text.KJV: Ye do the deeds of your father. Then said they to him, We be not born of fornication; we have one Father, even God.
text.NABRE: You are doing the works of your father!” [So] they said to him, “We are not illegitimate. We have one Father, God.”
text.DRB: You do the works of your father. They said therefore to him: We were not born of fornication. We have one Father, God.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and now I am here. I did not come on my own, but he sent me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—If, God, had been your father, ye had been loving me, for, I, from God, came forth, and am here; for, not even of myself, have I come, but, he, sent me forth.
text.KJV: Jesus said unto them, If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I proceeded forth and came from God; neither came I of myself, but he sent me.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and am here; I did not come on my own, but he sent me.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to them: If God were your Father, verily you would love me. For from God I proceeded, and came: for I came not of myself, but he sent me:

text.NRSV: Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot accept my word.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, is it, that, my speech, ye do not understand? because ye cannot hear my word.
text.KJV: Why do ye not understand my speech? even because ye cannot hear my word.
text.NABRE: Why do you not understand what I am saying? Because you cannot bear to hear my word.
text.DRB: Why do you not know my speech? Because you cannot hear my word.

text.NRSV: You are from your father the devil, and you choose to do your father’s desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks according to his own nature, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are, of your father—the adversary, and, the covetings of your father, ye choose to be doing. He, was, a murderer, from the beginning, and, in the truth, he stood not; because truth is not in him: Whensoever he speaketh falsehood, of his own, he speaketh; because, false, he is, and, the father of it.
text.KJV: Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do. He was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father of it.
text.NABRE: You belong to your father the devil and you willingly carry out your father’s desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in truth, because there is no truth in him. When he tells a lie, he speaks in character, because he is a liar and the father of lies.
text.DRB: You are of your father the devil, & the desires of your father you will do. He was a man-killer from the beginning, & he stood not in the verity; because verity is not in him. When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own, because he is a liar, & the father thereof.

text.NRSV: But because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.
text.Rotherham: But, as for me, because, the truth, I speak, ye do not believe me.
text.KJV: And because I tell you the truth, ye believe me not.
text.NABRE: But because I speak the truth, you do not believe me.
text.DRB: But because I say the verity, you believe me not.

text.NRSV: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?
text.Rotherham: Which of you convicteth me of sin? If, truth, I speak, wherefore, do, ye, not believe me?
text.KJV: Which of you convinceth me of sin? And if I say the truth, why do ye not believe me?
text.NABRE: Can any of you charge me with sin? If I am telling the truth, why do you not believe me?
text.DRB: Which of you shall argue me of sin? If I say the verity, why do you not believe me?

text.NRSV: Whoever is from God hears the words of God. The reason you do not hear them is that you are not from God.”
text.Rotherham: He that is of God, heareth, the sayings of God; therefore, do, ye, not hear, because, of God, ye are not.
text.KJV: He that is of God heareth God’s words: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God.
text.NABRE: Whoever belongs to God hears the words of God; for this reason you do not listen, because you do not belong to God.”
text.DRB: He that is of God, heareth the words of God. Therefore you hear not, because you are not of God.

text.NRSV: The Jews answered him, “Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and have a demon?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews answered, and said unto him—Do, we, not, well, say: Thou, art, a Samaritan, and hast a demon?
text.KJV: Then answered the Jews, and said unto him, Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil?
text.NABRE: The Jews answered and said to him, “Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and are possessed?”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore answered, and said to him: Do not we say well that thou art a Samaritane, and hast a devil?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “I do not have a demon; but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—I, have not, a demon, but honour my Father: and, ye, dishonour me.
text.KJV: Jesus answered, I have not a devil; but I honour my Father, and ye do dishonour me.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “I am not possessed; I honor my Father, but you dishonor me.
text.DRB: Jesus answered: I have no devil: but I do honour my Father, and you have dishonoured me.

text.NRSV: Yet I do not seek my own glory; there is one who seeks it and he is the judge.
text.Rotherham: But, I, seek not my glory: There is one who seeketh and judgeth.
text.KJV: And I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.
text.NABRE: I do not seek my own glory; there is one who seeks it and he is the one who judges.
text.DRB: but I seek not mine own glory. There is that seeketh and judgeth.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, whoever keeps my word will never see death.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you: If anyone shall keep, my word, death, shall he not see, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, whoever keeps my word will never see death.”
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to you: If any man keep my word, he shall not see death for ever.

text.NRSV: The Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, and so did the prophets; yet you say, ‘Whoever keeps my word will never taste death.’
text.Rotherham: The Jews said unto him—Now, we know that, a demon, thou hast:—Abraham, died, and, the prophets, and yet, thou, sayest: If anyone shall keep, my word, in nowise shall he taste of death, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: Then said the Jews unto him, Now we know that thou hast a devil. Abraham is dead, and the prophets; and thou sayest, If a man keep my saying, he shall never taste of death.
text.NABRE: [So] the Jews said to him, “Now we are sure that you are possessed. Abraham died, as did the prophets, yet you say, ‘Whoever keeps my word will never taste death.’
text.DRB: The Jews therefore said: Now we have known that thou hast a devil. Abraham is dead, and the Prophets; and thou sayest: If any man keep my word, he shall not taste death for ever.

text.NRSV: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? The prophets also died. Who do you claim to be?”
text.Rotherham: Surely, thou, art not, greater, than our father Abraham,—who, indeed, died? and, the prophets, died:—Whom, makest thou thyself?
text.KJV: Art thou greater than our father Abraham, which is dead? and the prophets are dead: whom makest thou thyself?
text.NABRE: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? Or the prophets, who died? Who do you make yourself out to be?”
text.DRB: Why, art thou greater than our father Abraham, who is dead? and the Prophets are dead. Whom doest thou make thyself?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, he of whom you say, ‘He is our God,’
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—If, I, glorify myself, my glory, is nothing: It is, my Father, that glorifieth me,—of whom, ye, say—He is, your God!
text.KJV: Jesus answered, If I honour myself, my honour is nothing: it is my Father that honoureth me; of whom ye say, that he is your God:
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is worth nothing; but it is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say, ‘He is our God.’
text.DRB: Jesus answered: If I do glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father that glorifieth me, whom you say that he is your God.

text.NRSV: though you do not know him. But I know him; if I would say that I do not know him, I would be a liar like you. But I do know him and I keep his word.
text.Rotherham: And yet ye have not come to know him; but, I, do know him: If I say, I know him not, I shall be like you—false; but I know him, and, his word, am I keeping.
text.KJV: Yet ye have not known him; but I know him: and if I should say, I know him not, I shall be a liar like unto you: but I know him, and keep his saying.
text.NABRE: You do not know him, but I know him. And if I should say that I do not know him, I would be like you a liar. But I do know him and I keep his word.
text.DRB: And you have not known him, but I know him. And if I shall say that I know him not, I shall be like to you, a liar. But I do know him, and do keep his word.

text.NRSV: Your ancestor Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day; he saw it and was glad.”
text.Rotherham: Abraham, your father, exulted that he should see my day; and he saw, and rejoiced.
text.KJV: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day: and he saw it, and was glad.
text.NABRE: Abraham your father rejoiced to see my day; he saw it and was glad.
text.DRB: Abraham your father rejoiced that he might see my day: and he saw, and was glad.

text.NRSV: Then the Jews said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, said unto him—Fifty years old, not yet art thou, and, Abraham, hast thou seen?
text.KJV: Then said the Jews unto him, Thou art not yet fifty years old, and hast thou seen Abraham?
text.NABRE: So the Jews said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old and you have seen Abraham?”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore said to him: Thou hast not yet fifty years and hast thou seen Abraham?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, before Abraham was, I am.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Before, Abraham, came into existence, I, am.
text.KJV: Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Amen, amen, I say to you, before Abraham came to be, I AM.”
text.DRB: Jesus said to them: Amen, amen I say to you, before that Abraham was made, I am.

text.NRSV: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple.
text.Rotherham: They took up stones, therefore, that they might cast at him; but, Jesus, was hidden, and went forth out of the temple.
text.KJV: Then took they up stones to cast at him: but Jesus hid himself, and went out of the temple, going through the midst of them, and so passed by.
text.NABRE: So they picked up stones to throw at him; but Jesus hid and went out of the temple area.
text.DRB: They took stones therefore to cast at him. But Jesus hid himself, and went out of the temple.

text.NRSV: As he walked along, he saw a man blind from birth.
text.Rotherham: And, passing along, he saw a man, blind from birth.
text.KJV: And as Jesus passed by, he saw a man which was blind from his birth.
text.NABRE: As he passed by he saw a man blind from birth.
text.DRB: And Jesus passing by, saw a man blind: from his nativity;

text.NRSV: His disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
text.Rotherham: And his disciples questioned him, saying—Rabbi! who sinned, this man or his parents, that, blind, he should be born?
text.KJV: And his disciples asked him, saying, Master, who did sin, this man, or his parents, that he was born blind?
text.NABRE: His disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
text.DRB: and his Disciples asked him: Rabbi, who hath sinned, this man, or his parents, that he should be born blind? Jesus answered:

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Neither this man nor his parents sinned; he was born blind so that God’s works might be revealed in him.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—Neither, this man, sinned nor his parents; but…that the works of God should be made manifest in him.
text.KJV: Jesus answered, Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “Neither he nor his parents sinned; it is so that the works of God might be made visible through him.
text.DRB: Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents; but that the works of God may be manifested in him.

text.NRSV: We must work the works of him who sent me while it is day; night is coming when no one can work.
text.Rotherham: We must needs be working the works of him that sent me, while it is, day: There cometh a night, when, no one, can work.
text.KJV: I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.
text.NABRE: We have to do the works of the one who sent me while it is day. Night is coming when no one can work.
text.DRB: I must work the works of him that sent me, whiles it is day. The night cometh, when no man can work.

text.NRSV: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”
text.Rotherham: Whensoever I may be, in the world, I am, the light, of the world.
text.KJV: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
text.NABRE: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”
text.DRB: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, he spat on the ground and made mud with the saliva and spread the mud on the man’s eyes,
text.Rotherham: These things, having said, he spat on the ground, and made clay with the spittle, and laid the clay upon his eyes;
text.KJV: When he had thus spoken, he spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and he anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay,
text.NABRE: When he had said this, he spat on the ground and made clay with the saliva, and smeared the clay on his eyes,
text.DRB: When he had said these things, he spit on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and spread the clay upon his eyes,

text.NRSV: saying to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means Sent). Then he went and washed and came back able to see.
text.Rotherham: and said unto him—Withdraw! wash in the pool of Siloam,—which is to be translated, Sent. He went away, therefore, and washed, and came, seeing.
text.KJV: And said unto him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam, (which is by interpretation, Sent.) He went his way therefore, and washed, and came seeing.
