text.NABRE: and said to him, “Go wash in the Pool of Siloam” (which means Sent). So he went and washed, and came back able to see.
text.DRB: and said to him: Go, wash in the pool of Siloe, which is interpreted, Sent. He went therefore, and washed; and he came seeing.

text.NRSV: The neighbors and those who had seen him before as a beggar began to ask, “Is this not the man who used to sit and beg?”
text.Rotherham: The neighbours, therefore, and they who used to observe him aforetime—that he was, a beggar, were saying—Is not, this, he that used to sit and beg?
text.KJV: The neighbours therefore, and they which before had seen him that he was blind, said, Is not this he that sat and begged?
text.NABRE: His neighbors and those who had seen him earlier as a beggar said, “Isn’t this the one who used to sit and beg?”
text.DRB: Therefore the neighbours, and they which had seen him before, that he was a beggar, said: Is not this he that sat, and begged? Others said: That this is he.

text.NRSV: Some were saying, “It is he.” Others were saying, “No, but it is someone like him.” He kept saying, “I am the man.”
text.Rotherham: Others, were saying—’Tis, the same. Others, were saying—Nay! but he is, like him. He, was saying—I, am he.
text.KJV: Some said, This is he: others said, He is like him: but he said, I am he.
text.NABRE: Some said, “It is,” but others said, “No, he just looks like him.” He said, “I am.”
text.DRB: But others: No, not so, but he is like him. But he said: That I am he.

text.NRSV: But they kept asking him, “Then how were your eyes opened?”
text.Rotherham: So they were saying unto him—How [then] were thine eyes opened?
text.KJV: Therefore said they unto him, How were thine eyes opened?
text.NABRE: So they said to him, “[So] how were your eyes opened?”
text.DRB: They said therefore to him: How were thine eyes opened?

text.NRSV: He answered, “The man called Jesus made mud, spread it on my eyes, and said to me, ‘Go to Siloam and wash.’ Then I went and washed and received my sight.”
text.Rotherham: He, answered—The man that is called Jesus, made, clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me: Withdraw unto the pool of Siloam, and wash. Going away, therefore, and washing, I received sight.
text.KJV: He answered and said, A man that is called Jesus made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me, Go to the pool of Siloam, and wash: and I went and washed, and I received sight.
text.NABRE: He replied, “The man called Jesus made clay and anointed my eyes and told me, ‘Go to Siloam and wash.’ So I went there and washed and was able to see.”
text.DRB: He answered: That man that is called Jesus, made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said to me: Go to the pool of Siloe, and wash. And I went, and washed, and saw.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Where is he?” He said, “I do not know.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto him—Where is, He? He saith—I know not.
text.KJV: Then said they unto him, Where is he? He said, I know not.
text.NABRE: And they said to him, “Where is he?” He said, “I don’t know.”
text.DRB: And they said to him: Where is he? He saith: I know not.

text.NRSV: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had formerly been blind.
text.Rotherham: They bring him unto the Pharisees—him at one time blind.
text.KJV: They brought to the Pharisees him that aforetime was blind.
text.NABRE: They brought the one who was once blind to the Pharisees.
text.DRB: They bring him that had been blind, to the Pharisees.

text.NRSV: Now it was a sabbath day when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.
text.Rotherham: Now it was Sabbath, on the day when Jesus made, the clay, and opened his eyes.
text.KJV: And it was the sabbath day when Jesus made the clay, and opened his eyes.
text.NABRE: Now Jesus had made clay and opened his eyes on a sabbath.
text.DRB: And it was the Sabboth when Jesus made the clay, and opened his eyes.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees also began to ask him how he had received his sight. He said to them, “He put mud on my eyes. Then I washed, and now I see.”
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, the Pharisees also questioned him, as to how he received sight. And, he, said unto them—Clay, laid he upon mine eyes, and I washed,—and do see.
text.KJV: Then again the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. He said unto them, He put clay upon mine eyes, and I washed, and do see.
text.NABRE: So then the Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. He said to them, “He put clay on my eyes, and I washed, and now I can see.”
text.DRB: Again therefore the Pharisees asked him, how he saw. But he said to them: He put clay upon mine eyes, & I washed; and I see.

text.NRSV: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, for he does not observe the sabbath.” But others said, “How can a man who is a sinner perform such signs?” And they were divided.
text.Rotherham: Certain from among the Pharisees, therefore, were saying—This man is not, from God, because, the Sabbath, he keepeth not. Others, [however] were saying—How can a sinful man, such signs as these, be doing? And there was, a division, among them.
text.KJV: Therefore said some of the Pharisees, This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the sabbath day. Others said, How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles? And there was a division among them.
text.NABRE: So some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, because he does not keep the sabbath.” [But] others said, “How can a sinful man do such signs?” And there was a division among them.
text.DRB: Certain therefore of the Pharisees said: This man is not of God, that keepeth not the Sabboth. But others said: How can a man that is a sinner do these signs? And there was a schism among them.

text.NRSV: So they said again to the blind man, “What do you say about him? It was your eyes he opened.” He said, “He is a prophet.”
text.Rotherham: So they were saying unto the blind man, again, What dost, thou, say concerning him, in that he opened thine eyes? And, he, said—A prophet, is he.
text.KJV: They say unto the blind man again, What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes? He said, He is a prophet.
text.NABRE: So they said to the blind man again, “What do you have to say about him, since he opened your eyes?” He said, “He is a prophet.”
text.DRB: They say therefore to the blind again: Thou, what sayest thou of him that opened thine eyes? And he said: That he is a Prophet.

text.NRSV: The Jews did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight until they called the parents of the man who had received his sight
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, did not believe, concerning him, that he was blind, and received sight,—until they called the parents of him that had received sight,
text.KJV: But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and received his sight, until they called the parents of him that had received his sight.
text.NABRE: Now the Jews did not believe that he had been blind and gained his sight until they summoned the parents of the one who had gained his sight.
text.DRB: The Jews therefore did not believe of him, that he had been blind and saw, until they called the parents of him that saw,

text.NRSV: and asked them, “Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”
text.Rotherham: and questioned them, saying—Is, this, your son, of whom, ye, say, that blind, he was, born? How, then, seeth he, even now?
text.KJV: And they asked them, saying, Is this your son, who ye say was born blind? how then doth he now see?
text.NABRE: They asked them, “Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How does he now see?”
text.DRB: and asked them, saying: Is this your son, whom you say that he was born blind? how then doth he now see?

text.NRSV: His parents answered, “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
text.Rotherham: His parents, therefore, answered, and said—We know that, this, is our son, and that, blind, he was born;
text.KJV: His parents answered them and said, We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind:
text.NABRE: His parents answered and said, “We know that this is our son and that he was born blind.
text.DRB: His parents answered them, and said: We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;

text.NRSV: but we do not know how it is that now he sees, nor do we know who opened his eyes. Ask him; he is of age. He will speak for himself.”
text.Rotherham: But, how he now seeth, we know not, or, who opened his eyes, we, know not,—Question, him, he is, of age, he, concerning himself, shall speak.
text.KJV: But by what means he now seeth, we know not; or who hath opened his eyes, we know not: he is of age; ask him: he shall speak for himself.
text.NABRE: We do not know how he sees now, nor do we know who opened his eyes. Ask him, he is of age; he can speak for himself.”
text.DRB: but how he now seeth, we know not, or who hath opened his eyes, we know not, ask himself; he is of age, let himself speak of himself.

text.NRSV: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that anyone who confessed Jesus to be the Messiah would be put out of the synagogue.
text.Rotherham: These things, said his parents, because they were in fear of the Jews,—for, already, had the Jews agreed together, that, if anyone should confess, him, to be Christ, an, excommunicant from the synagogue, should he be made.
text.KJV: These words spake his parents, because they feared the Jews: for the Jews had agreed already, that if any man did confess that he was Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue.
text.NABRE: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews, for the Jews had already agreed that if anyone acknowledged him as the Messiah, he would be expelled from the synagogue.
text.DRB: These things his parents said, because they feared the Jews. For the Jews had now conspired, that if any man should confess him to be Christ, he should be put out of the Synagogue.

text.NRSV: Therefore his parents said, “He is of age; ask him.”
text.Rotherham: For this cause, his parents said—He is, of age,—question him.
text.KJV: Therefore said his parents, He is of age; ask him.
text.NABRE: For this reason his parents said, “He is of age; question him.”
text.DRB: Therefore did his parents say: That he is of age, ask himself.

text.NRSV: So for the second time they called the man who had been blind, and they said to him, “Give glory to God! We know that this man is a sinner.”
text.Rotherham: So they called the man a second time—[him] who had been blind, and said unto him—Give glory unto God! We know that, this man, is, a sinner.
text.KJV: Then again called they the man that was blind, and said unto him, Give God the praise: we know that this man is a sinner.
text.NABRE: So a second time they called the man who had been blind and said to him, “Give God the praise! We know that this man is a sinner.”
text.DRB: They therefore again called the man that had been blind, and said to him: Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.

text.NRSV: He answered, “I do not know whether he is a sinner. One thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see.”
text.Rotherham: He, therefore, answered—Whether he is a sinner, I know not: One thing, I know,—That, whereas I was, blind, now, I see!
text.KJV: He answered and said, Whether he be a sinner or no, I know not: one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.
text.NABRE: He replied, “If he is a sinner, I do not know. One thing I do know is that I was blind and now I see.”
text.DRB: He therefore said to them: Whether he be a sinner, I know not: one thing I know, that whereas I was blind, now I see.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—What did he unto thee? How opened he thine eyes?
text.KJV: Then said they to him again, What did he to thee? how opened he thine eyes?
text.NABRE: So they said to him, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
text.DRB: They said therefore to him: What did he to thee? how did he open thine eyes?

text.NRSV: He answered them, “I have told you already, and you would not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text.Rotherham: He answered them—I told you just now, and ye did not hear: Why, again, do ye wish to hear? Are, ye also, wishing to become, his disciples?
text.KJV: He answered them, I have told you already, and ye did not hear: wherefore would ye hear it again? will ye also be his disciples?
text.NABRE: He answered them, “I told you already and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples, too?”
text.DRB: He answered them: I have now told you, and you have heard; why will you hear it again? will you also become his Disciples?

text.NRSV: Then they reviled him, saying, “You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
text.Rotherham: And they reviled him, and said—Thou, art, the disciple, of that man; but, we, are, Moses’, disciples:—
text.KJV: Then they reviled him, and said, Thou art his disciple; but we are Moses’ disciples.
text.NABRE: They ridiculed him and said, “You are that man’s disciple; we are disciples of Moses!
text.DRB: They reviled him therefore, & said: Be thou his Disciple: but we are the Disciples of Moyses.

text.NRSV: We know that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this man, we do not know where he comes from.”
text.Rotherham: We, know, that, unto Moses, hath God spoken; but, as for this man, we know not whence he is.
text.KJV: We know that God spake unto Moses: as for this fellow, we know not from whence he is.
text.NABRE: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we do not know where this one is from.”
text.DRB: We know that to Moyses God did speak; but this man we know not whence he is.

text.NRSV: The man answered, “Here is an astonishing thing! You do not know where he comes from, and yet he opened my eyes.
text.Rotherham: The man answered, and said unto them—Why! Herein, is, the marvel: That, ye, know not whence he is, and yet he opened mine eyes.
text.KJV: The man answered and said unto them, Why herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes.
text.NABRE: The man answered and said to them, “This is what is so amazing, that you do not know where he is from, yet he opened my eyes.
text.DRB: The man answered and said to them: For in this it is marvelous that you know not whence he is, and he hath opened mine eyes.

text.NRSV: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but he does listen to one who worships him and obeys his will.
text.Rotherham: We know that, God, unto sinners, doth not hearken: but, if one be, a worshipper of God, and be doing, his will, unto this one, he hearkeneth.
text.KJV: Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth.
text.NABRE: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but if one is devout and does his will, he listens to him.
text.DRB: And we know that sinners God doth not hear. But if a man be a server of God, and do the will of him, him he heareth.

text.NRSV: Never since the world began has it been heard that anyone opened the eyes of a person born blind.
text.Rotherham: Out of age-past time, hath it never been heard, that anyone opened the eyes of one who, blind, had been born.
text.KJV: Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.
text.NABRE: It is unheard of that anyone ever opened the eyes of a person born blind.
text.DRB: From the beginning of the world it hath not been heard that any man hath opened the eyes of one born blind.

text.NRSV: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.”
text.Rotherham: If this man were not from God, he could have done nothing.
text.KJV: If this man were not of God, he could do nothing.
text.NABRE: If this man were not from God, he would not be able to do anything.”
text.DRB: unless this man were of God, he could not do any thing.

text.NRSV: They answered him, “You were born entirely in sins, and are you trying to teach us?” And they drove him out.
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—In sins, wast, thou, born, altogether; and art, thou, teaching, us? And they cast him out.
text.KJV: They answered and said unto him, Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us? And they cast him out.
text.NABRE: They answered and said to him, “You were born totally in sin, and are you trying to teach us?” Then they threw him out.
text.DRB: They answered and said to him: Thou wast wholly born in sins, and doest thou teach us? And they did cast him forth.

text.NRSV: Jesus heard that they had driven him out, and when he found him, he said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus heard that they had cast him out: and, finding him, said—Dost, thou, believe on the son of Man?
text.KJV: Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?
text.NABRE: When Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, he found him and said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
text.DRB: Jesus heard that they cast him forth; & when he had found him, he said to him: Doest thou believe in the Son of God?

text.NRSV: He answered, “And who is he, sir? Tell me, so that I may believe in him.”
text.Rotherham: He answered [and said] —And, who, is he, Sir, that I may believe on him?
text.KJV: He answered and said, Who is he, Lord, that I might believe on him?
text.NABRE: He answered and said, “Who is he, sir, that I may believe in him?”
text.DRB: He answered, and said: Who is he Lord, that I may believe in him?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “You have seen him, and the one speaking with you is he.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto him—Thou hast both seen him and, he that is speaking with thee, is, he.
text.KJV: And Jesus said unto him, Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “You have seen him and the one speaking with you is he.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: Both thou hast seen him; and he that talketh with thee, he it is.

text.NRSV: He said, “Lord, I believe.” And he worshiped him.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—I believe, Sir! and worshipped him.
text.KJV: And he said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him.
text.NABRE: He said, “I do believe, Lord,” and he worshiped him.
text.DRB: But he said, I believe Lord. And falling down he adored him.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “I came into this world for judgment so that those who do not see may see, and those who do see may become blind.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said—For judgment, I, unto this world, came: that, they who were not seeing, might see, and, they who were seeing, might become, blind.
text.KJV: And Jesus said, For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus said, “I came into this world for judgment, so that those who do not see might see, and those who do see might become blind.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: For judgement came I into this world; that they that see not, may see: and they that see, may become blind.

text.NRSV: Some of the Pharisees near him heard this and said to him, “Surely we are not blind, are we?”
text.Rotherham: They of the Pharisees who were with him, heard, these things, and said unto him—Are, we also, blind?
text.KJV: And some of the Pharisees which were with him heard these words, and said unto him, Are we blind also?
text.NABRE: Some of the Pharisees who were with him heard this and said to him, “Surely we are not also blind, are we?”
text.DRB: And certain of the Pharisees that were with him, heard; and they said to him: Why, are we also blind?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would not have sin. But now that you say, ‘We see,’ your sin remains.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—If, blind, ye had been, ye had not had sin; but, now, ye say, We see, your sin, abideth.
text.KJV: Jesus said unto them, If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you are saying, ‘We see,’ so your sin remains.
text.DRB: Jesus said to them: If you were blind, you should not have sin, but now you say, That we see. Your sin remaineth.

text.NRSV: “Very truly, I tell you, anyone who does not enter the sheepfold by the gate but climbs in by another way is a thief and a bandit.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you:—He that entereth not, through the door, into the fold of the sheep, but goeth up from another place, that man, is, a thief, and, a robber.
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.
text.NABRE: “Amen, amen, I say to you, whoever does not enter a sheepfold through the gate but climbs over elsewhere is a thief and a robber.
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to you, he that entereth not by the door into the fold of the sheep, but climbeth up another way; he is a thief and a robber.

text.NRSV: The one who enters by the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
text.Rotherham: But, he that entereth through the door, is, shepherd, of the sheep:
text.KJV: But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
text.NABRE: But whoever enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
text.DRB: But he that entereth by the door, is the Pastor of the sheep.

text.NRSV: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep hear his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
text.Rotherham: To him, the porter openeth, and, the sheep, unto his voice, hearken: and, his own sheep, he calleth by name, and leadeth them forth.
text.KJV: To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them out.
text.NABRE: The gatekeeper opens it for him, and the sheep hear his voice, as he calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
text.DRB: To this man the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them forth.

text.NRSV: When he has brought out all his own, he goes ahead of them, and the sheep follow him because they know his voice.
text.Rotherham: As soon as, all his own, he hath put forth, before them, he moveth on, and, the sheep, follow him, because they know his voice;
text.KJV: And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice.
text.NABRE: When he has driven out all his own, he walks ahead of them, and the sheep follow him, because they recognize his voice.
text.DRB: And when he hath let forth his own sheep, he goeth before them: and the sheep follow him, because they know his voice.

text.NRSV: They will not follow a stranger, but they will run from him because they do not know the voice of strangers.”
text.Rotherham: But, a stranger, will they in nowise follow, but will flee from him, because they know not the voice, of strangers.
text.KJV: And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.
text.NABRE: But they will not follow a stranger; they will run away from him, because they do not recognize the voice of strangers.”
text.DRB: But a stranger they follow not, but fly from him because they know not the voice of strangers.

text.NRSV: Jesus used this figure of speech with them, but they did not understand what he was saying to them.
text.Rotherham: This similitude, spake Jesus unto them; but, those men, understood not what the things were which he was speaking unto them.
text.KJV: This parable spake Jesus unto them: but they understood not what things they were which he spake unto them.
text.NABRE: Although Jesus used this figure of speech, they did not realize what he was trying to tell them.
text.DRB: This proverb Jesus said to them. But they knew not what he spake to them.

text.NRSV: So again Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, I am the gate for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said, again—Verily, verily, I say unto you:—I, am the door of the sheep:
text.KJV: Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.
text.NABRE: So Jesus said again, “Amen, amen, I say to you, I am the gate for the sheep.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to them again: Amen, amen I say to you, that I am the door of the sheep.

text.NRSV: All who came before me are thieves and bandits; but the sheep did not listen to them.
text.Rotherham: All, as many as came before me, are thieves, and, robbers: but the sheep hearkened nor unto them.
text.KJV: All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.
text.NABRE: All who came [before me] are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.
text.DRB: And how many soever have come, are thieves and robbers: but the sheep heard them not.

text.NRSV: I am the gate. Whoever enters by me will be saved, and will come in and go out and find pasture.
text.Rotherham: I, am the door: through me, if anyone enter, he shall be saved, and shall come in and go out, and, pasture, shall find.
text.KJV: I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.
text.NABRE: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved, and will come in and go out and find pasture.
text.DRB: I am the door. By me if any enter, he shall be saved: & he shall go in and shall go out, & shall find pastures.

text.NRSV: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I came that they may have life, and have it abundantly.
text.Rotherham: The thief, cometh not, save that he may thieve and slay and destroy: I, came, that, life, they might have, and, above measure, might have.
text.KJV: The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.
text.NABRE: A thief comes only to steal and slaughter and destroy; I came so that they might have life and have it more abundantly.
text.DRB: The thief cometh not but to steal and kill & destroy. I came that they may have life, & may have more aboundantly.

text.NRSV: “I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: I, am the good shepherd: The good shepherd, his life, layeth down, for the sheep.
text.KJV: I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.
text.NABRE: I am the good shepherd. A good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
text.DRB: I am the good Pastor.

text.NRSV: The hired hand, who is not the shepherd and does not own the sheep, sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and runs away—and the wolf snatches them and scatters them.
text.Rotherham: The hireling, even because he is no shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, vieweth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth,—and, the wolf, seizeth them and scattereth,—
text.KJV: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep.
text.NABRE: A hired man, who is not a shepherd and whose sheep are not his own, sees a wolf coming and leaves the sheep and runs away, and the wolf catches and scatters them.
text.DRB: The good Pastor giveth his life for his sheep. But the hireling & he that is not the Pastor, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and flyeth: and the wolf raveneth, and disperseth the sheep.

text.NRSV: The hired hand runs away because a hired hand does not care for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: Because, a hireling, he is, and hath no care for the sheep.
text.KJV: The hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.
text.NABRE: This is because he works for pay and has no concern for the sheep.
text.DRB: And the hireling flyeth because he is a hireling; and he hath no care of the sheep.

text.NRSV: I am the good shepherd. I know my own and my own know me,
text.Rotherham: I, am the good shepherd, and know my own, and, my own, know me,—
text.KJV: I am the good shepherd, and know my sheep, and am known of mine.
text.NABRE: I am the good shepherd, and I know mine and mine know me,
text.DRB: I am the good Pastor; and I know mine, and mine know me.

text.NRSV: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. And I lay down my life for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: Just as, the Father, knoweth me, and, I, know, the Father; and, my life, I lay down for the sheep.
text.KJV: As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father: and I lay down my life for the sheep.
text.NABRE: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father; and I will lay down my life for the sheep.
text.DRB: As the Father knoweth me, and I know the Father: and I yield my life for my sheep.

text.NRSV: I have other sheep that do not belong to this fold. I must bring them also, and they will listen to my voice. So there will be one flock, one shepherd.
text.Rotherham: And, other sheep, have I, which are not of this fold: those also, I must needs bring, and, unto my voice, will they hearken, and there shall come to be, One flock, One shepherd.
text.KJV: And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one shepherd.
text.NABRE: I have other sheep that do not belong to this fold. These also I must lead, and they will hear my voice, and there will be one flock, one shepherd.
text.DRB: And other sheep I have that are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice, and there shall be made one fold and one Pastor.

text.NRSV: For this reason the Father loves me, because I lay down my life in order to take it up again.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, doth the Father, love, me, because, I, lay down my life, that, again, I may receive it:—
text.KJV: Therefore doth my Father love me, because I lay down my life, that I might take it again.
text.NABRE: This is why the Father loves me, because I lay down my life in order to take it up again.
text.DRB: Therefore the Father loveth me: because I yield my life, that I may take it again.

text.NRSV: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of my own accord. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it up again. I have received this command from my Father.”
text.Rotherham: No one, forced it from me, but, I, lay it down, of myself,—Authority, have I, to lay it down, and, authority, have I, again, to receive it: This commandment, received I, from my Father.
text.KJV: No man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. This commandment have I received of my Father.
text.NABRE: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down on my own. I have power to lay it down, and power to take it up again. This command I have received from my Father.”
text.DRB: No man taketh it away from me: but I yield it of myself. And I have power to yield it: and I have power to take it again. This commandment I received of my Father.

text.NRSV: Again the Jews were divided because of these words.
text.Rotherham: A division, again, took place among the Jews, because of these words.
text.KJV: There was a division therefore again among the Jews for these sayings.
text.NABRE: Again there was a division among the Jews because of these words.
text.DRB: Adissension rose again among the Jews for these words.

text.NRSV: Many of them were saying, “He has a demon and is out of his mind. Why listen to him?”
text.Rotherham: But many from among them were saying—A demon, he hath, and is raving,—Why, unto him, do ye hearken?
text.KJV: And many of them said, He hath a devil, and is mad; why hear ye him?
text.NABRE: Many of them said, “He is possessed and out of his mind; why listen to him?”
text.DRB: And many of them said: He hath a devil and is mad; why hear you him?

text.NRSV: Others were saying, “These are not the words of one who has a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
text.Rotherham: Others, said—These sayings, are not those of one demonized,—Can, a demon, open the eyes of, the blind?
text.KJV: Others said, These are not the words of him that hath a devil. Can a devil open the eyes of the blind?
text.NABRE: Others said, “These are not the words of one possessed; surely a demon cannot open the eyes of the blind, can he?”
text.DRB: Others said: These are not the words of one that hath a devil. Can a devil open the eyes of blind men?

text.NRSV: At that time the festival of the Dedication took place in Jerusalem. It was winter,
text.Rotherham: The feast of dedication took place at that time, in Jerusalem: it was, winter,
text.KJV: And it was at Jerusalem the feast of the dedication, and it was winter.
text.NABRE: Feast of the Dedication. The feast of the Dedication was then taking place in Jerusalem. It was winter.
text.DRB: And the Dedication was in Jerusalem: and it was winter.

text.NRSV: and Jesus was walking in the temple, in the portico of Solomon.
text.Rotherham: and Jesus was walking in the temple, in the porch of Solomon.
text.KJV: And Jesus walked in the temple in Solomon’s porch.
text.NABRE: And Jesus walked about in the temple area on the Portico of Solomon.
text.DRB: And Jesus walked in the temple, in Salomon's porch.

text.NRSV: So the Jews gathered around him and said to him, “How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly.”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, surrounded him, and were saying unto him—How long, holdest thou, our lives, in suspense? If, thou, art the Christ, tell us, plainly.
text.KJV: Then came the Jews round about him, and said unto him, How long dost thou make us to doubt? If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.
text.NABRE: So the Jews gathered around him and said to him, “How long are you going to keep us in suspense? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly.”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore compassed him round about, and said to him: How long doest thou hold our soul in suspense? if thou be Christ, tell us openly.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “I have told you, and you do not believe. The works that I do in my Father’s name testify to me;
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—I told you, and ye believe not: The works which, I, am doing in the name of my Father, the same, bear witness concerning me.
text.KJV: Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father’s name, they bear witness of me.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “I told you and you do not believe. The works I do in my Father’s name testify to me.
text.DRB: Jesus answered them: I speak to you; and you believe not; the works that I do in the name of my Father, they give testimony of me.

text.NRSV: but you do not believe, because you do not belong to my sheep.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, believe not, because ye are not of my sheep.
text.KJV: But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.
text.NABRE: But you do not believe, because you are not among my sheep.
text.DRB: But you do not believe, because you are not of my sheep.

text.NRSV: My sheep hear my voice. I know them, and they follow me.
text.Rotherham: My sheep, unto my voice, hearken,—and, I, know, them, and they follow me,—
text.KJV: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:
text.NABRE: My sheep hear my voice; I know them, and they follow me.
text.DRB: My sheep hear my voice; and I know them, and they follow me.

text.NRSV: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish. No one will snatch them out of my hand.
text.Rotherham: And, I, give unto them life age-abiding, and in nowise shall they perish, unto times age-abiding; and no one shall carry them off out of my hand.
text.KJV: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.
text.NABRE: I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish. No one can take them out of my hand.
text.DRB: And I give them life everlasting; and they shall not perish for ever, and no man shall pluck them out of my hand.

text.NRSV: What my Father has given me is greater than all else, and no one can snatch it out of the Father’s hand.
text.Rotherham: What, my Father, hath given me, is, something greater than all, and, no one, can carry off out of the hand of my Father:—
text.KJV: My Father, which gave them me, is greater than all; and no man is able to pluck them out of my Father’s hand.
text.NABRE: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one can take them out of the Father’s hand.
text.DRB: My Father, that which he hath given me, is greater than all: and no man can pluck them out of the hand of my Father.

text.NRSV: The Father and I are one.”
text.Rotherham: I and the Father, are, one.
text.KJV: I and my Father are one.
text.NABRE: The Father and I are one.”
text.DRB: I and the Father are one.

text.NRSV: The Jews took up stones again to stone him.
text.Rotherham: The Jews again lifted up stones, that they might stone him.
text.KJV: Then the Jews took up stones again to stone him.
text.NABRE: The Jews again picked up rocks to stone him.
text.DRB: The Jews took up stones, to stone him.

text.NRSV: Jesus replied, “I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of these are you going to stone me?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—Many works, have I showed you, noble ones, from my Father: For which of those works are ye stoning me?
text.KJV: Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “I have shown you many good works from my Father. For which of these are you trying to stone me?”
text.DRB: Jesus answered them: Many good works I have shewed you from my Father, for which of those works do you stone me?

text.NRSV: The Jews answered, “It is not for a good work that we are going to stone you, but for blasphemy, because you, though only a human being, are making yourself God.”
text.Rotherham: The Jews answered him—Concerning a noble work, are we not stoning thee; but concerning profane speech,—and because, thou, being, a man, art making thyself, God.
text.KJV: The Jews answered him, saying, For a good work we stone thee not; but for blasphemy; and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself God.
text.NABRE: The Jews answered him, “We are not stoning you for a good work but for blasphemy. You, a man, are making yourself God.”
text.DRB: The Jews answered him: For a good work we stone thee not, but for blasphemy, and because thou being a man, makest thyself God.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Is it not written in your law, ‘I said, you are gods’?
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—Is it not written in your law: I, said, Ye are, gods?
text.KJV: Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods?
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “Is it not written in your law, ‘I said, “You are gods”’?
text.DRB: Jesus answered them: Is it not written in your law, that I said, you are gods?

text.NRSV: If those to whom the word of God came were called ‘gods’—and the scripture cannot be annulled—
text.Rotherham: If, those, he called gods, unto whom, the word of God, came—and the Scripture cannot be broken—
text.KJV: If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;
text.NABRE: If it calls them gods to whom the word of God came, and scripture cannot be set aside,
text.DRB: If he called them gods, to whom the word of God was made, and the scripture cannot be broken;

text.NRSV: can you say that the one whom the Father has sanctified and sent into the world is blaspheming because I said, ‘I am God’s Son’?
text.Rotherham: Of him whom, the Father, hallowed and sent forth into the world, are, ye, saying—Thou speakest profanely, because I said, Son of God, I am?
text.KJV: Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?
text.NABRE: can you say that the one whom the Father has consecrated and sent into the world blasphemes because I said, ‘I am the Son of God’?
text.DRB: whom the Father hath sanctified and sent into the world, say you, That thou blasphemest, because I said I am the Son of God?

text.NRSV: If I am not doing the works of my Father, then do not believe me.
text.Rotherham: If I am not doing the works of my Father, do not believe in me;
text.KJV: If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.
text.NABRE: If I do not perform my Father’s works, do not believe me;
text.DRB: If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.

text.NRSV: But if I do them, even though you do not believe me, believe the works, so that you may know and understand that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.”
text.Rotherham: But, if I am doing them, even though, in me, ye believe not, in the works, believe,—that ye may get to know and go on to know, that the Father is, in me, and, I, am, in the Father.
text.KJV: But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the Father is in me, and I in him.
text.NABRE: but if I perform them, even if you do not believe me, believe the works, so that you may realize [and understand] that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.”
text.DRB: But if I do, and if you will not believe me, believe the works: that you may know and believe that the Father is in me, and I in the Father.

text.NRSV: Then they tried to arrest him again, but he escaped from their hands.
text.Rotherham: They were [therefore] again seeking to take him; and he went forth out of their hand.
text.KJV: Therefore they sought again to take him: but he escaped out of their hand,
text.NABRE: [Then] they tried again to arrest him; but he escaped from their power.
text.DRB: They sought therefore to apprehend him; and he went forth out of their hands.

text.NRSV: He went away again across the Jordan to the place where John had been baptizing earlier, and he remained there.
text.Rotherham: And he went away again, beyond the Jordan, unto the place where John was at the first, immersing; and he abode there.
text.KJV: And went away again beyond Jordan into the place where John at first baptized; and there he abode.
text.NABRE: He went back across the Jordan to the place where John first baptized, and there he remained.
text.DRB: And he went again beyond Jordan into that place where John was baptizing first; and he tarried there.

text.NRSV: Many came to him, and they were saying, “John performed no sign, but everything that John said about this man was true.”
text.Rotherham: And, many, came unto him, and were saying—John, indeed, did not so much as, one sign; but, all things, whatsoever John said concerning this one, were, true.
text.KJV: And many resorted unto him, and said, John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true.
text.NABRE: Many came to him and said, “John performed no sign, but everything John said about this man was true.”
text.DRB: and many came to him, and they said: That John indeed did no sign. But all things whatsoever John said of this man, were true.

text.NRSV: And many believed in him there.
text.Rotherham: And, many, believed on him there.
text.KJV: And many believed on him there.
text.NABRE: And many there began to believe in him.
text.DRB: And many believed in him.

text.NRSV: Now a certain man was ill, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
text.Rotherham: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus of Bethany, of the village of Mary and Martha her sister.
text.KJV: Now a certain man was sick, named Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.
text.NABRE: Now a man was ill, Lazarus from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
text.DRB: And there was a certain sick man, Lazarus of Bethania, of the town of Mary & Martha her sister.

text.NRSV: Mary was the one who anointed the Lord with perfume and wiped his feet with her hair; her brother Lazarus was ill.
text.Rotherham: And Mary was she who anointed the Lord with perfume, and wiped his feet with her hair—whose brother Lazarus was sick.
text.KJV: (It was that Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.)
text.NABRE: Mary was the one who had anointed the Lord with perfumed oil and dried his feet with her hair; it was her brother Lazarus who was ill.
text.DRB: (And Mary was she that anointed our Lord with ointment, & wiped his feet with her hair: whose brother Lazarus was sick.)

text.NRSV: So the sisters sent a message to Jesus, “Lord, he whom you love is ill.”
text.Rotherham: The sisters, therefore, sent out unto him, saying—Lord, see! he whom thou tenderly lovest, is sick.
text.KJV: Therefore his sisters sent unto him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.
text.NABRE: So the sisters sent word to him, saying, “Master, the one you love is ill.”
text.DRB: His sisters therefore sent to him saying: Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest, is sick.

text.NRSV: But when Jesus heard it, he said, “This illness does not lead to death; rather it is for God’s glory, so that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus hearing, said—This sickness, is not unto death, but for the glory of God,—that the Son of God may be glorified thereby.
text.KJV: When Jesus heard that, he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.
text.NABRE: When Jesus heard this he said, “This illness is not to end in death, but is for the glory of God, that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
text.DRB: And Jesus hearing, said to them: This sickness is not to death, but for the glory of God: that the Son of God may be glorified by it.

text.NRSV: Accordingly, though Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus,
text.Rotherham: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.
text.KJV: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.
text.NABRE: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
text.DRB: And Jesus loved Martha, and her sister Mary, and Lazarus.

text.NRSV: after having heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed two days longer in the place where he was.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he heard that he was sick, then, indeed, he abode in the place where he was, two days.
text.KJV: When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.
text.NABRE: So when he heard that he was ill, he remained for two days in the place where he was.
text.DRB: As he heard therefore that he was sick, then he tarried in the same place two days:

text.NRSV: Then after this he said to the disciples, “Let us go to Judea again.”
text.Rotherham: Then, after this, he saith unto the disciples—Let us be leading on into Judaea, again.
text.KJV: Then after that saith he to his disciples, Let us go into Judea again.
text.NABRE: Then after this he said to his disciples, “Let us go back to Judea.”
text.DRB: then after this he saith to his Disciples: Let us go into Jewry again.

text.NRSV: The disciples said to him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just now trying to stone you, and are you going there again?”
text.Rotherham: The disciples say unto him—Rabbi! just now, were the Jews seeking to stone thee—and, again, goest thou thither?
text.KJV: His disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?
text.NABRE: The disciples said to him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just trying to stone you, and you want to go back there?”
text.DRB: The Disciples say to him: Rabbi, now the Jews sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours of daylight? Those who walk during the day do not stumble, because they see the light of this world.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—Are there not, twelve hours, in the day? If one walk in the day, he doth no stumble, because, the light of this world, he seeth;
text.KJV: Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours in a day? If one walks during the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
text.DRB: Jesus answered: Are there not twelve hours of the day? If a man walk in the day, he stumbleth not: because he seeth the light of this world:

text.NRSV: But those who walk at night stumble, because the light is not in them.”
text.Rotherham: But, if one walk in the night, he stumbleth, because, the light, is not in him.
text.KJV: But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.
text.NABRE: But if one walks at night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.”
text.DRB: but if he walk in the night, he stumbleth, because the light is not in him.

text.NRSV: After saying this, he told them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I am going there to awaken him.”
text.Rotherham: These things, he said, and, after this, he saith unto them—Lazarus, our dear friend, hath fallen asleep; but I am going, that I may awake him.
text.KJV: These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.
text.NABRE: He said this, and then told them, “Our friend Lazarus is asleep, but I am going to awaken him.”
text.DRB: These things he said; and after this he saith to them: Lazarus our friend sleepeth; but I go that I may raise him from sleep.

text.NRSV: The disciples said to him, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will be all right.”
text.Rotherham: The disciples, therefore, said unto him—Lord! if he have fallen asleep, he will recover.
text.KJV: Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.
text.NABRE: So the disciples said to him, “Master, if he is asleep, he will be saved.”
text.DRB: His Disciples therefore said: Lord, if he sleep, he shall be safe.

text.NRSV: Jesus, however, had been speaking about his death, but they thought that he was referring merely to sleep.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus had spoken concerning his death; whereas, they, supposed that, concerning the taking of rest in sleep, he had been speaking.
text.KJV: Howbeit Jesus spake of his death: but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep.
text.NABRE: But Jesus was talking about his death, while they thought that he meant ordinary sleep.
text.DRB: But Jesus spake of his death; & they thought that he spake of the sleeping of sleep.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus told them plainly, “Lazarus is dead.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, then, said unto them, plainly,—Lazarus, died;
text.KJV: Then said Jesus unto them plainly, Lazarus is dead.
text.NABRE: So then Jesus said to them clearly, “Lazarus has died.
text.DRB: Then therefore Jesus said to them plainly: Lazarus is dead;

text.NRSV: For your sake I am glad I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.”
text.Rotherham: and I rejoice, for your sake—that ye may believe,—that I was not there; but let us be going unto him.
text.KJV: And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.
text.NABRE: And I am glad for you that I was not there, that you may believe. Let us go to him.”
text.DRB: and I am glad for your sake, that you may believe, because I was not there: but let us go to him.

text.NRSV: Thomas, who was called the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with him.”
text.Rotherham: Thomas, therefore, the one called Didymus, said unto his fellow-disciples—Let, us also, be going, that we may die with him.
text.KJV: Then said Thomas, which is called Didymus, unto his fellowdisciples, Let us also go, that we may die with him.
text.NABRE: So Thomas, called Didymus, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go to die with him.”
text.DRB: Thomas therefore, who is called Didymus, said to his condisciples: Let us also go, to die with him.

text.NRSV: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, coming, found that, four days already, had he been in the tomb.
text.KJV: Then when Jesus came, he found that he had lain in the grave four days already.
text.NABRE: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore came, and found him now having been four days in the grave.

text.NRSV: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, some two miles away,
text.Rotherham: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off;
text.KJV: Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off:
text.NABRE: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, only about two miles away.
text.DRB: (And Bethania was nigh to Jerusalem about fifteen furlongs.)

text.NRSV: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to console them about their brother.
text.Rotherham: and, many from among the Jews, had come unto Martha and Mary, that they might console them concerning their brother.
text.KJV: And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
text.NABRE: And many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to comfort them about their brother.
text.DRB: And many of the Jews were come to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.

text.NRSV: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went and met him, while Mary stayed at home.
text.Rotherham: Martha, therefore, when she heard that Jesus was coming, went to meet him; but, Mary, in the house, remained sitting.
text.KJV: Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met him: but Mary sat still in the house.
text.NABRE: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him; but Mary sat at home.
text.DRB: Martha therefore when she heard that Jesus was come, went to meet him; but Mary sat at home.

text.NRSV: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
text.Rotherham: Martha, therefore, said unto Jesus—Lord! if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died;
text.KJV: Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
text.NABRE: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
text.DRB: Martha therefore said to Jesus: Lord if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

text.NRSV: But even now I know that God will give you whatever you ask of him.”
text.Rotherham: And, now, I know, that, whatsoever thou shalt ask of God, God, will give unto thee.
text.KJV: But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give it thee.
text.NABRE: [But] even now I know that whatever you ask of God, God will give you.”
text.DRB: But now also I know that what things soever thou shalt ask of God, God will give thee.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Thy brother shall rise.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise.”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to her: Thy brother shall rise again.

text.NRSV: Martha said to him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection on the last day.”
text.Rotherham: Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise, in the resurrection, in the last day.
text.KJV: Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day.
text.NABRE: Martha said to him, “I know he will rise, in the resurrection on the last day.”
text.DRB: Martha saith to him: I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection, in the last day.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. Those who believe in me, even though they die, will live,
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto her—I, am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth on me, even though he die, shall live again!
text.KJV: Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:
text.NABRE: Jesus told her, “I am the resurrection and the life; whoever believes in me, even if he dies, will live,
text.DRB: Jesus saith to her: I am the resurrection and the life; he that believeth in me, although he be dead, shall live.

text.NRSV: and everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?”
text.Rotherham: And, no one who liveth again and believeth on me, shall in anywise die, unto times age-abiding. Believest thou this?
text.KJV: And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?
text.NABRE: and everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?”
text.DRB: And every one that liveth, and believeth in me, shall not die for ever. Believest thou this?

text.NRSV: She said to him, “Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Messiah, the Son of God, the one coming into the world.”
text.Rotherham: She saith unto him—Yea, Lord! I, have believed, that, thou, art the Christ, the Son of God,—He who, into the world, should come.
text.KJV: She saith unto him, Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son of God, which should come into the world.
text.NABRE: She said to him, “Yes, Lord. I have come to believe that you are the Messiah, the Son of God, the one who is coming into the world.”
text.DRB: She saith to him: Yea Lord, I have believed that thou art Christ the Son of God that art come into this world.

text.NRSV: When she had said this, she went back and called her sister Mary, and told her privately, “The Teacher is here and is calling for you.”
text.Rotherham: And this saying, she went away, and called Mary her sister, secretly, saying—The teacher, is present, and calleth thee.
text.KJV: And when she had so said, she went her way, and called Mary her sister secretly, saying, The Master is come, and calleth for thee.
text.NABRE: When she had said this, she went and called her sister Mary secretly, saying, “The teacher is here and is asking for you.”
text.DRB: And when she had said these things, she went, and called Mary her sister secretly, saying: The Master is come, & calleth thee.

text.NRSV: And when she heard it, she got up quickly and went to him.
text.Rotherham: And, she, when she heard, was roused up quickly, and was coming unto him.
text.KJV: As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly, and came unto him.
text.NABRE: As soon as she heard this, she rose quickly and went to him.
text.DRB: She, when she heard, riseth quickly & cometh to him.

text.NRSV: Now Jesus had not yet come to the village, but was still at the place where Martha had met him.
text.Rotherham: Not yet, however, had Jesus come into the village, but was still in the place where, Martha, met him.
text.KJV: Now Jesus was not yet come into the town, but was in that place where Martha met him.
text.NABRE: For Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was still where Martha had met him.
text.DRB: For Jesus was not yet come into the town: but he was yet in that place where Martha had met him.

text.NRSV: The Jews who were with her in the house, consoling her, saw Mary get up quickly and go out. They followed her because they thought that she was going to the tomb to weep there.
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, who were with her in the house and consoling her, seeing Mary, that quickly she arose and went out, followed her, supposing that she was withdrawing unto the tomb, that she might weep there.
text.KJV: The Jews then which were with her in the house, and comforted her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up hastily and went out, followed her, saying, She goeth unto the grave to weep there.
text.NABRE: So when the Jews who were with her in the house comforting her saw Mary get up quickly and go out, they followed her, presuming that she was going to the tomb to weep there.
text.DRB: The Jews therefore that were with her in the house and did comfort her, when they saw Mary that she rose quickly and went forth, followed her, saying: That she goeth to the grave, to weep there.

text.NRSV: When Mary came where Jesus was and saw him, she knelt at his feet and said to him, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
text.Rotherham: Mary, therefore, when she came where Jesus was, seeing him, fell at his feet, saying unto him—Lord! if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
text.KJV: Then when Mary was come where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying unto him, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
text.NABRE: When Mary came to where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said to him, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
text.DRB: Mary therefore when she was come where Jesus was, seeing him, fell at his feet, and saith to him: Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her also weeping, he was greatly disturbed in spirit and deeply moved.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, when he saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her weeping, was indignant in the spirit, and troubled himself,
text.KJV: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews also weeping which came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,
text.NABRE: When Jesus saw her weeping and the Jews who had come with her weeping, he became perturbed and deeply troubled,
text.DRB: Jesus therefore when he saw her, weeping, and the Jews that were come with her, weeping, he groaned in spirit, and troubled himself,

text.NRSV: He said, “Where have you laid him?” They said to him, “Lord, come and see.”
text.Rotherham: and said—Where have ye laid him? They say unto him—Lord! come and see.
text.KJV: And said, Where have ye laid him? They said unto him, Lord, come and see.
text.NABRE: and said, “Where have you laid him?” They said to him, “Sir, come and see.”
text.DRB: and said: Where have you laid him? They say to him: Lord, come and see.

text.NRSV: Jesus began to weep.
text.Rotherham: Jesus wept.
text.KJV: Jesus wept.
text.NABRE: And Jesus wept.
text.DRB: And Jesus wept.

text.NRSV: So the Jews said, “See how he loved him!”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, were saying—See! how tenderly he loved him!
text.KJV: Then said the Jews, Behold how he loved him!
text.NABRE: So the Jews said, “See how he loved him.”
text.DRB: The Jews therefore said: Behold how he loved him.

text.NRSV: But some of them said, “Could not he who opened the eyes of the blind man have kept this man from dying?”
text.Rotherham: But, some from among them, said—Could not this man, who opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that, this one also, should not have died?
text.KJV: And some of them said, Could not this man, which opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that even this man should not have died?
text.NABRE: But some of them said, “Could not the one who opened the eyes of the blind man have done something so that this man would not have died?”
text.DRB: But certain of them said: Could not he that opened the eyes of the blind man, make that this man should not die?

text.NRSV: Then Jesus, again greatly disturbed, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone was lying against it.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, again, being indignant within himself, cometh unto the tomb. Now it was a cave, and, a stone, was lying thereon.
text.KJV: Jesus therefore again groaning in himself cometh to the grave. It was a cave, and a stone lay upon it.
text.NABRE: So Jesus, perturbed again, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone lay across it.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore again groaning in himself, cometh to the grave: and it was a cave; and a stone was laid over it.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the sister of the dead man, said to him, “Lord, already there is a stench because he has been dead four days.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, saith, Take ye away the stone! Martha, the sister of the deceased, saith unto him—Lord! by this time, he stinketh, for it is, four days.
text.KJV: Jesus said, Take ye away the stone. Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto him, Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been dead four days.
text.NABRE: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the dead man’s sister, said to him, “Lord, by now there will be a stench; he has been dead for four days.”
text.DRB: Jesus saith: Take away the stone. Martha the sister of him that was dead, said to him: Lord, now he stinketh, for he is now of four days.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Did I not tell you that if you believed, you would see the glory of God?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldst believe, thou shouldst see the glory of God?
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto her, Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God?
text.NABRE: Jesus said to her, “Did I not tell you that if you believe you will see the glory of God?”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to her: Did not I say to thee, that if thou believe, thou shalt see the glory of God?

text.NRSV: So they took away the stone. And Jesus looked upward and said, “Father, I thank you for having heard me.
text.Rotherham: So they took away the stone. And, Jesus, lifted up his eyes on high, and said—Father! I thank thee, thou didst hear me:
text.KJV: Then they took away the stone from the place where the dead was laid. And Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me.
text.NABRE: So they took away the stone. And Jesus raised his eyes and said, “Father, I thank you for hearing me.
text.DRB: They took therefore the stone away. And Jesus lifting his eyes upward, said: Father, I give thee thanks that thou hast heard me.

text.NRSV: I knew that you always hear me, but I have said this for the sake of the crowd standing here, so that they may believe that you sent me.”
text.Rotherham: I, indeed, knew that, always, unto me, thou dost hearken; but, for the sake of the multitude standing around, I spake,—that they might believe that, thou, didst send me forth.
text.KJV: And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said it, that they may believe that thou hast sent me.
text.NABRE: I know that you always hear me; but because of the crowd here I have said this, that they may believe that you sent me.”
text.DRB: And I did know that thou doest always hear me, but for the people that standeth about, have I said it, that they may believe that thou hast sent me.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, he cried with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”
text.Rotherham: And, these things, having said, with a loud voice, he cried out—Lazarus! Come forth!
text.KJV: And when he thus had spoken, he cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth.
text.NABRE: And when he had said this, he cried out in a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”
text.DRB: When he had said these things, he cried with a loud voice: Lazarus, come forth.

text.NRSV: The dead man came out, his hands and feet bound with strips of cloth, and his face wrapped in a cloth. Jesus said to them, “Unbind him, and let him go.”
text.Rotherham: He that was dead came forth, bound feet and hands with bandages, and, his face, with a napkin, was bound about. Jesus saith unto them—Loose him, and let him go.
text.KJV: And he that was dead came forth, bound hand and foot with graveclothes: and his face was bound about with a napkin. Jesus saith unto them, Loose him, and let him go.
text.NABRE: The dead man came out, tied hand and foot with burial bands, and his face was wrapped in a cloth. So Jesus said to them, “Untie him and let him go.”
text.DRB: And forthwith he came forth that had been dead, bound feet and hands with winding bands, and his face was tied with a napkin. Jesus said to them: Loose him, and let him go.

text.NRSV: Many of the Jews therefore, who had come with Mary and had seen what Jesus did, believed in him.
text.Rotherham: Many therefore from among the Jews, who had come unto Mary, and gazed on what he did, believed on him
text.KJV: Then many of the Jews which came to Mary, and had seen the things which Jesus did, believed on him.
text.NABRE: Session of the Sanhedrin. Now many of the Jews who had come to Mary and seen what he had done began to believe in him.
text.DRB: Many therefore of the Jews that were come to Mary & Martha, & had seen the things that Jesus did, believed in him.

text.NRSV: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what he had done.
text.Rotherham: but, certain from among them, went away unto the Pharisees, and told them what Jesus had done.
text.KJV: But some of them went their ways to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.
text.NABRE: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
text.DRB: And certain of them went to the Pharisees, & told them the things that Jesus did.

text.NRSV: So the chief priests and the Pharisees called a meeting of the council, and said, “What are we to do? This man is performing many signs.
text.Rotherham: The High-priests and Pharisees, therefore, brought together a high-council, and were saying—What are we to do, in that, this man, doeth, many signs?
text.KJV: Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles.
text.NABRE: So the chief priests and the Pharisees convened the Sanhedrin and said, “What are we going to do? This man is performing many signs.
text.DRB: The chief Priests therefore & the Pharisees gathered a Council, & said: What do we, for this man doeth many signs.

text.NRSV: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and destroy both our holy place and our nation.”
text.Rotherham: If we let him alone thus, all, will believe on him, and the Romans will come, and take away, both our place and nation.
text.KJV: If we let him thus alone, all men will believe on him: and the Romans shall come and take away both our place and nation.
text.NABRE: If we leave him alone, all will believe in him, and the Romans will come and take away both our land and our nation.”
text.DRB: If we let him alone so, all will believe in him: & the Romans will come, & take away our place & nation.

text.NRSV: But one of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all!
text.Rotherham: But, a certain one from among them, Caiaphas, being, High-priest, for that year, said unto them—Ye, know, nothing at all;
text.KJV: And one of them, named Caiaphas, being the high priest that same year, said unto them, Ye know nothing at all,
text.NABRE: But one of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing,
text.DRB: But one of them named Caiphas, being the high Priest of that year, said to them: You know nothing,

text.NRSV: You do not understand that it is better for you to have one man die for the people than to have the whole nation destroyed.”
text.Rotherham: nor do ye take into account, that it is profitable for you that, one man, should die for the people, and not, the whole nation, perish.
text.KJV: Nor consider that it is expedient for us, that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not.
text.NABRE: nor do you consider that it is better for you that one man should die instead of the people, so that the whole nation may not perish.”
text.DRB: neither do you consider: that it is expedient for us that one man die for the people, & the whole nation perish not.

text.NRSV: He did not say this on his own, but being high priest that year he prophesied that Jesus was about to die for the nation,
text.Rotherham: This, however, from himself, he spake not, but, being, High-priest, for that year, he prophesied, that Jesus was about to die for the nation;
text.KJV: And this spake he not of himself: but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus should die for that nation;
text.NABRE: He did not say this on his own, but since he was high priest for that year, he prophesied that Jesus was going to die for the nation,
text.DRB: And this he said not of himself: but being the high Priest of that year, he prophesied that Jesus should die for the nation.

text.NRSV: and not for the nation only, but to gather into one the dispersed children of God.
text.Rotherham: and, not for the nation only, but that, the scattered children of God also, he might gather together into one.
text.KJV: And not for that nation only, but that also he should gather together in one the children of God that were scattered abroad.
text.NABRE: and not only for the nation, but also to gather into one the dispersed children of God.
text.DRB: And not only for the nation, but to gather into one the children of God that were dispersed.

text.NRSV: So from that day on they planned to put him to death.
text.Rotherham: From that day, therefore, they took counsel, that they might put him to death.
text.KJV: Then from that day forth they took counsel together for to put him to death.
text.NABRE: So from that day on they planned to kill him.
text.DRB: From that day therefore they devised to kill him.

text.NRSV: Jesus therefore no longer walked about openly among the Jews, but went from there to a town called Ephraim in the region near the wilderness; and he remained there with the disciples.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, no longer openly, was walking among the Jews, but departed thence, into the country near the desert, unto a city called, Ephraim; and, there, abode, with his disciples.
text.KJV: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples.
text.NABRE: So Jesus no longer walked about in public among the Jews, but he left for the region near the desert, to a town called Ephraim, and there he remained with his disciples.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews, but he went into the country beside the desert unto a city that is called Ephrem, and there he abode with his Disciples.

text.NRSV: Now the Passover of the Jews was near, and many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover to purify themselves.
text.Rotherham: Now the passover of the Jews was at hand, and many went up unto Jerusalem, out of the country, before the passover, that they might purify themselves.
text.KJV: And the Jews’ passover was nigh at hand: and many went out of the country up to Jerusalem before the passover, to purify themselves.
text.NABRE: The Last Passover. Now the Passover of the Jews was near, and many went up from the country to Jerusalem before Passover to purify themselves.
text.DRB: And the Pasch of the Jews was at hand: & many of the country went up to Jerusalem before the Pasch to sanctify themselves.

text.NRSV: They were looking for Jesus and were asking one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think? Surely he will not come to the festival, will he?”
text.Rotherham: They were therefore seeking Jesus, and were saying one to another, in the temple, standing—How seemeth it unto you? that he will in nowise come unto the feast?
text.KJV: Then sought they for Jesus, and spake among themselves, as they stood in the temple, What think ye, that he will not come to the feast?
text.NABRE: They looked for Jesus and said to one another as they were in the temple area, “What do you think? That he will not come to the feast?”
text.DRB: They sought Jesus therefore; and they communed one with another, standing in the temple: What think you, in that he is not come to the festival day? And the chief Priests & Pharisees had given commandment, that if any man should know where he was, he should tell, that they might apprehend him.

text.NRSV: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where Jesus was should let them know, so that they might arrest him.
text.Rotherham: Now the High-priests and the Pharisees had given commands, that, if anyone came to know where he was, he should inform [them], so that they might seize him.
text.KJV: Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where he were, he should shew it, that they might take him.
text.NABRE: For the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where he was, he should inform them, so that they might arrest him.

text.NRSV: Six days before the Passover Jesus came to Bethany, the home of Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, six days before the passover, came unto Bethany, where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from among the dead.
text.KJV: Then Jesus six days before the passover came to Bethany, where Lazarus was which had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.
text.NABRE: Six days before Passover Jesus came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore six days before the Pasch came to Bethania, where Lazarus was, that had been dead, whom Jesus raised.

text.NRSV: There they gave a dinner for him. Martha served, and Lazarus was one of those at the table with him.
text.Rotherham: So they made for him a supper, there; and, Martha, was ministering, but, Lazarus, was one of them who were reclining with him.
text.KJV: There they made him a supper; and Martha served: but Lazarus was one of them that sat at the table with him.
text.NABRE: They gave a dinner for him there, and Martha served, while Lazarus was one of those reclining at table with him.
text.DRB: And they made him a supper there: and Martha ministered, but Lazarus was one of them that sat at the table with him.

text.NRSV: Mary took a pound of costly perfume made of pure nard, anointed Jesus’ feet, and wiped them with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
text.Rotherham: Mary, therefore, taking a pound of pure nard perfume, very precious, anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped, with her hair, his feet; and, the house, was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
text.KJV: Then took Mary a pound of ointment of spikenard, very costly, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair: and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment.
text.NABRE: Mary took a liter of costly perfumed oil made from genuine aromatic nard and anointed the feet of Jesus and dried them with her hair; the house was filled with the fragrance of the oil.
text.DRB: Mary therefore took a pound of ointment of right spikenard, precious, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair: and the house was filled of the odour of the ointment.

text.NRSV: But Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples (the one who was about to betray him), said,
text.Rotherham: [But] Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples, he that was about to deliver him up, saith—
text.KJV: Then saith one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, which should betray him,
text.NABRE: Then Judas the Iscariot, one [of] his disciples, and the one who would betray him, said,
text.DRB: One therefore of his Disciples, Judas Iscariote, he that was to betray him, said:

text.NRSV: “Why was this perfume not sold for three hundred denarii and the money given to the poor?”
text.Rotherham: Why was, this perfume, not sold for three hundred denaries, and given unto the destitute?
