text.KJV: Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence, and given to the poor?
text.NABRE: “Why was this oil not sold for three hundred days’ wages and given to the poor?”
text.DRB: Why was not this ointment sold for three-hundred pence, and given to the poor?

text.NRSV: (He said this not because he cared about the poor, but because he was a thief; he kept the common purse and used to steal what was put into it.)
text.Rotherham: Howbeit he said this, not that, for the destitute, he cared, but because, a thief, he was, and holding, the bag, used to carry away, what was cast therein.
text.KJV: This he said, not that he cared for the poor; but because he was a thief, and had the bag, and bare what was put therein.
text.NABRE: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief and held the money bag and used to steal the contributions.
text.DRB: And he said this, not because he cared for the poor; but because he was a thief, and having the purse, carried the things that were put in.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “Leave her alone. She bought it so that she might keep it for the day of my burial.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said—Let her alone, that, for the day of my burial, she may observe it;
text.KJV: Then said Jesus, Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this.
text.NABRE: So Jesus said, “Leave her alone. Let her keep this for the day of my burial.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said: Let her alone that she may keep it for the day of my burial.

text.NRSV: You always have the poor with you, but you do not always have me.”
text.Rotherham: For, the destitute, always, have ye with you, whereas, me, not always, have ye.
text.KJV: For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.
text.NABRE: You always have the poor with you, but you do not always have me.”
text.DRB: For the poor you have always with you; but me you shall not have always.

text.NRSV: When the great crowd of the Jews learned that he was there, they came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
text.Rotherham: The great multitude of the Jews, therefore, got to know that he was, there, and came, not on account of Jesus only, but that, Lazarus also, they might see, whom he had raised from among the dead.
text.KJV: Much people of the Jews therefore knew that he was there: and they came not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.
text.NABRE: [The] large crowd of the Jews found out that he was there and came, not only because of Jesus, but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
text.DRB: A great multitude therefore of the Jews knew that he was there; and they came, not for Jesus only, but that they might see Lazarus, whom he raised from the dead.

text.NRSV: So the chief priests planned to put Lazarus to death as well,
text.Rotherham: But the High-priests took counsel, that Lazarus also, they might put to death;
text.KJV: But the chief priests consulted that they might put Lazarus also to death;
text.NABRE: And the chief priests plotted to kill Lazarus too,
text.DRB: But the chief Priests devised for to kill Lazarus also:

text.NRSV: since it was on account of him that many of the Jews were deserting and were believing in Jesus.
text.Rotherham: because, many of the Jews, by reason of him, were withdrawing, and believing on Jesus.
text.KJV: Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
text.NABRE: because many of the Jews were turning away and believing in Jesus because of him. The Entry into Jerusalem.
text.DRB: because many for him of the Jews went away, and believed in Jesus.

text.NRSV: The next day the great crowd that had come to the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, the great multitude that had come unto the feast, hearing that Jesus was coming into Jerusalem,
text.KJV: On the next day much people that were come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
text.NABRE: On the next day, when the great crowd that had come to the feast heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
text.DRB: And on the morrow a great multitude that was come to the festival day, when they had heard that Jesus cometh to Jerusalem,

text.NRSV: So they took branches of palm trees and went out to meet him, shouting, “Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord— the King of Israel!”
text.Rotherham: took the branches of the palm trees, and went out to meet him, and began crying aloud—Hosanna! Blessed is he that is coming in the name of the Lord,—even the King of Israel!
text.KJV: Took branches of palm trees, and went forth to meet him, and cried, Hosanna: Blessed is the King of Israel that cometh in the name of the Lord.
text.NABRE: they took palm branches and went out to meet him, and cried out: “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, [even] the king of Israel.”
text.DRB: they took the boughs of palms, and went forth to meet him, & cried: Hosanna, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of our Lord, the King of Israel.

text.NRSV: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it; as it is written:
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, finding a young ass, took his seat thereon, just as it is written—
text.KJV: And Jesus, when he had found a young ass, sat thereon; as it is written,
text.NABRE: Jesus found an ass and sat upon it, as is written:
text.DRB: And Jesus found a young ass, and sat upon it, as it is written:

text.NRSV: “Do not be afraid, daughter of Zion. Look, your king is coming, sitting on a donkey’s colt!”
text.Rotherham: Do not fear, O daughter of Zion! Lo! thy king, cometh, sitting upon the colt of an ass.
text.KJV: Fear not, daughter of Sion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass’s colt.
text.NABRE: “Fear no more, O daughter Zion; see, your king comes, seated upon an ass’s colt.”
text.DRB: Fear not daughter of Sion: behold, thy King cometh sitting upon an ass's colt.

text.NRSV: His disciples did not understand these things at first; but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things had been written of him and had been done to him.
text.Rotherham: These things, his disciples noticed not, at the first; but, when Jesus was glorified, then, remembered they that, these things, had, for him, been written,—and that, these things, they had done unto him.
text.KJV: These things understood not his disciples at the first: but when Jesus was glorified, then remembered they that these things were written of him, and that they had done these things unto him.
text.NABRE: His disciples did not understand this at first, but when Jesus had been glorified they remembered that these things were written about him and that they had done this for him.
text.DRB: These things his Disciples did not know at the first: but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things had been written of him, and these things they did to him.

text.NRSV: So the crowd that had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead continued to testify.
text.Rotherham: The multitude, therefore, that was with him when he called, Lazarus, out of the tomb, and raised him from among the dead, was bearing witness.
text.KJV: The people therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of his grave, and raised him from the dead, bare record.
text.NABRE: So the crowd that was with him when he called Lazarus from the tomb and raised him from death continued to testify.
text.DRB: The multitude therefore gave testimony, which was with him when he called Lazarus out of the grave, and raised him from the dead.

text.NRSV: It was also because they heard that he had performed this sign that the crowd went to meet him.
text.Rotherham: On this account, the multitude met him also, because they heard that he had done, this sign.
text.KJV: For this cause the people also met him, for that they heard that he had done this miracle.
text.NABRE: This was [also] why the crowd went to meet him, because they heard that he had done this sign.
text.DRB: For therefore also the multitude came to meet him, because they heard that he had done this sign.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees then said to one another, “You see, you can do nothing. Look, the world has gone after him!”
text.Rotherham: the Pharisees, therefore, said among themselves—Ye observe that ye are profiting nothing: See! the world, after him, hath gone away.
text.KJV: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, Perceive ye how ye prevail nothing? behold, the world is gone after him.
text.NABRE: So the Pharisees said to one another, “You see that you are gaining nothing. Look, the whole world has gone after him.” The Coming of Jesus’ Hour.
text.DRB: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves: Do you see that we prevail nothing? Behold the whole world is gone after him.

text.NRSV: Now among those who went up to worship at the festival were some Greeks.
text.Rotherham: Now there were certain Greeks, from among them who were coming up that they might worship in the feast.
text.KJV: And there were certain Greeks among them that came up to worship at the feast:
text.NABRE: Now there were some Greeks among those who had come up to worship at the feast.
text.DRB: And there were certain Gentiles of them that came up to adore in the festival day.

text.NRSV: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and said to him, “Sir, we wish to see Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: These, therefore, came unto Philip, him who was from Bethsaida of Galilee,—and were requesting him, saying—Sir! We desire to see, Jesus.
text.KJV: The same came therefore to Philip, which was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and desired him, saying, Sir, we would see Jesus.
text.NABRE: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and asked him, “Sir, we would like to see Jesus.”
text.DRB: These therefore came to Philippe who was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and desired him, saying: Sir, we are desirous to see Jesus.

text.NRSV: Philip went and told Andrew; then Andrew and Philip went and told Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Philip cometh, and telleth Andrew: Andrew and Philip come, and tell Jesus.
text.KJV: Philip cometh and telleth Andrew: and again Andrew and Philip tell Jesus.
text.NABRE: Philip went and told Andrew; then Andrew and Philip went and told Jesus.
text.DRB: Philippe cometh and telleth Andrew. Again Andrew & Philippe told Jesus.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, answereth them, saying—The hour hath come, that the Son of Man should be glorified!
text.KJV: And Jesus answered them, saying, The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
text.DRB: But Jesus answered them, saying: The hour is come, that the Son of man shall be glorified.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains just a single grain; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.
text.Rotherham: Verily! Verily, I say unto you: Except, the kernel of wheat, shall fall into the ground, and die, it, alone, abideth; but, if it die, much fruit, it beareth.
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone: but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains just a grain of wheat; but if it dies, it produces much fruit.
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to you, unless the grain of wheat falling into the ground, die: itself remaineth alone. but if it die, it bringeth much fruit.

text.NRSV: Those who love their life lose it, and those who hate their life in this world will keep it for eternal life.
text.Rotherham: He that loveth his life, loseth it; but, he that hateth his life, in this world, unto life age-abiding, shall guard it.
text.KJV: He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.
text.NABRE: Whoever loves his life loses it, and whoever hates his life in this world will preserve it for eternal life.
text.DRB: He that loveth his life shall lose it: and he that hateth his life in this world, doth keep it to life everlasting.

text.NRSV: Whoever serves me must follow me, and where I am, there will my servant be also. Whoever serves me, the Father will honor.
text.Rotherham: If, with me, anyone be ministering, with me, let him be following; and, where, I, am, there, my minister also, shall be. If anyone, with me, be ministering, the Father, will honour him.
text.KJV: If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will my Father honour.
text.NABRE: Whoever serves me must follow me, and where I am, there also will my servant be. The Father will honor whoever serves me.
text.DRB: If any man minister to me, let him follow me: and where I am, there also shall my minister be. If any man minister to me, my Father will honour him.

text.NRSV: “Now my soul is troubled. And what should I say—‘Father, save me from this hour’? No, it is for this reason that I have come to this hour.
text.Rotherham: Now, is my soul troubled,—and what can I say? Father! save me from this hour? But, on this account, came I unto this hour. Father, glorify thy name!
text.KJV: Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour.
text.NABRE: “I am troubled now. Yet what should I say? ‘Father, save me from this hour’? But it was for this purpose that I came to this hour.
text.DRB: Now my soul is troubled. And what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour. But therefore came I into this hour.

text.NRSV: Father, glorify your name.” Then a voice came from heaven, “I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.”
text.Rotherham: There came, therefore, a voice out of heaven—I both have glorified it, and will glorify it again.
text.KJV: Father, glorify thy name. Then came there a voice from heaven, saying, I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.
text.NABRE: Father, glorify your name.” Then a voice came from heaven, “I have glorified it and will glorify it again.”
text.DRB: Father, glorify thy name. A voice therefore came from Heaven: Both I have glorified it, and again I will glorify it.

text.NRSV: The crowd standing there heard it and said that it was thunder. Others said, “An angel has spoken to him.”
text.Rotherham: [So], the multitude that was standing by, and heard it, were saying—It hath, thundered. Others, were saying—A messenger, unto him, hath spoken.
text.KJV: The people therefore, that stood by, and heard it, said that it thundered: others said, An angel spake to him.
text.NABRE: The crowd there heard it and said it was thunder; but others said, “An angel has spoken to him.”
text.DRB: The multitude therefore that stood and had heard, said that it thundered. Others said, An Angel spake to him.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “This voice has come for your sake, not for mine.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said—Not for my sake, hath this voice come, but, for your sake.
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said, This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said, “This voice did not come for my sake but for yours.
text.DRB: Jesus answered, and said: This voice came not for me, but for your sake.

text.NRSV: Now is the judgment of this world; now the ruler of this world will be driven out.
text.Rotherham: Now, is there, a judging, of this world,—Now, the ruler of this world, shall be cast out;
text.KJV: Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.
text.NABRE: Now is the time of judgment on this world; now the ruler of this world will be driven out.
text.DRB: Now is the judgement of the world: now the Prince of this world shall be cast forth.

text.NRSV: And I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.”
text.Rotherham: And, I, if I be lifted up out of the earth, will draw, all, unto myself.
text.KJV: And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all men unto me.
text.NABRE: And when I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to myself.”
text.DRB: And I, if I be exalted from the earth, will draw all things to myself.

text.NRSV: He said this to indicate the kind of death he was to die.
text.Rotherham: But, this, he was saying, signifying, by what manner of death, he was about to die.
text.KJV: This he said, signifying what death he should die.
text.NABRE: He said this indicating the kind of death he would die.
text.DRB: (And this he said, signifying what death he should die.)

text.NRSV: The crowd answered him, “We have heard from the law that the Messiah remains forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
text.Rotherham: The multitude, therefore, answered him—We, have heard, out of the law, that, the Christ, abideth evermore; How then dost, thou, say,—It behoveth the Son of Man to be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?
text.KJV: The people answered him, We have heard out of the law that Christ abideth for ever: and how sayest thou, The Son of man must be lifted up? who is this Son of man?
text.NABRE: So the crowd answered him, “We have heard from the law that the Messiah remains forever. Then how can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
text.DRB: The multitude answered him: We have heard out of the law, that Christ abideth for ever; and how sayest thou: The Son of man must be exalted? Who is this Son of man?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “The light is with you for a little longer. Walk while you have the light, so that the darkness may not overtake you. If you walk in the darkness, you do not know where you are going.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto them—Yet a little while, the light, is, among you: Walk, while ye have, the light, lest, darkness, on you should lay hold; and, he that walketh in darkness, knoweth not whither he drifteth.
text.KJV: Then Jesus said unto them, Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “The light will be among you only a little while. Walk while you have the light, so that darkness may not overcome you. Whoever walks in the dark does not know where he is going.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to them: Yet a little while, the light is among you. Walk whiles you have the light, that the darkness overtake you not. And he that walketh in darkness, knoweth not whither he goeth.

text.NRSV: While you have the light, believe in the light, so that you may become children of light.” After Jesus had said this, he departed and hid from them.
text.Rotherham: While, the light, ye have, believe on the light, that, sons of light, ye may become. These things, spake Jesus,—and, departing, was hid from them.
text.KJV: While ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light. These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide himself from them.
text.NABRE: While you have the light, believe in the light, so that you may become children of the light.” Unbelief and Belief Among the Jews. After he had said this, Jesus left and hid from them.
text.DRB: Whiles you have the light, believe in the light, that you may be the children of light. These things Jesus spake and he went away, and hid himself from them.

text.NRSV: Although he had performed so many signs in their presence, they did not believe in him.
text.Rotherham: And, although such signs, as these, he had done before them, they were not believing on him:—
text.KJV: But though he had done so many miracles before them, yet they believed not on him:
text.NABRE: Although he had performed so many signs in their presence they did not believe in him,
text.DRB: And whereas he had done so many signs before them, they believed not in him:

text.NRSV: This was to fulfill the word spoken by the prophet Isaiah: “Lord, who has believed our message, and to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”
text.Rotherham: that, the word of Isaiah the prophet, might be fulfilled, which said—Lord! who believed, what we have heard? And, the arm of the Lord, to whom, was it revealed?
text.KJV: That the saying of Esaias the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spake, Lord, who hath believed our report? and to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?
text.NABRE: in order that the word which Isaiah the prophet spoke might be fulfilled: “Lord, who has believed our preaching, to whom has the might of the Lord been revealed?”
text.DRB: that the saying of Esay the Prophet might be fulfilled, which he said: Lord, who hath believed the hearing of us? and the arm of our Lord to whom hath it been revealed?

text.NRSV: And so they could not believe, because Isaiah also said,
text.Rotherham: On this account, they could not believe, because, again, said Isaiah—
text.KJV: Therefore they could not believe, because that Esaias said again,
text.NABRE: For this reason they could not believe, because again Isaiah said:
text.DRB: Therefore they could not believe, because Esay said again:

text.NRSV: “He has blinded their eyes and hardened their heart, so that they might not look with their eyes, and understand with their heart and turn— and I would heal them.”
text.Rotherham: He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart; lest they should see with their eyes, and should understand with their heart, and should turn,—and I should heal them.
text.KJV: He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart; that they should not see with their eyes, nor understand with their heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.
text.NABRE: “He blinded their eyes and hardened their heart, so that they might not see with their eyes and understand with their heart and be converted, and I would heal them.”
text.DRB: He hath blinded their eyes, and indurated their heart: that they may not see with their eyes, nor understand with their heart, and be converted, and I heal them.

text.NRSV: Isaiah said this because he saw his glory and spoke about him.
text.Rotherham: These things, said Isaiah, because he saw his glory, and spake concerning him.
text.KJV: These things said Esaias, when he saw his glory, and spake of him.
text.NABRE: Isaiah said this because he saw his glory and spoke about him.
text.DRB: These things said Esaie, when he saw his glory, & spake of him.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless many, even of the authorities, believed in him. But because of the Pharisees they did not confess it, for fear that they would be put out of the synagogue;
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, however, even from among the rulers, many believed on him; but, because of the Pharisees, they were not confessing him, lest, excommunicants from the synagogue, they should be made;
text.KJV: Nevertheless among the chief rulers also many believed on him; but because of the Pharisees they did not confess him, lest they should be put out of the synagogue:
text.NABRE: Nevertheless, many, even among the authorities, believed in him, but because of the Pharisees they did not acknowledge it openly in order not to be expelled from the synagogue.
text.DRB: But yet of the Princes also many believed in him: but for the Pharisees they did not confess, that they might not be cast out of the Synagogue.

text.NRSV: for they loved human glory more than the glory that comes from God.
text.Rotherham: for they loved the glory of men, more than the glory of God.
text.KJV: For they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.
text.NABRE: For they preferred human praise to the glory of God.
text.DRB: For they loved the glory of men more, than the glory of God.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus cried aloud: “Whoever believes in me believes not in me but in him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, cried aloud, and said—He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me;
text.KJV: Jesus cried and said, He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.
text.NABRE: Recapitulation. Jesus cried out and said, “Whoever believes in me believes not only in me but also in the one who sent me,
text.DRB: But Jesus cried, and said: He that believeth in me, doth not believe in me, but in him that sent me.

text.NRSV: And whoever sees me sees him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: And, he that vieweth me, vieweth him that sent me.
text.KJV: And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.
text.NABRE: and whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
text.DRB: And he that seeth me, seeth him that sent me.

text.NRSV: I have come as light into the world, so that everyone who believes in me should not remain in the darkness.
text.Rotherham: I, a light, into the world have come, that, no one who believeth on me, in darkness, should abide.
text.KJV: I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness.
text.NABRE: I came into the world as light, so that everyone who believes in me might not remain in darkness.
text.DRB: I a light am come into this world: that every one which believeth in me, may not remain in the darkness.

text.NRSV: I do not judge anyone who hears my words and does not keep them, for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.
text.Rotherham: And, if anyone shall hearken unto my sayings, and not guard them, I, am not judging him; for I came not that I might judge the world, but that I might save the world.
text.KJV: And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.
text.NABRE: And if anyone hears my words and does not observe them, I do not condemn him, for I did not come to condemn the world but to save the world.
text.DRB: And if any man hear my words, and keep them not: I do not judge him. For I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.

text.NRSV: The one who rejects me and does not receive my word has a judge; on the last day the word that I have spoken will serve as judge,
text.Rotherham: He that setteth me aside, and receiveth not my sayings, hath that which is to judge him: The word that I spake, that, will judge him, in the last day.
text.KJV: He that rejecteth me, and receiveth not my words, hath one that judgeth him: the word that I have spoken, the same shall judge him in the last day.
text.NABRE: Whoever rejects me and does not accept my words has something to judge him: the word that I spoke, it will condemn him on the last day,
text.DRB: He that despiseth me, & receiveth not my words, hath that judgeth him. the word that I have spoken, that shall judge him in the last day.

text.NRSV: for I have not spoken on my own, but the Father who sent me has himself given me a commandment about what to say and what to speak.
text.Rotherham: Because, I, out of myself, spake not, but, the Father who sent me, hath, himself, given me commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.
text.KJV: For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.
text.NABRE: because I did not speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me what to say and speak.
text.DRB: Because of myself I have not spoken, but the Father that sent me, he gave me commandment what I should say, and what I should speak.

text.NRSV: And I know that his commandment is eternal life. What I speak, therefore, I speak just as the Father has told me.”
text.Rotherham: And I know that, his commandment, is, life age-abiding; The things, therefore, which I speak, just as the Father hath told me, so, I speak.
text.KJV: And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.
text.NABRE: And I know that his commandment is eternal life. So what I say, I say as the Father told me.”
text.DRB: And I know that his commandment is life everlasting. The things therefore that I speak: as the Father said to me, so do I speak.

text.NRSV: Now before the festival of the Passover, Jesus knew that his hour had come to depart from this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the end.
text.Rotherham: Now, before the feast of the passover, Jesus, knowing that his hour had come, that he should remove out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own that were in the world, unto the end, loved them.
text.KJV: Now before the feast of the passover, when Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own which were in the world, he loved them unto the end.
text.NABRE: Before the feast of Passover, Jesus knew that his hour had come to pass from this world to the Father. He loved his own in the world and he loved them to the end.
text.DRB: And before the festival day of Pasch, Jesus knowing that his hour was come that he should pass out of this world to his Father: whereas he had loved his that were in the world, unto the end he loved them.

text.NRSV: The devil had already put it into the heart of Judas son of Simon Iscariot to betray him. And during supper
text.Rotherham: And, supper, being in progress, the adversary, having already thrust into the heart of Judas son of Simon Iscariot, that he should deliver him up,—
text.KJV: And supper being ended, the devil having now put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, to betray him;
text.NABRE: The devil had already induced Judas, son of Simon the Iscariot, to hand him over. So, during supper,
text.DRB: And when supper was done, whereas the devil now had put into the heart of Judas Iscariote the son of Simon, to betray him:

text.NRSV: Jesus, knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he had come from God and was going to God,
text.Rotherham: [Jesus] knowing that, all things, the Father had given unto him, into his hands, and that, from God, he had come, and, unto God, he was going,
text.KJV: Jesus knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he was come from God, and went to God;
text.NABRE: fully aware that the Father had put everything into his power and that he had come from God and was returning to God,
text.DRB: knowing that the Father gave him all things into his hands, & that he came from God, and goeth to God:

text.NRSV: got up from the table, took off his outer robe, and tied a towel around himself.
text.Rotherham: rouseth himself out of the supper, and layeth aside his garments, and taking a linen cloth, girded himself.
text.KJV: He riseth from supper, and laid aside his garments; and took a towel, and girded himself.
text.NABRE: he rose from supper and took off his outer garments. He took a towel and tied it around his waist.
text.DRB: he riseth from supper, and layeth aside his garments, and having taken a towel, girded himself.

text.NRSV: Then he poured water into a basin and began to wash the disciples’ feet and to wipe them with the towel that was tied around him.
text.Rotherham: Next, he poureth water into the wash-basin, and began to be washing the feet of the disciples, and to be wiping them with the linen cloth wherewith he was girded.
text.KJV: After that he poureth water into a bason, and began to wash the disciples’ feet, and to wipe them with the towel wherewith he was girded.
text.NABRE: Then he poured water into a basin and began to wash the disciples’ feet and dry them with the towel around his waist.
text.DRB: After that, he put water into a basin, and began to wash the feet of the Disciples, and to wipe them with the towel where with he was girded.

text.NRSV: He came to Simon Peter, who said to him, “Lord, are you going to wash my feet?”
text.Rotherham: So he cometh unto Simon Peter. He saith unto him—Lord, dost, thou, wash my feet?
text.KJV: Then cometh he to Simon Peter: and Peter saith unto him, Lord, dost thou wash my feet?
text.NABRE: He came to Simon Peter, who said to him, “Master, are you going to wash my feet?”
text.DRB: He cometh therefore to Simon Peter. And Peter saith to him; Lord: doest thou wash my feet?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “You do not know now what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—What, I, am doing, thou, knowest not, as yet; howbeit, thou shalt got to know, hereafter.
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto him, What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to him, “What I am doing, you do not understand now, but you will understand later.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered and said to him: That which I do, thou knowest not now, hereafter thou shalt know.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “You will never wash my feet.” Jesus answered, “Unless I wash you, you have no share with me.”
text.Rotherham: Peter saith unto him—In nowise shalt thou, ever, wash my feet. Jesus answered him—If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.
text.KJV: Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.
text.NABRE: Peter said to him, “You will never wash my feet.” Jesus answered him, “Unless I wash you, you will have no inheritance with me.”
text.DRB: Peter saith to him: Thou shalt not wash my feet for ever. Jesus answered him: If I wash thee not, thou shalt not have part with me.

text.NRSV: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, not my feet only but also my hands and my head!”
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter saith unto him—Lord! not my feet only, but my hands also, and my head.
text.KJV: Simon Peter saith unto him, Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head.
text.NABRE: Simon Peter said to him, “Master, then not only my feet, but my hands and head as well.”
text.DRB: Simon Peter saith to him: Lord, not only my feet, but also hands, and head.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “One who has bathed does not need to wash, except for the feet, but is entirely clean. And you are clean, though not all of you.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—He that hath bathed himself, hath no need [save as to the feet] to get washed; but is pure, as a whole. And, ye, are, pure, but not ye, all.
text.KJV: Jesus saith to him, He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “Whoever has bathed has no need except to have his feet washed, for he is clean all over; so you are clean, but not all.”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to him: He that is washed, needeth not but to wash his feet, but is clean wholly. And you are clean, but not all.

text.NRSV: For he knew who was to betray him; for this reason he said, “Not all of you are clean.”
text.Rotherham: For he know the man that was delivering him up; therefore, said he—Not ye all, are pure.
text.KJV: For he knew who should betray him; therefore said he, Ye are not all clean.
text.NABRE: For he knew who would betray him; for this reason, he said, “Not all of you are clean.”
text.DRB: For he knew who he was that would betray him. therefore he said: You are not clean all.

text.NRSV: After he had washed their feet, had put on his robe, and had returned to the table, he said to them, “Do you know what I have done to you?
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he had washed their feet, and taken his garments, and reclined, again said he unto them—Are ye taking note, what I have done unto you?
text.KJV: So after he had washed their feet, and had taken his garments, and was set down again, he said unto them, Know ye what I have done to you?
text.NABRE: So when he had washed their feet [and] put his garments back on and reclined at table again, he said to them, “Do you realize what I have done for you?
text.DRB: Therefore, after he had washed their feet, and taken his garments, being set down, again he said to them: Know you what I have done to you?

text.NRSV: You call me Teacher and Lord—and you are right, for that is what I am.
text.Rotherham: Ye, call me, The Teacher, and, The Lord,—and, well, say, for I am.
text.KJV: Ye call me Master and Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.
text.NABRE: You call me ‘teacher’ and ‘master,’ and rightly so, for indeed I am.
text.DRB: You call me, Master, and Lord: and you say well, for I am so.

text.NRSV: So if I, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another’s feet.
text.Rotherham: If then, I, have washed your feet,—[I] The Lord, and, The Teacher, ye also, ought to wash, one another’s, feet;
text.KJV: If I then, your Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another’s feet.
text.NABRE: If I, therefore, the master and teacher, have washed your feet, you ought to wash one another’s feet.
text.DRB: If then I have washed your feet, Lord and Master; you also ought to wash one another's feet.

text.NRSV: For I have set you an example, that you also should do as I have done to you.
text.Rotherham: For, an example, have I given you,—that, just as, I, did unto you, ye also, should be doing.
text.KJV: For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.
text.NABRE: I have given you a model to follow, so that as I have done for you, you should also do.
text.DRB: For I have given you an example, that as I have done to you, so you do also.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, servants are not greater than their master, nor are messengers greater than the one who sent them.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you—A servant is not greater than his lord, neither one sent forth greater than he that sent him.
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, no slave is greater than his master nor any messenger greater than the one who sent him.
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to you, a servant is not greater than his Lord, neither is an Apostle greater than he that sent him.

text.NRSV: If you know these things, you are blessed if you do them.
text.Rotherham: If, these things, ye know, happy, are ye, if ye be doing them.
text.KJV: If ye know these things, happy are ye if ye do them.
text.NABRE: If you understand this, blessed are you if you do it.
text.DRB: If you know these things, you shall be blessed if you do them.

text.NRSV: I am not speaking of all of you; I know whom I have chosen. But it is to fulfill the scripture, ‘The one who ate my bread has lifted his heel against me.’
text.Rotherham: Not, concerning you all, am I speaking,—for, I, know of whom I made choice; but…that, the Scripture, might be fulfilled: He that feedeth on my bread, hath lifted up against me, his heel.
text.KJV: I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me.
text.NABRE: I am not speaking of all of you. I know those whom I have chosen. But so that the scripture might be fulfilled, ‘The one who ate my food has raised his heel against me.’
text.DRB: Ispeak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen. But that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me shall lift up his heel against me.

text.NRSV: I tell you this now, before it occurs, so that when it does occur, you may believe that I am he.
text.Rotherham: Henceforth, I tell you, before it cometh to pass,—that ye may believe, whensoever it doth come to pass, that, I, am he.
text.KJV: Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he.
text.NABRE: From now on I am telling you before it happens, so that when it happens you may believe that I AM.
text.DRB: From this time I tell you, before it come to pass: that when it shall come to pass, you may believe, that I am he.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, whoever receives one whom I send receives me; and whoever receives me receives him who sent me.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you—He that receiveth whomsoever I shall send, receiveth, me; and, he that receiveth, me, receiveth him that sent me.
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, whoever receives the one I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.” Announcement of Judas’s Betrayal.
text.DRB: Amen, amen, I say to you, he that receiveth any that I send, receiveth me: & he that receiveth me, receiveth him that sent me.

text.NRSV: After saying this Jesus was troubled in spirit, and declared, “Very truly, I tell you, one of you will betray me.”
text.Rotherham: These things, having said, Jesus, was troubled in spirit, and bare witness, and said—Verily, verily, I say unto you—One from among you, will deliver me up.
text.KJV: When Jesus had thus said, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
text.NABRE: When he had said this, Jesus was deeply troubled and testified, “Amen, amen, I say to you, one of you will betray me.”
text.DRB: When Jesus had said these things, he was troubled in spirit: and he protested, and said: Amen, amen I say to you that one of you shall betray me.

text.NRSV: The disciples looked at one another, uncertain of whom he was speaking.
text.Rotherham: The disciples began to look one at another, being at a loss concerning whom he was speaking.
text.KJV: Then the disciples looked one on another, doubting of whom he spake.
text.NABRE: The disciples looked at one another, at a loss as to whom he meant.
text.DRB: The Disciples therefore looked one upon another, doubting of whom he spake.

text.NRSV: One of his disciples—the one whom Jesus loved—was reclining next to him;
text.Rotherham: One of Jesus’ disciples was reclining in his bosom, one whom Jesus loved:
text.KJV: Now there was leaning on Jesus’ bosom one of his disciples, whom Jesus loved.
text.NABRE: One of his disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was reclining at Jesus’ side.
text.DRB: There was therefore one of his Disciples leaning in the bosom of Jesus, he whom Jesus loved.

text.NRSV: Simon Peter therefore motioned to him to ask Jesus of whom he was speaking.
text.Rotherham: so Simon Peter beckoneth unto the same, and saith unto him—Say, Who is it? concerning whom he speaketh.
text.KJV: Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, that he should ask who it should be of whom he spake.
text.NABRE: So Simon Peter nodded to him to find out whom he meant.
text.DRB: Therefore Simon Peter beckoneth to him, and said to him: Who is it of whom he speaketh?

text.NRSV: So while reclining next to Jesus, he asked him, “Lord, who is it?”
text.Rotherham: He, falling back thus, upon the breast of Jesus, saith unto him—Lord! who is it?
text.KJV: He then lying on Jesus’ breast saith unto him, Lord, who is it?
text.NABRE: He leaned back against Jesus’ chest and said to him, “Master, who is it?”
text.DRB: He therefore leaning upon the breast of Jesus saith to him: Lord, who is he?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “It is the one to whom I give this piece of bread when I have dipped it in the dish.” So when he had dipped the piece of bread, he gave it to Judas son of Simon Iscariot.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, answereth—That one, it is, for whom, I, shall dip the morsel, and give unto him. So, dipping the morsel, he taketh and giveth it unto Judas, son of Simon Iscariot.
text.KJV: Jesus answered, He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it. And when he had dipped the sop, he gave it to Judas Iscariot, the son of Simon.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “It is the one to whom I hand the morsel after I have dipped it.” So he dipped the morsel and [took it and] handed it to Judas, son of Simon the Iscariot.
text.DRB: Jesus answered: He it is to whom I shall reach the dipped bread. And when he had dipped the bread, he gave it to Judas Iscariote Simon's son.

text.NRSV: After he received the piece of bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus said to him, “Do quickly what you are going to do.”
text.Rotherham: And, after the morsel, then, entered, Satan, into that man. Jesus, therefore, saith unto him—What thou art doing, do quickly!
text.KJV: And after the sop Satan entered into him. Then said Jesus unto him, That thou doest, do quickly.
text.NABRE: After he took the morsel, Satan entered him. So Jesus said to him, “What you are going to do, do quickly.”
text.DRB: And after the morsel, then Satan entered into him. And Jesus saith to him: That which thou doest, do it quickly.

text.NRSV: Now no one at the table knew why he said this to him.
text.Rotherham: But, as to this, none of them who were reclining with him knew respecting what, he said it to him.
text.KJV: Now no man at the table knew for what intent he spake this unto him.
text.NABRE: [Now] none of those reclining at table realized why he said this to him.
text.DRB: But no man knew of those that sat at table to what purpose he said this unto him.

text.NRSV: Some thought that, because Judas had the common purse, Jesus was telling him, “Buy what we need for the festival”; or, that he should give something to the poor.
text.Rotherham: For, some, were thinking, since Judas held the bag, that Jesus was saying to him—Buy the things of which we have, need, for the feast; or that, unto the destitute, he should give something.
text.KJV: For some of them thought, because Judas had the bag, that Jesus had said unto him, Buy those things that we have need of against the feast; or, that he should give something to the poor.
text.NABRE: Some thought that since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus had told him, “Buy what we need for the feast,” or to give something to the poor.
text.DRB: For certain thought, because Judas had the purse, that Jesus had said to him: Buy those things which are needful for us to the festival day: or that he should give something to the poor.

text.NRSV: So, after receiving the piece of bread, he immediately went out. And it was night.
text.Rotherham: So, he, taking the morsel, went out straightway;—and it was night.
text.KJV: He then having received the sop went immediately out: and it was night.
text.NABRE: So he took the morsel and left at once. And it was night.
text.DRB: He therefore having received the morsel, incontinent went forth. And it was night.

text.NRSV: When he had gone out, Jesus said, “Now the Son of Man has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he had gone out, Jesus saith—Just now, was, the Son of Man, glorified, and, God, was glorified in him;
text.KJV: Therefore, when he was gone out, Jesus said, Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him.
text.NABRE: The New Commandment. When he had left, Jesus said, “Now is the Son of Man glorified, and God is glorified in him.
text.DRB: When he therefore was gone forth, Jesus said: Now the Son of man is glorified, and God is glorified in him.

text.NRSV: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify him in himself and will glorify him at once.
text.Rotherham: And, God, will glorify him, in himself,—and, straightway, glorify him.
text.KJV: If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him.
text.NABRE: [If God is glorified in him,] God will also glorify him in himself, and he will glorify him at once.
text.DRB: If God be glorified in him, God also will glorify him in himself, and incontinent will he glorify him.

text.NRSV: Little children, I am with you only a little longer. You will look for me; and as I said to the Jews so now I say to you, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come.’
text.Rotherham: Dear children! Yet a little, am I, with you. Ye shall seek me, and, just as I said unto the Jews, Whither, I, go, ye, cannot come, unto you also, I say it, even now.
text.KJV: Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.
text.NABRE: My children, I will be with you only a little while longer. You will look for me, and as I told the Jews, ‘Where I go you cannot come,’ so now I say it to you.
text.DRB: Little children, yet a little while I am with you. You shall seek me, & as I said to the Jews, whither I go, you cannot come: to you also I say now.

text.NRSV: I give you a new commandment, that you love one another. Just as I have loved you, you also should love one another.
text.Rotherham: A new commandment, give I unto you,—That ye be loving one another: Just as I loved you, that, ye also, be loving one another:
text.KJV: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.
text.NABRE: I give you a new commandment: love one another. As I have loved you, so you also should love one another.
text.DRB: A new commandment I give to you, That you love one another; as I have loved you, that you also love one another.

text.NRSV: By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.”
text.Rotherham: By this, shall all men take knowledge, that, my disciples, ye are,—If ye have, love, one to another.
text.KJV: By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.
text.NABRE: This is how all will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.”
text.DRB: In this all men shall know that you are my Disciples, if you have love one to another.

text.NRSV: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “Where I am going, you cannot follow me now; but you will follow afterward.”
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter saith unto him—Lord! whither dost thou withdraw? Jesus answered—Whither I withdraw, thou canst not, now, follow me, but thou shalt follow, hereafter.
text.KJV: Simon Peter said unto him, Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered him, Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.
text.NABRE: Peter’s Denial Predicted. Simon Peter said to him, “Master, where are you going?” Jesus answered [him], “Where I am going, you cannot follow me now, though you will follow later.”
text.DRB: Simon Peter saith to him: Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered, Whither I go, thou canst not now follow me, but hereafter thou shalt follow.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “Lord, why can I not follow you now? I will lay down my life for you.”
text.Rotherham: Peter saith unto him—Lord! why, cannot I follow thee, even now? My life, in thy behalf, will I lay down.
text.KJV: Peter said unto him, Lord, why cannot I follow thee now? I will lay down my life for thy sake.
text.NABRE: Peter said to him, “Master, why can’t I follow you now? I will lay down my life for you.”
text.DRB: Peter saith to him: Why cannot I follow thee now? I will yield my life for thee.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Will you lay down your life for me? Very truly, I tell you, before the cock crows, you will have denied me three times.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answereth—Thy life, in my behalf, wilt thou lay down? Verily, verily, I say unto thee—In nowise shall a cock crow till thou hast denied me, thrice.
text.KJV: Jesus answered him, Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “Will you lay down your life for me? Amen, amen, I say to you, the cock will not crow before you deny me three times.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered him: Thy life wilt thou yield for me? Amen, amen I say to thee, the cock shall not crow, until thou deny me thrice.

text.NRSV: “Do not let your hearts be troubled. Believe in God, believe also in me.
text.Rotherham: Let not your heart be troubled: Believe on God, and, on me, believe.
text.KJV: Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
text.NABRE: “Do not let your hearts be troubled. You have faith in God; have faith also in me.
text.DRB: Let not your heart be troubled. You believe in God, believe in me also.

text.NRSV: In my Father’s house there are many dwelling places. If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you?
text.Rotherham: In the house of my Father, are, many dwellings; or else I would have told you, I go to prepare a place for you.
text.KJV: In my Father’s house are many mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.
text.NABRE: In my Father’s house there are many dwelling places. If there were not, would I have told you that I am going to prepare a place for you?
text.DRB: In my Father's house there be many mansions. If not, I would have told you, Because I go to prepare you a place.

text.NRSV: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and will take you to myself, so that where I am, there you may be also.
text.Rotherham: And, if I go, and prepare a place for you, again, am I coming, and will take you home unto—myself, that, where, I, am, ye also, may be.
text.KJV: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.
text.NABRE: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back again and take you to myself, so that where I am you also may be.
text.DRB: And if I go, and prepare you a place: I come again and will take you to myself, that where I am, you also may be.

text.NRSV: And you know the way to the place where I am going.”
text.Rotherham: And, whither, I, go, ye know, the way.
text.KJV: And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.
text.NABRE: Where [I] am going you know the way.”
text.DRB: And whither I go you know, and the way you know.

text.NRSV: Thomas said to him, “Lord, we do not know where you are going. How can we know the way?”
text.Rotherham: Thomas saith unto him—Lord! we know not whither thou goest: How know we, the way?
text.KJV: Thomas saith unto him, Lord, we know not whither thou goest; and how can we know the way?
text.NABRE: Thomas said to him, “Master, we do not know where you are going; how can we know the way?”
text.DRB: Thomas saith to him: Lord, we know not whither thou goest; and how can we know the way?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—I, am the way, and the truth, and the life: No one, cometh unto the Father, but through me.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
text.DRB: Jesus saith to him: I am the way, and the verity, and the life. No man cometh to the Father; but by me.

text.NRSV: If you know me, you will know my Father also. From now on you do know him and have seen him.”
text.Rotherham: If ye had been getting to know me, my Father also, had ye known: from henceforth, are ye getting to know him, and have seen him.
text.KJV: If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.
text.NABRE: If you know me, then you will also know my Father. From now on you do know him and have seen him.”
text.DRB: If you had known me, my Father also certes you had known: and from hence forth you shall know him, and you have seen him.

text.NRSV: Philip said to him, “Lord, show us the Father, and we will be satisfied.”
text.Rotherham: Philip saith unto him—Lord! show us the Father, and it sufficeth us.
text.KJV: Philip saith unto him, Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us.
text.NABRE: Philip said to him, “Master, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us.”
text.DRB: Philippe saith to him: Lord shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you all this time, Philip, and you still do not know me? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—So long a time as this, have I been, with you,—and thou hast not come to know me, Philip? He that hath seen me, hath seen the Father. How art, thou, saying, Show us the Father?
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto him, Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou then, Shew us the Father?
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you for so long a time and you still do not know me, Philip? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
text.DRB: Jesus saith to him: So long time I am with you; & have you not known me? Philippe, he that seeth me, seeth the Father also. How sayest thou, Shew us the Father?

text.NRSV: Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words that I say to you I do not speak on my own; but the Father who dwells in me does his works.
text.Rotherham: Believest thou not, that, I, am in the Father, and, the Father, is, in me? The things which I am saying unto you, from myself, I speak not; but, the Father, within me abiding, doeth his works.
text.KJV: Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? the words that I speak unto you I speak not of myself: but the Father that dwelleth in me, he doeth the works.
text.NABRE: Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words that I speak to you I do not speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me is doing his works.
text.DRB: Doest thou not believe that I am in the Father, & the Father in me? The words that I speak to you, of myself I speak not. But my Father that abideth in me, he doeth the works.

text.NRSV: Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me; but if you do not, then believe me because of the works themselves.
text.Rotherham: Believe me, That, I, am in the Father, and, the Father, in me;—or else, on account of the works themselves, believe ye.
text.KJV: Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father in me: or else believe me for the very works’ sake.
text.NABRE: Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or else, believe because of the works themselves.
text.DRB: Believe you not, that I am in the Father and the Father in me? Otherwise for the works themselves believe.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, the one who believes in me will also do the works that I do and, in fact, will do greater works than these, because I am going to the Father.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you—He that believeth on me, the works which, I, am doing, he also, shall do; and, greater than these, shall he do, because, I, unto the Father, am going,—
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do; because I go unto my Father.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, whoever believes in me will do the works that I do, and will do greater ones than these, because I am going to the Father.
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to you, he that believeth in me, the works that I do, he also shall do, and greater than these shall he do,

text.NRSV: I will do whatever you ask in my name, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
text.Rotherham: And because, whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, the same, will I do, that, the Father, may be glorified, in the Son:
text.KJV: And whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
text.NABRE: And whatever you ask in my name, I will do, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
text.DRB: because I go to the Father, & whatsoever you shall ask in my name, that will I do: that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

text.NRSV: If in my name you ask me for anything, I will do it.
text.Rotherham: If anything ye shall ask [me] in my name, the same, will I do.
text.KJV: If ye shall ask any thing in my name, I will do it.
text.NABRE: If you ask anything of me in my name, I will do it.
text.DRB: If you ask me any thing in my name, that will I do.

text.NRSV: “If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
text.Rotherham: If ye be loving me, my commandments, ye will keep;
text.KJV: If ye love me, keep my commandments.
text.NABRE: The Advocate. “If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
text.DRB: If you love me, keep my commandments.

text.NRSV: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Advocate, to be with you forever.
text.Rotherham: And, I, will request the Father, and, Another Advocate, will he give unto you, that he may be with you age-abidingly,
text.KJV: And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever;
text.NABRE: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Advocate to be with you always,
text.DRB: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Paraclete, that he may abide with you for ever,

text.NRSV: This is the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it neither sees him nor knows him. You know him, because he abides with you, and he will be in you.
text.Rotherham: The Spirit of truth,—which, the world, cannot receive, because it beholdeth it not, nor getteth to know it. But, ye, are getting to know it; because, with you, it abideth, and, in you, it is.
text.KJV: Even the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him: but ye know him; for he dwelleth with you, and shall be in you.
text.NABRE: the Spirit of truth, which the world cannot accept, because it neither sees nor knows it. But you know it, because it remains with you, and will be in you.
text.DRB: the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him, but you know him: because he shall abide with you, and shall be in you.

text.NRSV: “I will not leave you orphaned; I am coming to you.
text.Rotherham: I will not leave you bereft,—I am coming unto you.
text.KJV: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.
text.NABRE: I will not leave you orphans; I will come to you.
text.DRB: I will not leave you orphans: I will come to you.

text.NRSV: In a little while the world will no longer see me, but you will see me; because I live, you also will live.
text.Rotherham: Yet a little, and, the world, no longer beholdeth me; but, ye, behold me,—Because, I, live, ye also, shall live.
text.KJV: Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.
text.NABRE: In a little while the world will no longer see me, but you will see me, because I live and you will live.
text.DRB: Yet a little while: and the world seeth me no more. But you see me: because I live, and you shall live.

text.NRSV: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall ye get to know, That, I, am in my Father, and, ye, in me, and, I, in you.
text.KJV: At that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.
text.NABRE: On that day you will realize that I am in my Father and you are in me and I in you.
text.DRB: In that day you shall know that I am in my Father, and you in me; and I in you.

text.NRSV: They who have my commandments and keep them are those who love me; and those who love me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.”
text.Rotherham: He that hath my commandments and keepeth them, he, it is that loveth me; and, he that loveth me, shall be loved by my Father, and, I, will love him, and will manifest, myself, unto him.
text.KJV: He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.
text.NABRE: Whoever has my commandments and observes them is the one who loves me. And whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him and reveal myself to him.”
text.DRB: He that hath my commandments, & keepeth them: he it is that loveth me. And he that loveth me, shall be loved of my Father: and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.

text.NRSV: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, “Lord, how is it that you will reveal yourself to us, and not to the world?”
text.Rotherham: Judas, not the Iscariot, saith unto him—Lord! what hath happened, that, unto us, thou art about to manifest thyself, and, not unto the world?
text.KJV: Judas saith unto him, not Iscariot, Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?
text.NABRE: Judas, not the Iscariot, said to him, “Master, [then] what happened that you will reveal yourself to us and not to the world?”
text.DRB: Judas saith to him, not that Iscariote: Lord, what is done, that thou wilt manifest thyself to us, and not to the world?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “Those who love me will keep my word, and my Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—If any man be loving me, my word, he will keep, and, my Father, will love him,—and, unto him, will we come, and, an abode with him, will we make.
text.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto him, If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered and said to him, “Whoever loves me will keep my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our dwelling with him.
text.DRB: Jesus answered, and said to him: If any love me, he will keep my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him, and will make abode with him.

text.NRSV: Whoever does not love me does not keep my words; and the word that you hear is not mine, but is from the Father who sent me.
text.Rotherham: He that loveth me not, doth not keep, my word;—and, the word which ye hear, is not mine, but, the Father’s who sent me.
text.KJV: He that loveth me not keepeth not my sayings: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father’s which sent me.
text.NABRE: Whoever does not love me does not keep my words; yet the word you hear is not mine but that of the Father who sent me.
text.DRB: He that loveth me not, keepeth not my words. And the word which you have heard, is not mine; but his that sent me, the Father's.

text.NRSV: “I have said these things to you while I am still with you.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, With you abiding;
text.KJV: These things have I spoken unto you, being yet present with you.
text.NABRE: “I have told you this while I am with you.
text.DRB: These things have I spoken to you abiding with you.

text.NRSV: But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything, and remind you of all that I have said to you.
text.Rotherham: But, the Advocate, The Holy Spirit, which the Father will send in my name, He, will teach you all things, and will put you in mind, of all things which, I, told you.
text.KJV: But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.
text.NABRE: The Advocate, the holy Spirit that the Father will send in my name—he will teach you everything and remind you of all that [I] told you.
text.DRB: But the Paraclete the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, & suggest unto you all things whatsoever I shall say to you.

text.NRSV: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled, and do not let them be afraid.
text.Rotherham: Peace, I leave with you, My own peace, give, I, unto you,—Not as, the world, giveth, give, I, unto you:—Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
text.KJV: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
text.NABRE: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give it to you. Do not let your hearts be troubled or afraid.
text.DRB: Peace I leave to you, my peace I give to you; not as the world giveth, do I give to you. Let not your heart be troubled, nor fear.

text.NRSV: You heard me say to you, ‘I am going away, and I am coming to you.’ If you loved me, you would rejoice that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I.
text.Rotherham: Ye heard that, I, said unto you—I go my way, and I come unto you,—Had ye loved me, ye would have rejoiced, that I am going unto the Father, for, the Father, is, greater than I.
text.KJV: Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come again unto you. If ye loved me, ye would rejoice, because I said, I go unto the Father: for my Father is greater than I.
text.NABRE: You heard me tell you, ‘I am going away and I will come back to you.’ If you loved me, you would rejoice that I am going to the Father; for the Father is greater than I.
text.DRB: You have heard that I said to you, I go, and I come to you. If you loved me, you would be glad verily, that I go to the Father: because the Father is greater than I.

text.NRSV: And now I have told you this before it occurs, so that when it does occur, you may believe.
text.Rotherham: But, now, have I told you, before it cometh to pass, that, whensoever it shall come to pass, ye may believe.
text.KJV: And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe.
text.NABRE: And now I have told you this before it happens, so that when it happens you may believe.
text.DRB: And now I have told you before it come to pass: that when it shall come to pass, you may believe.

text.NRSV: I will no longer talk much with you, for the ruler of this world is coming. He has no power over me;
text.Rotherham: No longer, many things, will I speak with you; for, the world’s ruler, is coming, and, in me, hath, nothing,—
text.KJV: Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me.
text.NABRE: I will no longer speak much with you, for the ruler of the world is coming. He has no power over me,
text.DRB: Now I will not speak many things with you. For the Prince of this world cometh, and in me he hath not any thing.

text.NRSV: but I do as the Father has commanded me, so that the world may know that I love the Father. Rise, let us be on our way.
text.Rotherham: But, that the world may get to know that I love the Father, and just as the Father hath given me commandment, so, I do. Be rousing yourselves! let us be leading on from hence.
text.KJV: But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.
text.NABRE: but the world must know that I love the Father and that I do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up, let us go.
text.DRB: But that the world may know that I love the Father: and as the Father hath given me commandment, so do I: Arise, let us go hence.

text.NRSV: “I am the true vine, and my Father is the vinegrower.
text.Rotherham: I, am the real vine, and, my Father, is, the husbandman.
text.KJV: I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.
text.NABRE: “I am the true vine, and my Father is the vine grower.
text.DRB: I AM the true vine; and my Father is the husband-man.

text.NRSV: He removes every branch in me that bears no fruit. Every branch that bears fruit he prunes to make it bear more fruit.
text.Rotherham: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit, He taketh it away; and, every one that beareth, fruit, He pruneth it, that, more fruit, it may bear.
text.KJV: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.
text.NABRE: He takes away every branch in me that does not bear fruit, and every one that does he prunes so that it bears more fruit.
text.DRB: Every branch in me, not bearing fruit, he will take it away: & every one that beareth fruit, he will purge it, that it may bring more fruit.

text.NRSV: You have already been cleansed by the word that I have spoken to you.
text.Rotherham: Already, ye, are, pure, because of the word which I have spoken unto you:
text.KJV: Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.
text.NABRE: You are already pruned because of the word that I spoke to you.
text.DRB: Now you are clean for the word which I have spoken to you.

text.NRSV: Abide in me as I abide in you. Just as the branch cannot bear fruit by itself unless it abides in the vine, neither can you unless you abide in me.
text.Rotherham: Abide in me, and, I, in you. Just as, the branch, cannot be bearing fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine, so, neither, ye, except, in me, ye abide.
text.KJV: Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.
text.NABRE: Remain in me, as I remain in you. Just as a branch cannot bear fruit on its own unless it remains on the vine, so neither can you unless you remain in me.
text.DRB: Abide in me: and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, unless it abide in the vine, so you neither unless you abide in me.

text.NRSV: I am the vine, you are the branches. Those who abide in me and I in them bear much fruit, because apart from me you can do nothing.
text.Rotherham: I, am the vine: Ye, are the branches. He that abideth in me and, I, in him, the same, beareth much fruit; because, apart from me, ye can bring forth, nothing.
text.KJV: I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.
text.NABRE: I am the vine, you are the branches. Whoever remains in me and I in him will bear much fruit, because without me you can do nothing.
text.DRB: I am the vine: you the branches. He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same beareth much fruit: for without me you can do nothing.

text.NRSV: Whoever does not abide in me is thrown away like a branch and withers; such branches are gathered, thrown into the fire, and burned.
text.Rotherham: If one abide not me, he is cast out as the branch, and withered, and they gather them,—and, into fire, they cast them, and they are burned.
text.KJV: If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.
text.NABRE: Anyone who does not remain in me will be thrown out like a branch and wither; people will gather them and throw them into a fire and they will be burned.
text.DRB: If any abide not in me: he shall be cast forth as the branch, and shall wither, and they shall gather him up, and cast him into the fire, and he burneth.

text.NRSV: If you abide in me, and my words abide in you, ask for whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
text.Rotherham: If ye abide in me, and, my sayings, in you, abide, whatsoever ye may be desiring, ask! and it shall be brought to pass for you.
text.KJV: If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.
text.NABRE: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
text.DRB: If you abide in me, & my words abide in you: you shall ask what thing soever you will, and it shall be done to you.

text.NRSV: My Father is glorified by this, that you bear much fruit and become my disciples.
text.Rotherham: Herein, was my Father glorified, that, much fruit, ye should bear, and become my disciples.
text.KJV: Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.
text.NABRE: By this is my Father glorified, that you bear much fruit and become my disciples.
text.DRB: In this my Father is glorified; that you bring very much fruit, and become my Disciples.

text.NRSV: As the Father has loved me, so I have loved you; abide in my love.
text.Rotherham: Just as the Father loved me, I also, loved you: Abide ye in my love.
text.KJV: As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.
text.NABRE: As the Father loves me, so I also love you. Remain in my love.
text.DRB: As my Father hath loved me, I also have loved you. Abide in my love.

text.NRSV: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, just as I have kept my Father’s commandments and abide in his love.
text.Rotherham: If, my commandments, ye keep, ye shall abide in my love,—just as, I, the Father’s commandments, have kept, and abide in his love.
text.KJV: If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and abide in his love.
text.NABRE: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I have kept my Father’s commandments and remain in his love.
text.DRB: If you keep my precepts, you shall abide in my love: as I also have kept my Father's precepts, and do abide in his love.

text.NRSV: I have said these things to you so that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be complete.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, that, my own joy, in you, may be, and, your joy, may be made full.
text.KJV: These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and that your joy might be full.
text.NABRE: “I have told you this so that my joy may be in you and your joy may be complete.
text.DRB: These things I have spoken to you, that my joy may be in you, and your joy may be filled.

text.NRSV: “This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
text.Rotherham: This, is my own commandment, That ye be loving one another, just as I loved you.
text.KJV: This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you.
text.NABRE: This is my commandment: love one another as I love you.
text.DRB: This is my precept, that you love one another, as I have loved you.

text.NRSV: No one has greater love than this, to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
text.Rotherham: Greater love than this, hath, no one, That, his life, one should lay down in behalf of his friends.
text.KJV: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
text.NABRE: No one has greater love than this, to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
text.DRB: Greater love than this no man hath, that a man yield his life for his friends.

text.NRSV: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are, friends of mine, if ye be doing that which, I, am commanding you.
text.KJV: Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you.
text.NABRE: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
text.DRB: You are my friends, if you do the things that I command you.

text.NRSV: I do not call you servants any longer, because the servant does not know what the master is doing; but I have called you friends, because I have made known to you everything that I have heard from my Father.
text.Rotherham: No longer, do I call you, servants, for, the servant, knoweth not what, his lord, is doing. But, you, I have called, friends, because, all things which I heard from my Father, made I known unto you.
text.KJV: Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you.
