text.NABRE: I no longer call you slaves, because a slave does not know what his master is doing. I have called you friends, because I have told you everything I have heard from my Father.
text.DRB: Now I call you not servants: for the servant knoweth not what his Lord doeth. But you I have called friends; because all things whatsoever I heard of my Father, I have notified unto you.

text.NRSV: You did not choose me but I chose you. And I appointed you to go and bear fruit, fruit that will last, so that the Father will give you whatever you ask him in my name.
text.Rotherham: Not, ye, chose, me, but, I, chose you, and placed you, that ye should go your way and bear, fruit,—and, your fruit, should abide: that, whatsoever ye should ask the Father in my name, he might give unto you.
text.KJV: Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and that your fruit should remain: that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my name, he may give it you.
text.NABRE: It was not you who chose me, but I who chose you and appointed you to go and bear fruit that will remain, so that whatever you ask the Father in my name he may give you.
text.DRB: You chose not me, but I chose you; and have appointed you: that you go, & bring fruit; and your fruit abide: that whatsoever you ask the Father in my name, he may give it you.

text.NRSV: I am giving you these commands so that you may love one another.
text.Rotherham: These things, I command you, That ye be loving one another.
text.KJV: These things I command you, that ye love one another.
text.NABRE: This I command you: love one another. The World’s Hatred.
text.DRB: These things I command you, that you love one another.

text.NRSV: “If the world hates you, be aware that it hated me before it hated you.
text.Rotherham: If, the world, is hating, you, ye are getting to know that, me, before you, it hath hated.
text.KJV: If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you.
text.NABRE: “If the world hates you, realize that it hated me first.
text.DRB: If the world hate you, know ye that it hath hated me before you.

text.NRSV: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. Because you do not belong to the world, but I have chosen you out of the world—therefore the world hates you.
text.Rotherham: If, of the world, ye had been, the world, of its own, had been fond; but, because, of the world, ye are not, on the contrary, I, chose you out of the world, therefore, the world, doth hate you.
text.KJV: If ye were of the world, the world would love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.
text.NABRE: If you belonged to the world, the world would love its own; but because you do not belong to the world, and I have chosen you out of the world, the world hates you.
text.DRB: If you had been of the world, the world would love his own. But because you are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.

text.NRSV: Remember the word that I said to you, ‘Servants are not greater than their master.’ If they persecuted me, they will persecute you; if they kept my word, they will keep yours also.
text.Rotherham: Remember the word which, I, spake unto you: A servant is not greater than his lord. If, me, they persecuted, you too, will they persecute,—If, my word, they kept, your own also, will they keep.
text.KJV: Remember the word that I said unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep yours also.
text.NABRE: Remember the word I spoke to you, ‘No slave is greater than his master.’ If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, they will also keep yours.
text.DRB: Remember my word that I said to you: The servant is not greater than his Master. If they have persecuted me, you also will they persecute. If they have kept my word, yours also will they keep.

text.NRSV: But they will do all these things to you on account of my name, because they do not know him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: But, all these things, will they do unto you, on account of my name, because they know not him that sent me.
text.KJV: But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me.
text.NABRE: And they will do all these things to you on account of my name, because they do not know the one who sent me.
text.DRB: But all these things they will do to you for my name sake; because they know not him that sent me.

text.NRSV: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.
text.Rotherham: Had I not come and spoken unto them, Sin, had they none; but, now, have they no, excuse, for their sin.
text.KJV: If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloke for their sin.
text.NABRE: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would have no sin; but as it is they have no excuse for their sin.
text.DRB: If I had not come, and spoken to them, they should not have sin: but now they have no excuse of their sin.

text.NRSV: Whoever hates me hates my Father also.
text.Rotherham: He that hateth me, hateth, my Father also.
text.KJV: He that hateth me hateth my Father also.
text.NABRE: Whoever hates me also hates my Father.
text.DRB: He that hateth me, hateth my Father also.

text.NRSV: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not have sin. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
text.Rotherham: Had I not done among them, the works, which, no other, had done, sin, had they none; but, now, have they, both seen and hated both me and my Father.
text.KJV: If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father.
text.NABRE: If I had not done works among them that no one else ever did, they would not have sin; but as it is, they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
text.DRB: If I had not done among them works that no other man hath done, they should not have sin: but now both they have seen, and they do hate both me and my Father.

text.NRSV: It was to fulfill the word that is written in their law, ‘They hated me without a cause.’
text.Rotherham: But…that the word which, in their law, is written, might be fulfilled—They hated me without cause.
text.KJV: But this cometh to pass, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.
text.NABRE: But in order that the word written in their law might be fulfilled, ‘They hated me without cause.’
text.DRB: But that the word may be fulfilled, which is written in their law: That they hated me gratis.

text.NRSV: “When the Advocate comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who comes from the Father, he will testify on my behalf.
text.Rotherham: Whensoever the Advocate shall come, Whom, I, will send unto you from the Father, The Spirit of truth, which, from the Father, cometh forth, He, will bear witness concerning me;
text.KJV: But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall testify of me:
text.NABRE: “When the Advocate comes whom I will send you from the Father, the Spirit of truth that proceeds from the Father, he will testify to me.
text.DRB: But when the Paraclete cometh whom I will send you from the Father, the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall give testimony of me:

text.NRSV: You also are to testify because you have been with me from the beginning.
text.Rotherham: And do, ye also, bear witness, because, from the beginning, ye are, with me,
text.KJV: And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.
text.NABRE: And you also testify, because you have been with me from the beginning.
text.DRB: and you shall give testimony, because you are with me from the beginning.

text.NRSV: “I have said these things to you to keep you from stumbling.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, that ye may not be caused to stumble:
text.KJV: These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.
text.NABRE: “I have told you this so that you may not fall away.
text.DRB: These things have I spoken to you, that you be not scandalized.

text.NRSV: They will put you out of the synagogues. Indeed, an hour is coming when those who kill you will think that by doing so they are offering worship to God.
text.Rotherham: Excommunicants from the synagogue, will they make you; Nay! there cometh an hour, that, every one who killeth you, shall think to be rendering, divine service, unto God!
text.KJV: They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.
text.NABRE: They will expel you from the synagogues; in fact, the hour is coming when everyone who kills you will think he is offering worship to God.
text.DRB: Out of the Synagogues they will cast you: but the hour cometh, that every one which killeth you, shall think that he doeth service to God.

text.NRSV: And they will do this because they have not known the Father or me.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, will they do, because they got to know, neither the Father nor me.
text.KJV: And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me.
text.NABRE: They will do this because they have not known either the Father or me.
text.DRB: And these things they will do to you, because they have not known the Father, nor me.

text.NRSV: But I have said these things to you so that when their hour comes you may remember that I told you about them. “I did not say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
text.Rotherham: But, these things, have I told you,—That, whensoever their hour shall come, ye may remember, that, thereof, I told you. These things, however, I told you not, from the beginning, because I was, with you;
text.KJV: But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.
text.NABRE: I have told you this so that when their hour comes you may remember that I told you. Jesus’ Departure; Coming of the Advocate. “I did not tell you this from the beginning, because I was with you.
text.DRB: But these things I have spoken to you: that when the hour shall come, you may remember them, that I told you.

text.NRSV: But now I am going to him who sent me; yet none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’
text.Rotherham: But, now, I go my way unto him that sent me, and, not one from among you, questioneth me—Whither goest thou?
text.KJV: But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?
text.NABRE: But now I am going to the one who sent me, and not one of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’
text.DRB: But I told you not these things from the beginning, because I was with you. And now I go to him that sent me, and none of you asketh me: Whither goest thou?

text.NRSV: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your hearts.
text.Rotherham: But, because, these things, I have told you, sorrow, hath filled your heart.
text.KJV: But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.
text.NABRE: But because I told you this, grief has filled your hearts.
text.DRB: But because I have spoken these things to you, sorrow hath filled your heart.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless I tell you the truth: it is to your advantage that I go away, for if I do not go away, the Advocate will not come to you; but if I go, I will send him to you.
text.Rotherham: But, I, the truth, am telling you—It is profitable for you, that, I, depart; for, if I should not depart, The Advocate, would in nowise come unto you, but, if I go, I will send him unto you.
text.KJV: Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you.
text.NABRE: But I tell you the truth, it is better for you that I go. For if I do not go, the Advocate will not come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
text.DRB: But I tell you the truth, it is expedient for you that I go. For if I go not, the Paraclete shall not come to you: but if I go, I will send him to you.

text.NRSV: And when he comes, he will prove the world wrong about sin and righteousness and judgment:
text.Rotherham: And, having come, He, will reprove the world—Concerning sin, and concerning righteousness, and concerning judgment:
text.KJV: And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:
text.NABRE: And when he comes he will convict the world in regard to sin and righteousness and condemnation:
text.DRB: And when he is come, he shall argue the world of sin, and of justice, and of judgement.

text.NRSV: about sin, because they do not believe in me;
text.Rotherham: Concerning sin, indeed, because they are not believing on me;
text.KJV: Of sin, because they believe not on me;
text.NABRE: sin, because they do not believe in me;
text.DRB: Of sin: because they believed not in me.

text.NRSV: about righteousness, because I am going to the Father and you will see me no longer;
text.Rotherham: But, concerning righteousness, because, unto the Father, I go my way, and, no longer, do ye behold me;
text.KJV: Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more;
text.NABRE: righteousness, because I am going to the Father and you will no longer see me;
text.DRB: But of justice: because I go to the Father; and now you shall not see me.

text.NRSV: about judgment, because the ruler of this world has been condemned.
text.Rotherham: And, concerning judgment, because, the ruler of this world, hath been judged.
text.KJV: Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.
text.NABRE: condemnation, because the ruler of this world has been condemned.
text.DRB: And of judgement: because the Prince of this world is now judged.

text.NRSV: “I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
text.Rotherham: Yet many things, have I, unto you, to be saying,—but ye cannot bear them, just now;
text.KJV: I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.
text.NABRE: “I have much more to tell you, but you cannot bear it now.
text.DRB: Yet many things I have to say to you: but you cannot bear them now.

text.NRSV: When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all the truth; for he will not speak on his own, but will speak whatever he hears, and he will declare to you the things that are to come.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, as soon as, he, hath come—The Spirit of truth, he will guide you into all truth; for he will not speak from himself, but, whatsoever he heareth, he will speak, and, the coming things, will he announce unto you.
text.KJV: Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come.
text.NABRE: But when he comes, the Spirit of truth, he will guide you to all truth. He will not speak on his own, but he will speak what he hears, and will declare to you the things that are coming.
text.DRB: But when he, the Spirit of truth, cometh, he shall teach you all truth. For he shall not speak of himself; but what things soever he shall hear, he shall speak: and the things that are to come he shall shew you.

text.NRSV: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
text.Rotherham: He, shall glorify me; for, of mine, shall he receive, and announce unto you.
text.KJV: He shall glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew it unto you.
text.NABRE: He will glorify me, because he will take from what is mine and declare it to you.
text.DRB: He shall glorify me; because he shall receive of mine, and shall shew to you.

text.NRSV: All that the Father has is mine. For this reason I said that he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
text.Rotherham: All things, whatsoever the Father hath, are, my own; therefore, said I—Of mine, shall he receive, and announce unto you.
text.KJV: All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew it unto you.
text.NABRE: Everything that the Father has is mine; for this reason I told you that he will take from what is mine and declare it to you.
text.DRB: All things whatsoever the Father hath, be mine. Therefore I said, that he shall receive of mine, and shall shew to you.

text.NRSV: “A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me.”
text.Rotherham: A little while, and, no longer, ye behold me; and, again a little while, and ye shall see me.
text.KJV: A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me, because I go to the Father.
text.NABRE: “A little while and you will no longer see me, and again a little while later and you will see me.”
text.DRB: A little while, and now you shall not see me; & again a little while, & you shall see me: because I go to the Father.

text.NRSV: Then some of his disciples said to one another, “What does he mean by saying to us, ‘A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’; and ‘Because I am going to the Father’?”
text.Rotherham: Some of his disciples, therefore, said one to another—What is this which he is saying to us:—A little while, and ye behold me not, and, again a little while, and ye shall see me; and—Because I go my way unto the Father?
text.KJV: Then said some of his disciples among themselves, What is this that he saith unto us, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me: and, Because I go to the Father?
text.NABRE: So some of his disciples said to one another, “What does this mean that he is saying to us, ‘A little while and you will not see me, and again a little while and you will see me,’ and ‘Because I am going to the Father’?”
text.DRB: Some therefore of his Disciples said one to another: What is this that he saith to us: A little while, and you shall not see me; and again a little while and you shall see me, and, because I go to the Father?

text.NRSV: They said, “What does he mean by this ‘a little while’? We do not know what he is talking about.”
text.Rotherham: They were saying, therefore—What is this which he saith:—A little while? We know not [what he is saying].
text.KJV: They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A little while? we cannot tell what he saith.
text.NABRE: So they said, “What is this ‘little while’ [of which he speaks]? We do not know what he means.”
text.DRB: They said therefore: What is this that he saith, A little while? we know not what he speaketh.

text.NRSV: Jesus knew that they wanted to ask him, so he said to them, “Are you discussing among yourselves what I meant when I said, ‘A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’?
text.Rotherham: Jesus took note, that they were wishing to question him, and said unto them—Concerning this, are ye enquiring one with another,—because I said:—A little while, and ye behold me not, and, again, a little while, and ye shall see me?
text.KJV: Now Jesus knew that they were desirous to ask him, and said unto them, Do ye enquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me?
text.NABRE: Jesus knew that they wanted to ask him, so he said to them, “Are you discussing with one another what I said, ‘A little while and you will not see me, and again a little while and you will see me’?
text.DRB: And Jesus knew, that they would ask him; & he said to them: Of this you do question among yourselves, because I said to you: A little while, and you shall not see me; and again a little while, and you shall see me.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, you will weep and mourn, but the world will rejoice; you will have pain, but your pain will turn into joy.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you—Ye, shall weep and lament, but, the world, shall rejoice: Ye, shall be grieved, but, your grief, into joy, shall be turned.
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, you will weep and mourn, while the world rejoices; you will grieve, but your grief will become joy.
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to you, that you shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and you shall be made sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.

text.NRSV: When a woman is in labor, she has pain, because her hour has come. But when her child is born, she no longer remembers the anguish because of the joy of having brought a human being into the world.
text.Rotherham: A woman, as soon as she is about to bring forth, hath, grief, because her hour hath come; but, as soon as she hath given birth to the child, no longer, remembereth she the anguish, by reason of the joy, that a human being into the world hath been born.
text.KJV: A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy that a man is born into the world.
text.NABRE: When a woman is in labor, she is in anguish because her hour has arrived; but when she has given birth to a child, she no longer remembers the pain because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
text.DRB: Awoman when she travaileth, hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but when she hath brought forth the child, now she remembereth not the anguish for joy, that a man is born into the world.

text.NRSV: So you have pain now; but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy from you.
text.Rotherham: And, ye, therefore, now, indeed have grief; but, again, will I see you, and your heart shall rejoice,—and, your joy, no one, shall force from you.
text.KJV: And ye now therefore have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no man taketh from you.
text.NABRE: So you also are now in anguish. But I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy away from you.
text.DRB: And you therefore, now indeed you have sorrow, but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice; and your joy no man shall take from you.

text.NRSV: On that day you will ask nothing of me. Very truly, I tell you, if you ask anything of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
text.Rotherham: And, in that day, shall ye request me, nothing:—Verily, verily, I say unto you—Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father, He will give you, in my name.
text.KJV: And in that day ye shall ask me nothing. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give it you.
text.NABRE: On that day you will not question me about anything. Amen, amen, I say to you, whatever you ask the Father in my name he will give you.
text.DRB: And in that day me you shall not ask any thing. Amen, amen I say to you, if you ask the Father any thing in my name, he will give it you.

text.NRSV: Until now you have not asked for anything in my name. Ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be complete.
text.Rotherham: Until even now, ye have asked nothing in my name: Be asking, and ye shall receive,—that, your joy, may be made full.
text.KJV: Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name: ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.
text.NABRE: Until now you have not asked anything in my name; ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be complete.
text.DRB: Until now you have not asked any thing in my name. Ask and you shall receive; that your joy may be full.

text.NRSV: “I have said these things to you in figures of speech. The hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures, but will tell you plainly of the Father.
text.Rotherham: These things, in similitudes, have I spoken unto you: There cometh an hour, when, no longer in similitudes, will I speak unto you, but, openly, concerning the Father, will I tell you.
text.KJV: These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.
text.NABRE: “I have told you this in figures of speech. The hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures but I will tell you clearly about the Father.
text.DRB: These things in proverbs I have spoken to you. The hour cometh when in proverbs I will no more speak to you, but plainly of the Father I will shew you.

text.NRSV: On that day you will ask in my name. I do not say to you that I will ask the Father on your behalf;
text.Rotherham: In that day, In my name, shall ye ask:—and I say not that, I, will request the Father for you;
text.KJV: At that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you:
text.NABRE: On that day you will ask in my name, and I do not tell you that I will ask the Father for you.
text.DRB: In that day you shall ask in my name: and I say not to you, that I will ask the Father for you.

text.NRSV: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
text.Rotherham: For, the Father himself, dearly loveth you, because, ye, have dearly loved me, and believed that, I, from the Father, came forth:—
text.KJV: For the Father himself loveth you, because ye have loved me, and have believed that I came out from God.
text.NABRE: For the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have come to believe that I came from God.
text.DRB: For the Father himself loveth you, because you have loved me, and have believed that I came forth from God.

text.NRSV: I came from the Father and have come into the world; again, I am leaving the world and am going to the Father.”
text.Rotherham: I came forth out of the Father, and have come into the world,—Again, I leave the world, and go, unto the Father.
text.KJV: I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.
text.NABRE: I came from the Father and have come into the world. Now I am leaving the world and going back to the Father.”
text.DRB: I came forth from the Father, and came into the world: again I leave the world, and I go to the Father.

text.NRSV: His disciples said, “Yes, now you are speaking plainly, not in any figure of speech!
text.Rotherham: His disciples say—See! now, openly, art thou speaking, and, not a single similitude, art thou using:
text.KJV: His disciples said unto him, Lo, now speakest thou plainly, and speakest no proverb.
text.NABRE: His disciples said, “Now you are talking plainly, and not in any figure of speech.
text.DRB: His Disciples say to him: Behold now thou speakest plainly, and sayest no proverb.

text.NRSV: Now we know that you know all things, and do not need to have anyone question you; by this we believe that you came from God.”
text.Rotherham: Now, we know, that thou knowest all things, and hast, no need, that one be questioning thee. Hereby, do we believe, that, from God, thou camest forth.
text.KJV: Now are we sure that thou knowest all things, and needest not that any man should ask thee: by this we believe that thou camest forth from God.
text.NABRE: Now we realize that you know everything and that you do not need to have anyone question you. Because of this we believe that you came from God.”
text.DRB: Now we know that thou knowest all things, and thou needest not that any man ask thee. In this we believe that thou camest forth from God.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—As yet, ye believe:
text.KJV: Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “Do you believe now?
text.DRB: Jesus answered them: Now do you believe?

text.NRSV: The hour is coming, indeed it has come, when you will be scattered, each one to his home, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone because the Father is with me.
text.Rotherham: Lo! there cometh an hour, and hath come, that ye should be scattered, each, unto his own home; and, me, alone, should leave;—And yet I am not, alone, but, the Father, is, with me!
text.KJV: Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
text.NABRE: Behold, the hour is coming and has arrived when each of you will be scattered to his own home and you will leave me alone. But I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
text.DRB: Behold the hour cometh, and it is now come, that you shall be scattered every man into his own, and me you shall leave alone: and I am not alone because the Father is with me.

text.NRSV: I have said this to you, so that in me you may have peace. In the world you face persecution. But take courage; I have conquered the world!”
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, that, in me, ye may have, peace: In the world, ye have, tribulation; but be taking courage,—I, have overcome the world.
text.KJV: These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
text.NABRE: I have told you this so that you might have peace in me. In the world you will have trouble, but take courage, I have conquered the world.”
text.DRB: These things I have spoken to you, that in me you may have peace. In the world you shall have distress, but have confidence, I have overcome the world.

text.NRSV: After Jesus had spoken these words, he looked up to heaven and said, “Father, the hour has come; glorify your Son so that the Son may glorify you,
text.Rotherham: These things, spake Jesus, and, lifting up his eyes unto heaven, said:—Father! The hour is come! Glorify thy Son, that, the Son, may glorify, thee,—
text.KJV: These words spake Jesus, and lifted up his eyes to heaven, and said, Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that thy Son also may glorify thee:
text.NABRE: When Jesus had said this, he raised his eyes to heaven and said, “Father, the hour has come. Give glory to your son, so that your son may glorify you,
text.DRB: These things spake Jesus: and lifting up his eyes into Heaven, he said: Father, the hour is come, glorify thy Son, that thy Son may glorify thee.

text.NRSV: since you have given him authority over all people, to give eternal life to all whom you have given him.
text.Rotherham: Even as thou gavest him authority over all flesh, that, as touching whatsoever thou hast given him, he might give unto them, life age-abiding.
text.KJV: As thou hast given him power over all flesh, that he should give eternal life to as many as thou hast given him.
text.NABRE: just as you gave him authority over all people, so that he may give eternal life to all you gave him.
text.DRB: As thou hast given him power over all flesh, that all which thou hast given him, to them he may give life everlasting.

text.NRSV: And this is eternal life, that they may know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the age-abiding life, That they get to know thee, the only real God, and him whom thou didst send, Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent.
text.NABRE: Now this is eternal life, that they should know you, the only true God, and the one whom you sent, Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: And this is life everlasting that they know thee, the only true God, and whom thou hast sent Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: I glorified you on earth by finishing the work that you gave me to do.
text.Rotherham: I, glorified thee on the earth, The work, finishing, which thou hast given me that I should do.
text.KJV: I have glorified thee on the earth: I have finished the work which thou gavest me to do.
text.NABRE: I glorified you on earth by accomplishing the work that you gave me to do.
text.DRB: I have glorified thee upon the earth: I have consummated the work which thou gavest me to do:

text.NRSV: So now, Father, glorify me in your own presence with the glory that I had in your presence before the world existed.
text.Rotherham: And, now, glorify me—thou, Father! with thyself, with the glory which I had, before the world’s existence, with thee.
text.KJV: And now, O Father, glorify thou me with thine own self with the glory which I had with thee before the world was.
text.NABRE: Now glorify me, Father, with you, with the glory that I had with you before the world began.
text.DRB: and now glorify thou me O Father with thyself, with the glory which I had before the world was, with thee.

text.NRSV: “I have made your name known to those whom you gave me from the world. They were yours, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
text.Rotherham: I manifested thy name, unto the men whom thou gavest me out of the world: Thine, they were, and, to me, thou gavest them, and, thy word, have they kept:
text.KJV: I have manifested thy name unto the men which thou gavest me out of the world: thine they were, and thou gavest them me; and they have kept thy word.
text.NABRE: “I revealed your name to those whom you gave me out of the world. They belonged to you, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
text.DRB: I have manifested thy name to the men whom thou gavest me out of the world. Thine they were, and to me thou gavest them: and they have kept thy word.

text.NRSV: Now they know that everything you have given me is from you;
text.Rotherham: Now, have they come to know, That, all things, as many as thou gavest me, are, from thee;
text.KJV: Now they have known that all things whatsoever thou hast given me are of thee.
text.NABRE: Now they know that everything you gave me is from you,
text.DRB: Now they have known that all things which thou gavest me are from thee:

text.NRSV: for the words that you gave to me I have given to them, and they have received them and know in truth that I came from you; and they have believed that you sent me.
text.Rotherham: And, the declarations which thou gavest me, have I given them, and, they, received them, and came to know in truth, that, from thee, came I forth,—and they believed, that, thou, didst send me forth.
text.KJV: For I have given unto them the words which thou gavest me; and they have received them, and have known surely that I came out from thee, and they have believed that thou didst send me.
text.NABRE: because the words you gave to me I have given to them, and they accepted them and truly understood that I came from you, and they have believed that you sent me.
text.DRB: because the words which thou gavest me, I have given them: and they have received, and known in very deed that I came forth from thee, and have believed that thou didst send me.

text.NRSV: I am asking on their behalf; I am not asking on behalf of the world, but on behalf of those whom you gave me, because they are yours.
text.Rotherham: I, concerning them, make request: not, concerning the world, do I make request, but concerning them whom thou hast given me, because, thine, they are,—
text.KJV: I pray for them: I pray not for the world, but for them which thou hast given me; for they are thine.
text.NABRE: I pray for them. I do not pray for the world but for the ones you have given me, because they are yours,
text.DRB: For them do I pray: Not for the world do I pray, but for them whom thou hast given me;

text.NRSV: All mine are yours, and yours are mine; and I have been glorified in them.
text.Rotherham: And, all my possessions, are, thine, and, thy possessions, mine, and I have been glorified in them.
text.KJV: And all mine are thine, and thine are mine; and I am glorified in them.
text.NABRE: and everything of mine is yours and everything of yours is mine, and I have been glorified in them.
text.DRB: because they be thine: and all things be thine, and thine be mine: and I am glorified in them. And now I am not in the world, and these are in the world, & I come to thee.

text.NRSV: And now I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, protect them in your name that you have given me, so that they may be one, as we are one.
text.Rotherham: And, no longer, am I in the world, and, they, are, in the world,—and, I, unto thee, am coming. Holy Father! Keep them in thy name which thou hast given me, that they may be one, as, we.
text.KJV: And now I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep through thine own name those whom thou hast given me, that they may be one, as we are.
text.NABRE: And now I will no longer be in the world, but they are in the world, while I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them in your name that you have given me, so that they may be one just as we are.
text.DRB: Holy father, keep them in thy name, whom thou hast given me; that they may be one, as also we.

text.NRSV: While I was with them, I protected them in your name that you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost except the one destined to be lost, so that the scripture might be fulfilled.
text.Rotherham: When I was with them, I, kept them in thy name which thou hast given me,—And I kept watch, and, none from among them, went the destruction,—save the son of destruction, that, the Scripture, might be fulfilled.
text.KJV: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy name: those that thou gavest me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled.
text.NABRE: When I was with them I protected them in your name that you gave me, and I guarded them, and none of them was lost except the son of destruction, in order that the scripture might be fulfilled.
text.DRB: When I was with them, I kept them in thy name. Those whom thou gavest me, have I kept: and none of them perished, but the son of perdition, that the scripture may be fulfilled.

text.NRSV: But now I am coming to you, and I speak these things in the world so that they may have my joy made complete in themselves.
text.Rotherham: But, now, unto thee, am I coming; and, these things, am I speaking in the world, that they may have my own joy fulfilled in themselves.
text.KJV: And now come I to thee; and these things I speak in the world, that they might have my joy fulfilled in themselves.
text.NABRE: But now I am coming to you. I speak this in the world so that they may share my joy completely.
text.DRB: And now I come to thee: and these things I speak in the world, that they may have my joy filled in themselves.

text.NRSV: I have given them your word, and the world has hated them because they do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
text.Rotherham: I, have given them thy word, and, the world, hated them, because they are not of the world—even as, I, am not of the world.
text.KJV: I have given them thy word; and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
text.NABRE: I gave them your word, and the world hated them, because they do not belong to the world any more than I belong to the world.
text.DRB: I have given them thy word, and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world: as I also am not of the world.

text.NRSV: I am not asking you to take them out of the world, but I ask you to protect them from the evil one.
text.Rotherham: I request not, that thou wouldst take them out of the world, but that thou wouldst keep them out of the evil:
text.KJV: I pray not that thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that thou shouldest keep them from the evil.
text.NABRE: I do not ask that you take them out of the world but that you keep them from the evil one.
text.DRB: I pray not that thou take them away out of the world, but that thou preserve them from evil.

text.NRSV: They do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
text.Rotherham: Of the world, they are not, even as, I, am not of the world.
text.KJV: They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
text.NABRE: They do not belong to the world any more than I belong to the world.
text.DRB: Of the world they are not: as I also am not of the world.

text.NRSV: Sanctify them in the truth; your word is truth.
text.Rotherham: Hallow them in the truth: Thine own word, is, truth:
text.KJV: Sanctify them through thy truth: thy word is truth.
text.NABRE: Consecrate them in the truth. Your word is truth.
text.DRB: Sanctify them in truth. Thy word is truth.

text.NRSV: As you have sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
text.Rotherham: Even as thou didst send me forth into the world, I also, send them forth into the world;
text.KJV: As thou hast sent me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world.
text.NABRE: As you sent me into the world, so I sent them into the world.
text.DRB: As thou didst send me into the world, I also have sent them into the world.

text.NRSV: And for their sakes I sanctify myself, so that they also may be sanctified in truth.
text.Rotherham: And, on their behalf, I, hallow myself, that, they also, may have become hallowed in truth.
text.KJV: And for their sakes I sanctify myself, that they also might be sanctified through the truth.
text.NABRE: And I consecrate myself for them, so that they also may be consecrated in truth.
text.DRB: And for them I do sanctify myself: that they also may be sanctified in truth.

text.NRSV: “I ask not only on behalf of these, but also on behalf of those who will believe in me through their word,
text.Rotherham: Not however concerning these alone, do I make request, but, concerning them also who believe, through their word, on me:
text.KJV: Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall believe on me through their word;
text.NABRE: “I pray not only for them, but also for those who will believe in me through their word,
text.DRB: And not for them only do I pray, but for them also that by their word shall believe in me:

text.NRSV: that they may all be one. As you, Father, are in me and I am in you, may they also be in us, so that the world may believe that you have sent me.
text.Rotherham: That they, all, may be, one,—even as, thou, Father, in me, and, I, in thee,—that, they also, in us, may be;—that, the world, may believe that, thou, didst send me forth.
text.KJV: That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us: that the world may believe that thou hast sent me.
text.NABRE: so that they may all be one, as you, Father, are in me and I in you, that they also may be in us, that the world may believe that you sent me.
text.DRB: that they all may be one, as thou (Father) in me & I in thee; that they also in us may be one: that the world may believe that thou hast sent me.

text.NRSV: The glory that you have given me I have given them, so that they may be one, as we are one,
text.Rotherham: And, I, the glory which thou hast given to me, have given to them, that they may be one, even as, we, are, one.—
text.KJV: And the glory which thou gavest me I have given them; that they may be one, even as we are one:
text.NABRE: And I have given them the glory you gave me, so that they may be one, as we are one,
text.DRB: And the glory that thou hast given me, have I given to them; that they may be one as we also are one.

text.NRSV: I in them and you in me, that they may become completely one, so that the world may know that you have sent me and have loved them even as you have loved me.
text.Rotherham: I, in them and, thou, in me; That they may have been perfected into, one,—That the world may get to know, that, thou, didst send me forth, and didst love them even as thou didst love, me.
text.KJV: I in them, and thou in me, that they may be made perfect in one; and that the world may know that thou hast sent me, and hast loved them, as thou hast loved me.
text.NABRE: I in them and you in me, that they may be brought to perfection as one, that the world may know that you sent me, and that you loved them even as you loved me.
text.DRB: I in them, and thou in me: that they may be consummate in one: and the world may know that thou hast sent me, and hast loved them, as me also thou hast loved.

text.NRSV: Father, I desire that those also, whom you have given me, may be with me where I am, to see my glory, which you have given me because you loved me before the foundation of the world.
text.Rotherham: Father! As touching that which thou hast given me, I desire—That, where, I, am, they also, may be, with me, that they may behold my own glory which thou hast given me,—because thou lovedst me, before the foundation of the world.
text.KJV: Father, I will that they also, whom thou hast given me, be with me where I am; that they may behold my glory, which thou hast given me: for thou lovedst me before the foundation of the world.
text.NABRE: Father, they are your gift to me. I wish that where I am they also may be with me, that they may see my glory that you gave me, because you loved me before the foundation of the world.
text.DRB: Father, whom thou hast given me, I will, that where I am, they also may be with me: that they may see my glory which thou hast given me, because thou hast loved me before the creation of the world.

text.NRSV: “Righteous Father, the world does not know you, but I know you; and these know that you have sent me.
text.Rotherham: Righteous Father! And so, the world, came not to know thee; but, I, came to know thee, and, these, came to know that, thou, didst send me forth:
text.KJV: O righteous Father, the world hath not known thee: but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me.
text.NABRE: Righteous Father, the world also does not know you, but I know you, and they know that you sent me.
text.DRB: Just Father, the world hath not known thee. But I have known thee: and these have known, that thou didst send me.

text.NRSV: I made your name known to them, and I will make it known, so that the love with which you have loved me may be in them, and I in them.”
text.Rotherham: And I made known unto them thy name, and will make known,—that, the love wherewith thou lovedst me, in them, may be, and, I, in them.
text.KJV: And I have declared unto them thy name, and will declare it: that the love wherewith thou hast loved me may be in them, and I in them.
text.NABRE: I made known to them your name and I will make it known, that the love with which you loved me may be in them and I in them.”
text.DRB: And I have notified thy name to them, and will notify it: that the love wherewith thou hast loved me may be in them, and I in them.

text.NRSV: After Jesus had spoken these words, he went out with his disciples across the Kidron valley to a place where there was a garden, which he and his disciples entered.
text.Rotherham: Having said these things, Jesus, went out, with his disciples, across the winter-torrent of the Kedron, where was a garden,—into which he entered, he, and his disciples.
text.KJV: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went forth with his disciples over the brook Cedron, where was a garden, into the which he entered, and his disciples.
text.NABRE: When he had said this, Jesus went out with his disciples across the Kidron valley to where there was a garden, into which he and his disciples entered.
text.DRB: When Jesus had said these things, he went forth with his Disciples beyond the Torrent-Cedron, where was a garden, into the which he entered and his Disciples.

text.NRSV: Now Judas, who betrayed him, also knew the place, because Jesus often met there with his disciples.
text.Rotherham: Now, Judas also, who was delivering him up, knew the place: because, oft, had Jesus been gathered there, with his disciples.
text.KJV: And Judas also, which betrayed him, knew the place: for Jesus ofttimes resorted thither with his disciples.
text.NABRE: Judas his betrayer also knew the place, because Jesus had often met there with his disciples.
text.DRB: And Judas also, that betrayed him, knew the place: because Jesus had often resorted thither together with his Disciples.

text.NRSV: So Judas brought a detachment of soldiers together with police from the chief priests and the Pharisees, and they came there with lanterns and torches and weapons.
text.Rotherham: Judas, therefore, receiving the band, and officers, from among the High-priests and [from among] the Pharisees, cometh thither, with lights and torches and weapons.
text.KJV: Judas then, having received a band of men and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, cometh thither with lanterns and torches and weapons.
text.NABRE: So Judas got a band of soldiers and guards from the chief priests and the Pharisees and went there with lanterns, torches, and weapons.
text.DRB: Judas therefore having received the band of men, and of the chief Priests and Pharisees, ministers, cometh thither with lanterns and torches and weapons.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus, knowing all that was to happen to him, came forward and asked them, “Whom are you looking for?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, knowing all the things coming upon him, went forth, and saith unto them—Whom, seek ye?
text.KJV: Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth, and said unto them, Whom seek ye?
text.NABRE: Jesus, knowing everything that was going to happen to him, went out and said to them, “Whom are you looking for?”
text.DRB: Jesus therefore knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth, and said to them: Whom seek ye?

text.NRSV: They answered, “Jesus of Nazareth.” Jesus replied, “I am he.” Judas, who betrayed him, was standing with them.
text.Rotherham: They answered him—Jesus, the Nazarene. He saith unto them—I, am he. Now Judas also, who was delivering him up, was standing with them.
text.KJV: They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith unto them, I am he. And Judas also, which betrayed him, stood with them.
text.NABRE: They answered him, “Jesus the Nazorean.” He said to them, “I AM.” Judas his betrayer was also with them.
text.DRB: They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith to them: I am he. And Judas also that betrayed him, stood with them.

text.NRSV: When Jesus said to them, “I am he,” they stepped back and fell to the ground.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he said unto them, I, am he, they went backwards, and fell to the ground.
text.KJV: As soon then as he had said unto them, I am he, they went backward, and fell to the ground.
text.NABRE: When he said to them, “I AM,” they turned away and fell to the ground.
text.DRB: As soon therefore as he said to them, I am he; they went backward, and fell to the ground.

text.NRSV: Again he asked them, “Whom are you looking for?” And they said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, he questioned them—Whom, seek ye? And, they, said—Jesus, the Nazarene.
text.KJV: Then asked he them again, Whom seek ye? And they said, Jesus of Nazareth.
text.NABRE: So he again asked them, “Whom are you looking for?” They said, “Jesus the Nazorean.”
text.DRB: Again therefore he asked them: Whom seek ye? And they said: Jesus of Nazareth.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am he. So if you are looking for me, let these men go.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—I told you, I, am he. If, then, ye seek me, let these go their way:—
text.KJV: Jesus answered, I have told you that I am he: if therefore ye seek me, let these go their way:
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “I told you that I AM. So if you are looking for me, let these men go.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered, I have told you, that I am he. If therefore you seek me, let these go their ways.

text.NRSV: This was to fulfill the word that he had spoken, “I did not lose a single one of those whom you gave me.”
text.Rotherham: that the word might be fulfilled which he had said—As touching them whom thou hast given me, I lost from among them, not so much as one.
text.KJV: That the saying might be fulfilled, which he spake, Of them which thou gavest me have I lost none.
text.NABRE: This was to fulfill what he had said, “I have not lost any of those you gave me.”
text.DRB: That the word might be fulfilled which he said, That of them whom thou hast given me, I have not lost any.

text.NRSV: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it, struck the high priest’s slave, and cut off his right ear. The slave’s name was Malchus.
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter, therefore, having a sword, drew it, and smote the High-priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. Now the name of the servant was Malchus.
text.KJV: Then Simon Peter having a sword drew it, and smote the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.
text.NABRE: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it, struck the high priest’s slave, and cut off his right ear. The slave’s name was Malchus.
text.DRB: Simon Peter therefore having a sword, drew it out; and smote the servant of the high Priest, & cut off his right ear. And the name of the servant was Malchus.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to Peter, “Put your sword back into its sheath. Am I not to drink the cup that the Father has given me?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto Peter—Thrust the sword into its sheath:—The cup which the Father hath given me, shall I in anywise not drink it?
text.KJV: Then said Jesus unto Peter, Put up thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?
text.NABRE: Jesus said to Peter, “Put your sword into its scabbard. Shall I not drink the cup that the Father gave me?”
text.DRB: Jesus therefore said to Peter: Put up thy sword into the scabbard. The chalice which my Father hath given me, shall not I drink it?

text.NRSV: So the soldiers, their officer, and the Jewish police arrested Jesus and bound him.
text.Rotherham: The band, therefore, and the captain, and the officers of the Jews, apprehended Jesus, and bound him,
text.KJV: Then the band and the captain and officers of the Jews took Jesus, and bound him,
text.NABRE: So the band of soldiers, the tribune, and the Jewish guards seized Jesus, bound him,
text.DRB: The band therefore and the Tribune & the ministers of the Jews apprehended Jesus, and bound him:

text.NRSV: First they took him to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year.
text.Rotherham: and led him unto Annas, first; for he was father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was the High-priest of that year.
text.KJV: And led him away to Annas first; for he was father in law to Caiaphas, which was the high priest that same year.
text.NABRE: and brought him to Annas first. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.
text.DRB: and they brought him to Annas first, for he was father in law to Caiphas, who was the high Priest of that year.

text.NRSV: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jews that it was better to have one person die for the people.
text.Rotherham: Now Caiaphas was he that gave counsel unto the Jews, that it was profitable for, one man, to die in behalf of the people.
text.KJV: Now Caiaphas was he, which gave counsel to the Jews, that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.
text.NABRE: It was Caiaphas who had counseled the Jews that it was better that one man should die rather than the people. Peter’s First Denial.
text.DRB: And Caiphas was he that had given the counsel to the Jews, That it is expedient that one man die for the people.

text.NRSV: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Since that disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest,
text.Rotherham: And Simon Peter was following with Jesus, also another disciple. But, that disciple, was known unto the High-priest, and entered in, with Jesus, into the court of the High-priest;
text.KJV: And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple: that disciple was known unto the high priest, and went in with Jesus into the palace of the high priest.
text.NABRE: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Now the other disciple was known to the high priest, and he entered the courtyard of the high priest with Jesus.
text.DRB: And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and another Disciple. And that Disciple was known to the high Priest, and went in with Jesus into the court of the high Priest.

text.NRSV: but Peter was standing outside at the gate. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out, spoke to the woman who guarded the gate, and brought Peter in.
text.Rotherham: whereas, Peter, remained standing at the door outside. The other disciple, therefore, that was known of the High-priest, went out, and spake unto the portress, and brought in Peter.
text.KJV: But Peter stood at the door without. Then went out that other disciple, which was known unto the high priest, and spake unto her that kept the door, and brought in Peter.
text.NABRE: But Peter stood at the gate outside. So the other disciple, the acquaintance of the high priest, went out and spoke to the gatekeeper and brought Peter in.
text.DRB: But Peter stood at the door without. The other Disciple therefore that was known to the high Priest, went forth, and spake to the portress, and brought in Peter.

text.NRSV: The woman said to Peter, “You are not also one of this man’s disciples, are you?” He said, “I am not.”
text.Rotherham: The female servant, therefore, the portress, saith unto Peter—Art, thou also, from among the disciples of this man? He, saith—I am not!
text.KJV: Then saith the damsel that kept the door unto Peter, Art not thou also one of this man’s disciples? He saith, I am not.
text.NABRE: Then the maid who was the gatekeeper said to Peter, “You are not one of this man’s disciples, are you?” He said, “I am not.”
text.DRB: The wench therefore that was portress, saith to Peter: Art not thou also of this man's Disciples? He saith to her, I am not.

text.NRSV: Now the slaves and the police had made a charcoal fire because it was cold, and they were standing around it and warming themselves. Peter also was standing with them and warming himself.
text.Rotherham: Now the servants and the officers were standing by, having made, a coal fire, because it was cold,—and were warming themselves; and Peter also was with them, standing and warming himself.
text.KJV: And the servants and officers stood there, who had made a fire of coals; for it was cold: and they warmed themselves: and Peter stood with them, and warmed himself.
text.NABRE: Now the slaves and the guards were standing around a charcoal fire that they had made, because it was cold, and were warming themselves. Peter was also standing there keeping warm. The Inquiry Before Annas.
text.DRB: And the servants & ministers stood at a fire of coals, because it was cold, and warmed themselves. And with them was Peter also standing, and warming himself.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and about his teaching.
text.Rotherham: The High-priest, therefore, questioned Jesus concerning his disciples, and concerning his teaching.
text.KJV: The high priest then asked Jesus of his disciples, and of his doctrine.
text.NABRE: The high priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and about his doctrine.
text.DRB: The high Priest therefore asked Jesus of his Disciples, and of his doctrine.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “I have spoken openly to the world; I have always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews come together. I have said nothing in secret.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered him—I, openly, have spoken, unto the world,—I, ever, taught, in synagogue, and in the temple, where all the Jews gather together; and, in secret, spake nothing:
text.KJV: Jesus answered him, I spake openly to the world; I ever taught in the synagogue, and in the temple, whither the Jews always resort; and in secret have I said nothing.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered him, “I have spoken publicly to the world. I have always taught in a synagogue or in the temple area where all the Jews gather, and in secret I have said nothing.
text.DRB: Jesus answered him: I have openly spoken to the world: I have always taught in the Synagogue, and in the temple whither all the Jews resort together: and in secret I have spoken nothing.

text.NRSV: Why do you ask me? Ask those who heard what I said to them; they know what I said.”
text.Rotherham: Why questionest thou me? question them who have heard, what I spake unto them. See! these, know what, I, said.
text.KJV: Why askest thou me? ask them which heard me, what I have said unto them: behold, they know what I said.
text.NABRE: Why ask me? Ask those who heard me what I said to them. They know what I said.”
text.DRB: Why askest thou me? ask them that have heard what I have spoken unto them: behold they know what things I have said.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, one of the police standing nearby struck Jesus on the face, saying, “Is that how you answer the high priest?”
text.Rotherham: And, when, these things, he had said, one of the officers who was standing by, gave a smart blow to Jesus, saying—Thus, answerest thou, the High-priest?
text.KJV: And when he had thus spoken, one of the officers which stood by struck Jesus with the palm of his hand, saying, Answerest thou the high priest so?
text.NABRE: When he had said this, one of the temple guards standing there struck Jesus and said, “Is this the way you answer the high priest?”
text.DRB: When he had said these things, one of the ministers standing by, gave Jesus a blow, saying: Answerest thou the high Priest so?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “If I have spoken wrongly, testify to the wrong. But if I have spoken rightly, why do you strike me?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered him—If, with abuse, I spake, bear witness of the abuse: but, if with respect, why, smitest thou me?
text.KJV: Jesus answered him, If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?
text.NABRE: Jesus answered him, “If I have spoken wrongly, testify to the wrong; but if I have spoken rightly, why do you strike me?”
text.DRB: Jesus answered him: If I have spoken ill, give testimony of evil: but if well, why strikest thou me?

text.NRSV: Then Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.
text.Rotherham: Annas therefore sent him forth, bound, unto Caiaphas the High-priest.
text.KJV: Now Annas had sent him bound unto Caiaphas the high priest.
text.NABRE: Then Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas the high priest. Peter Denies Jesus Again.
text.DRB: And Annas sent him bound to Caiphas the high priest.

text.NRSV: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They asked him, “You are not also one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not.”
text.Rotherham: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said, therefore, unto him—Art, thou also, from among his disciples? He, denied, and said—I am not!
text.KJV: And Simon Peter stood and warmed himself. They said therefore unto him, Art not thou also one of his disciples? He denied it, and said, I am not.
text.NABRE: Now Simon Peter was standing there keeping warm. And they said to him, “You are not one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not.”
text.DRB: And Simon Peter was standing, and warming himself. They said therefore to him: Art not thou also of his Disciples: He denied and said: I am not.

text.NRSV: One of the slaves of the high priest, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, asked, “Did I not see you in the garden with him?”
text.Rotherham: Saith one from among the servants of the High-priest, being, kinsman, of him whose ear Peter cut off,—Did not, I, see thee, in the garden with him?
text.KJV: One of the servants of the high priest, being his kinsman whose ear Peter cut off, saith, Did not I see thee in the garden with him?
text.NABRE: One of the slaves of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said, “Didn’t I see you in the garden with him?”
text.DRB: One of the servants of the high Priest saith to him, his cousin whose ear Peter did cut off: Did not I see thee in the garden with him?

text.NRSV: Again Peter denied it, and at that moment the cock crowed.
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, Peter denied. And, straightway, a cock crew.
text.KJV: Peter then denied again: and immediately the cock crew.
text.NABRE: Again Peter denied it. And immediately the cock crowed.
text.DRB: Again therefore Peter denied: and forthwith the cock crew.

text.NRSV: Then they took Jesus from Caiaphas to Pilate’s headquarters. It was early in the morning. They themselves did not enter the headquarters, so as to avoid ritual defilement and to be able to eat the Passover.
text.Rotherham: So they lead Jesus from Caiaphas unto the judgment-hall. Now it was early; and, they themselves, entered not into the judgment-hall, that they might not be defiled, but might eat the passover.
text.KJV: Then led they Jesus from Caiaphas unto the hall of judgment: and it was early; and they themselves went not into the judgment hall, lest they should be defiled; but that they might eat the passover.
text.NABRE: The Trial Before Pilate. Then they brought Jesus from Caiaphas to the praetorium. It was morning. And they themselves did not enter the praetorium, in order not to be defiled so that they could eat the Passover.
text.DRB: They therefore bring Jesus from Caiphas into the Palace. And it was morning: and they went not in into the Palace, that they might not be contaminated, but that they might eat the Pasch.

text.NRSV: So Pilate went out to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, went forth outside unto them, and saith,—What accusation bring ye against this man?
text.KJV: Pilate then went out unto them, and said, What accusation bring ye against this man?
text.NABRE: So Pilate came out to them and said, “What charge do you bring [against] this man?”
text.DRB: Pilate therefore went forth to them without, and said: What accusation bring you against this man?

text.NRSV: They answered, “If this man were not a criminal, we would not have handed him over to you.”
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—If this one had not been doing, mischief, unto thee, had we not delivered him up.
text.KJV: They answered and said unto him, If he were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered him up unto thee.
text.NABRE: They answered and said to him, “If he were not a criminal, we would not have handed him over to you.”
text.DRB: They answered and said to him: If he were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered him up to thee.

text.NRSV: Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves and judge him according to your law.” The Jews replied, “We are not permitted to put anyone to death.”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, said unto them—Ye, take him, and, according to your law, judge ye him. The Jews said unto him—Unto us, it is not allowed, to kill anyone!—
text.KJV: Then said Pilate unto them, Take ye him, and judge him according to your law. The Jews therefore said unto him, It is not lawful for us to put any man to death:
text.NABRE: At this, Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves, and judge him according to your law.” The Jews answered him, “We do not have the right to execute anyone,”
text.DRB: Pilate therefore said to them: Take him you, and according to your law judge him. The Jews therefore said to him: It is not lawful for us to kill any man.

text.NRSV: (This was to fulfill what Jesus had said when he indicated the kind of death he was to die.)
text.Rotherham: that, the word of Jesus, might be fulfilled, which he spake, signifying, by what manner of death, he was about to die.
text.KJV: That the saying of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spake, signifying what death he should die.
text.NABRE: in order that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled that he said indicating the kind of death he would die.
text.DRB: That the word of Jesus might be fulfilled which he said, signifying what death he should die.

text.NRSV: Then Pilate entered the headquarters again, summoned Jesus, and asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, entered again into the judgment-hall; and addressed Jesus, and said unto him—Art, thou, the king of the Jews?
text.KJV: Then Pilate entered into the judgment hall again, and called Jesus, and said unto him, Art thou the King of the Jews?
text.NABRE: So Pilate went back into the praetorium and summoned Jesus and said to him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”
text.DRB: Pilate therefore went into the Palace again, and called Jesus, and said to him: Art thou the King of the Jews?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Do you ask this on your own, or did others tell you about me?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—Of thyself, art, thou, this thing saying; or did, others, tell thee concerning me?
text.KJV: Jesus answered him, Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “Do you say this on your own or have others told you about me?”
text.DRB: Jesus answered: Sayest thou this of thyself, or have others told it thee of me?

text.NRSV: Pilate replied, “I am not a Jew, am I? Your own nation and the chief priests have handed you over to me. What have you done?”
text.Rotherham: Pilate answered—Am, I, a Jew? Thine own nation, and the High-priests, delivered thee up, unto me! What, hast thou done?
text.KJV: Pilate answered, Am I a Jew? Thine own nation and the chief priests have delivered thee unto me: what hast thou done?
text.NABRE: Pilate answered, “I am not a Jew, am I? Your own nation and the chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done?”
text.DRB: Pilate answered: Why, am I a Jew? Thy Nation, and the chief Priests have delivered thee up to me: what hast thou done?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not from this world. If my kingdom were from this world, my followers would be fighting to keep me from being handed over to the Jews. But as it is, my kingdom is not from here.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—My kingdom, is not of this world: If, of this world, had been my kingdom, mine own officers, would have been striving, that I should not be delivered up unto the Jews: but, now, my kingdom, is not from hence.
text.KJV: Jesus answered, My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered, “My kingdom does not belong to this world. If my kingdom did belong to this world, my attendants [would] be fighting to keep me from being handed over to the Jews. But as it is, my kingdom is not here.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered: My Kingdom is not of this world. If my Kingdom were of this world, my ministers verily would strive that I should not be delivered to the Jews. But now my Kingdom is not from hence.

text.NRSV: Pilate asked him, “So you are a king?” Jesus answered, “You say that I am a king. For this I was born, and for this I came into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone who belongs to the truth listens to my voice.”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, said unto him—And yet, thou, art, not a king? Jesus answered—Thou, sayest, that, a king, I am: I, for this, have been born, and, for this, have come into the world,—that I may bear witness unto the truth: Every one who is of the truth, hearkeneth unto my voice.
text.KJV: Pilate therefore said unto him, Art thou a king then? Jesus answered, Thou sayest that I am a king. To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice.
text.NABRE: So Pilate said to him, “Then you are a king?” Jesus answered, “You say I am a king. For this I was born and for this I came into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone who belongs to the truth listens to my voice.”
text.DRB: Pilate therefore said to him: Art thou a King then? Jesus answered: Thou sayest, that I am a King. For this was I born, and for this came I into the world: that I should give testimony to the truth. Every one that is of the truth, heareth my voice.

text.NRSV: Pilate asked him, “What is truth?” After he had said this, he went out to the Jews again and told them, “I find no case against him.
text.Rotherham: Pilate saith unto him—What, is truth? And, this saying, again, went he out to the Jews, and saith unto them,—I, find in him, not a single fault.
text.KJV: Pilate saith unto him, What is truth? And when he had said this, he went out again unto the Jews, and saith unto them, I find in him no fault at all.
text.NABRE: Pilate said to him, “What is truth?” When he had said this, he again went out to the Jews and said to them, “I find no guilt in him.
text.DRB: Pilate saith to him: What is truth? And when he had said this, he went forth again to the Jews, and saith to them: I find no cause in him.

text.NRSV: But you have a custom that I release someone for you at the Passover. Do you want me to release for you the King of the Jews?”
text.Rotherham: Howbeit ye have, a custom, that, some one, I should release unto you, during the passover:—Are ye minded, then, that I release unto you the king of the Jews?
text.KJV: But ye have a custom, that I should release unto you one at the passover: will ye therefore that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
text.NABRE: But you have a custom that I release one prisoner to you at Passover. Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”
text.DRB: But you have a custom that I should release one to you in the Pasch: will you therefore that I release unto you the King of the Jews?

text.NRSV: They shouted in reply, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a bandit.
text.Rotherham: So they cried aloud again, saying—Not this man, but Barabbas! Now, Barabbas, was, a robber.
text.KJV: Then cried they all again, saying, Not this man, but Barabbas. Now Barabbas was a robber.
text.NABRE: They cried out again, “Not this one but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a revolutionary.
text.DRB: They all therefore cried again, saying: Not him but Barabbas. And Barabbas was a thief.

text.NRSV: Then Pilate took Jesus and had him flogged.
text.Rotherham: Then Pilate, therefore, took Jesus, and scourged him.
text.KJV: Then Pilate therefore took Jesus, and scourged him.
text.NABRE: Then Pilate took Jesus and had him scourged.
text.DRB: Then therefore Pilate took Jesus, and scourged him.

text.NRSV: And the soldiers wove a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and they dressed him in a purple robe.
text.Rotherham: And, the soldiers, plaiting a crown out of thorns, placed it upon his head, and, a purple robe, cast they about him;
text.KJV: And the soldiers platted a crown of thorns, and put it on his head, and they put on him a purple robe,
text.NABRE: And the soldiers wove a crown out of thorns and placed it on his head, and clothed him in a purple cloak,
text.DRB: And the soldiers platting a crown of thorns, put it upon his head: and they put about him a purple garment.

text.NRSV: They kept coming up to him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!” and striking him on the face.
text.Rotherham: and kept coming unto him, and saying—Joy to thee! O King of the Jews!—and were giving unto him smart blows.
text.KJV: And said, Hail, King of the Jews! and they smote him with their hands.
text.NABRE: and they came to him and said, “Hail, King of the Jews!” And they struck him repeatedly.
text.DRB: And they came to him, and said: Hail King of the Jews; and they gave him blows.

text.NRSV: Pilate went out again and said to them, “Look, I am bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no case against him.”
text.Rotherham: And Pilate went forth again outside, and saith unto them—See! I lead him unto you outside, that ye may take knowledge, that, no single fault, do I find in him.
text.KJV: Pilate therefore went forth again, and saith unto them, Behold, I bring him forth to you, that ye may know that I find no fault in him.
text.NABRE: Once more Pilate went out and said to them, “Look, I am bringing him out to you, so that you may know that I find no guilt in him.”
text.DRB: Pilate went forth again, and saith to them: Behold I bring him forth unto you, that you may know that I find no cause in him.

text.NRSV: So Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said to them, “Here is the man!”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, came forth outside, wearing the thorn crown, and the purple mantle. And he saith unto them—Lo! the Man!
text.KJV: Then came Jesus forth, wearing the crown of thorns, and the purple robe. And Pilate saith unto them, Behold the man!
text.NABRE: So Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple cloak. And he said to them, “Behold, the man!”
text.DRB: Jesus therefore went forth carrying the crown of thorns, and the purple vestiment. And he saith to them: Lo the man.

text.NRSV: When the chief priests and the police saw him, they shouted, “Crucify him! Crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves and crucify him; I find no case against him.”
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, the High-priests and the officers saw him, they cried aloud, saying—Crucify! Crucify! Pilate saith unto them—Ye, take him, and crucify; for, I, find not in him, a fault.
text.KJV: When the chief priests therefore and officers saw him, they cried out, saying, Crucify him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Take ye him, and crucify him: for I find no fault in him.
text.NABRE: When the chief priests and the guards saw him they cried out, “Crucify him, crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves and crucify him. I find no guilt in him.”
text.DRB: When the chief Priests therefore and the ministers had seen him, they cried, saying: Crucify, crucify him. Pilate saith to them: Take him you, and crucify him. For I find no cause in him.

text.NRSV: The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and according to that law he ought to die because he has claimed to be the Son of God.”
text.Rotherham: The Jews answered him—We, have, a law, and, according to the law, he ought to die, because, Son of God, himself, he made.
text.KJV: The Jews answered him, We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.
text.NABRE: The Jews answered, “We have a law, and according to that law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.”
text.DRB: The Jews answered him: We have a Law; and according to the Law he ought to die; because he hath made himself the Son of God.

text.NRSV: Now when Pilate heard this, he was more afraid than ever.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, Pilate heard this word, he was the more afraid;
text.KJV: When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he was the more afraid;
text.NABRE: Now when Pilate heard this statement, he became even more afraid,
text.DRB: When Pilate therefore had heard this saying, he feared more.

text.NRSV: He entered his headquarters again and asked Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus gave him no answer.
