text.Rotherham: and entered into the judgment-hall again, and saith unto Jesus—Whence, art, thou? But, Jesus, gave him no, answer.
text.KJV: And went again into the judgment hall, and saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.
text.NABRE: and went back into the praetorium and said to Jesus, “Where are you from?” Jesus did not answer him.
text.DRB: And he entered into the Palace again; and he saith to Jesus: Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.

text.NRSV: Pilate therefore said to him, “Do you refuse to speak to me? Do you not know that I have power to release you, and power to crucify you?”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, saith unto him—Unto me, dost thou not speak? Knowest thou not, that, authority, have I to release thee, and, authority, have I to crucify thee?
text.KJV: Then saith Pilate unto him, Speakest thou not unto me? knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and have power to release thee?
text.NABRE: So Pilate said to him, “Do you not speak to me? Do you not know that I have power to release you and I have power to crucify you?”
text.DRB: Pilate therefore saith to him: Speakest thou not to me? knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and I have power to release thee?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “You would have no power over me unless it had been given you from above; therefore the one who handed me over to you is guilty of a greater sin.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered him—Thou couldst have had no authority against me, at all, if it had not been given unto thee from above. Therefore, he that delivered me unto thee, hath, greater sin.
text.KJV: Jesus answered, Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered [him], “You would have no power over me if it had not been given to you from above. For this reason the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.”
text.DRB: Jesus answered: Thou shouldest not have any power against me, unless it were given thee from above. Therefore he that hath betrayed me to thee, hath the greater sin.

text.NRSV: From then on Pilate tried to release him, but the Jews cried out, “If you release this man, you are no friend of the emperor. Everyone who claims to be a king sets himself against the emperor.”
text.Rotherham: For this cause, Pilate, began seeking to release him; but, the Jews, cried aloud saying—If this man thou release, thou art not a friend of Caesar, for, every one who maketh himself king, speaketh against Caesar.
text.KJV: And from thenceforth Pilate sought to release him: but the Jews cried out, saying, If thou let this man go, thou art not Cesar’s friend: whosoever maketh himself a king speaketh against Cesar.
text.NABRE: Consequently, Pilate tried to release him; but the Jews cried out, “If you release him, you are not a Friend of Caesar. Everyone who makes himself a king opposes Caesar.”
text.DRB: From thence forth Pilate sought to release him. But the Jews cried, saying: If thou release this man, thou art not Cæsar's friend. Every one that maketh himself a king, speaketh against Cæsar.

text.NRSV: When Pilate heard these words, he brought Jesus outside and sat on the judge’s bench at a place called The Stone Pavement, or in Hebrew Gabbatha.
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, when he heard these words, led Jesus outside, and sat down upon a raised seat, in a place called Pavement, but, in Hebrew, Gabbatha.
text.KJV: When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Jesus forth, and sat down in the judgment seat in a place that is called the Pavement, but in the Hebrew, Gabbatha.
text.NABRE: When Pilate heard these words he brought Jesus out and seated him on the judge’s bench in the place called Stone Pavement, in Hebrew, Gabbatha.
text.DRB: But Pilate when he had heard these words, brought forth Jesus: and he sat in the judgement seat, in the place that is called Lithostrotos, and in Hebrew Gabbatha.

text.NRSV: Now it was the day of Preparation for the Passover; and it was about noon. He said to the Jews, “Here is your King!”
text.Rotherham: Now it was the preparation of the passover,—it was about the sixth hour. And he saith unto the Jews—See! your King!
text.KJV: And it was the preparation of the passover, and about the sixth hour: and he saith unto the Jews, Behold your King!
text.NABRE: It was preparation day for Passover, and it was about noon. And he said to the Jews, “Behold, your king!”
text.DRB: And it was the Parasceve of Pasch, about the sixth hour, and he saith to the Jews: Lo your King.

text.NRSV: They cried out, “Away with him! Away with him! Crucify him!” Pilate asked them, “Shall I crucify your King?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but the emperor.”
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, cried aloud—Away! away! Crucify him! Pilate saith unto them—Your king, shall I crucify? The High-priests answered—We have no king but Caesar!
text.KJV: But they cried out, Away with him, away with him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Shall I crucify your King? The chief priests answered, We have no king but Cesar.
text.NABRE: They cried out, “Take him away, take him away! Crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your king?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar.”
text.DRB: But they cried: Away, away with him, crucify him. Pilate saith to them: Shall I crucify your King? The chief Priests answered: We have no King, but Cæsar.

text.NRSV: Then he handed him over to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus;
text.Rotherham: Then, therefore, he delivered him up unto them, that he might be crucified. They took possession, therefore, of Jesus.
text.KJV: Then delivered he him therefore unto them to be crucified. And they took Jesus, and led him away.
text.NABRE: Then he handed him over to them to be crucified. The Crucifixion of Jesus. So they took Jesus,
text.DRB: Then therefore he delivered him unto them for to be crucified. And they took Jesus, and led him forth.

text.NRSV: and carrying the cross by himself, he went out to what is called The Place of the Skull, which in Hebrew is called Golgotha.
text.Rotherham: And, bearing for himself the cross, he went forth unto the so-called Skull-place, which is named, in Hebrew, Golgotha;
text.KJV: And he bearing his cross went forth into a place called the place of a skull, which is called in the Hebrew Golgotha:
text.NABRE: and carrying the cross himself he went out to what is called the Place of the Skull, in Hebrew, Golgotha.
text.DRB: And bearing his own cross he went forth into that which is called the place of Calvary, in Hebrew Golgotha.

text.NRSV: There they crucified him, and with him two others, one on either side, with Jesus between them.
text.Rotherham: where, him, they crucified; and, with him, other two, on this side and on that, and, in the midst, Jesus.
text.KJV: Where they crucified him, and two other with him, on either side one, and Jesus in the midst.
text.NABRE: There they crucified him, and with him two others, one on either side, with Jesus in the middle.
text.DRB: Where they crucified him, and with him two others, on the one side & on the other, and in the midst Jesus.

text.NRSV: Pilate also had an inscription written and put on the cross. It read, “Jesus of Nazareth, the King of the Jews.”
text.Rotherham: And Pilate wrote a title also, and placed on the cross; and there was written—JESUS, THE NAZARENE, THE KING OF THE JEWS.
text.KJV: And Pilate wrote a title, and put it on the cross. And the writing was, JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS.
text.NABRE: Pilate also had an inscription written and put on the cross. It read, “Jesus the Nazorean, the King of the Jews.”
text.DRB: And Pilate wrote a title also: and he put it upon the cross. And it was written, Jesus of Nazareth The King of The Jews.

text.NRSV: Many of the Jews read this inscription, because the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, and in Greek.
text.Rotherham: This title, therefore, read many of the Jews, because, near, was the place to the city where Jesus was crucified; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, in Greek.
text.KJV: This title then read many of the Jews: for the place where Jesus was crucified was nigh to the city: and it was written in Hebrew, and Greek, and Latin.
text.NABRE: Now many of the Jews read this inscription, because the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city; and it was written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek.
text.DRB: This title therefore many of the Jews did read: because the place where Jesus was crucified, was nigh to the city: and it was written in Hebrew, in Greek, and in Latin.

text.NRSV: Then the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate, “Do not write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but, ‘This man said, I am King of the Jews.’”
text.Rotherham: The High-priests of the Jews, therefore, were saying unto Pilate—Do not be writing, The King of the Jews; but that, he, said: King of the Jews, I am.
text.KJV: Then said the chief priests of the Jews to Pilate, Write not, The King of the Jews; but that he said, I am King of the Jews.
text.NABRE: So the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate, “Do not write ‘The King of the Jews,’ but that he said, ‘I am the King of the Jews.’”
text.DRB: The chief Priests therefore of the Jews said to Pilate: Write not, The King of the Jews; but that he said, I am King of the Jews.

text.NRSV: Pilate answered, “What I have written I have written.”
text.Rotherham: Pilate answered—What I have written, I have written!
text.KJV: Pilate answered, What I have written I have written.
text.NABRE: Pilate answered, “What I have written, I have written.”
text.DRB: Pilate answered: That which I have written, I have written.

text.NRSV: When the soldiers had crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and divided them into four parts, one for each soldier. They also took his tunic; now the tunic was seamless, woven in one piece from the top.
text.Rotherham: The soldiers, therefore, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments and made four parts, unto each soldier, a part; also the tunic. Howbeit, the tunic was without seam, from above, woven throughout.
text.KJV: Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments, and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also his coat: now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout.
text.NABRE: When the soldiers had crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and divided them into four shares, a share for each soldier. They also took his tunic, but the tunic was seamless, woven in one piece from the top down.
text.DRB: The soldiers therefore when they had crucified him, took his garments (and they made four parts, to every soldier a part) & his coat. And his coat was without seam, wrought from the top throughout.

text.NRSV: So they said to one another, “Let us not tear it, but cast lots for it to see who will get it.” This was to fulfill what the scripture says, “They divided my clothes among themselves, and for my clothing they cast lots.”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, one to another—Let us not rend it, but cast lots for it, whose, it shall be;—that, the Scripture, might be fulfilled—They parted my garments amongst them, and, for my vestment, they cast lots:—yes verily, the soldiers, these things did.
text.KJV: They said therefore among themselves, Let us not rend it, but cast lots for it, whose it shall be: that the scripture might be fulfilled, which saith, They parted my raiment among them, and for my vesture they did cast lots. These things therefore the soldiers did.
text.NABRE: So they said to one another, “Let’s not tear it, but cast lots for it to see whose it will be,” in order that the passage of scripture might be fulfilled [that says]: “They divided my garments among them, and for my vesture they cast lots.” This is what the soldiers did.
text.DRB: They said therefore one to another: Let us not cut it, but let us cast lots for it whose it shall be. That the scripture might be fulfilled saying: They have parted my garments among them: and upon my vesture they have cast lots. And the soldiers did these things.

text.NRSV: And that is what the soldiers did. Meanwhile, standing near the cross of Jesus were his mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.
text.Rotherham: And there were standing by the cross of Jesus, his mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary the Magdalene.
text.KJV: Now there stood by the cross of Jesus his mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Cleophas, and Mary Magdalene.
text.NABRE: Standing by the cross of Jesus were his mother and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary of Magdala.
text.DRB: And there stood beside the cross of Jesus, his mother, and his mother's sister, Mary of Cleophas, and Mary Magdalene.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing beside her, he said to his mother, “Woman, here is your son.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, seeing his mother and the disciple whom he loved, saith unto his mother—O woman, see! thy son!
text.KJV: When Jesus therefore saw his mother, and the disciple standing by, whom he loved, he saith unto his mother, Woman, behold thy son!
text.NABRE: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple there whom he loved, he said to his mother, “Woman, behold, your son.”
text.DRB: When Jesus therefore had seen his mother and the Disciple standing whom he loved, he saith to his mother: Woman, behold thy son.

text.NRSV: Then he said to the disciple, “Here is your mother.” And from that hour the disciple took her into his own home.
text.Rotherham: Afterwards, he saith unto the disciple—See! thy mother! And, from that hour, the disciple took her unto his own home.
text.KJV: Then saith he to the disciple, Behold thy mother! And from that hour that disciple took her unto his own home.
text.NABRE: Then he said to the disciple, “Behold, your mother.” And from that hour the disciple took her into his home.
text.DRB: After that, he saith to the Disciple: Behold thy mother. And from that hour the Disciple took her to his own.

text.NRSV: After this, when Jesus knew that all was now finished, he said (in order to fulfill the scripture), “I am thirsty.”
text.Rotherham: After this, Jesus, knowing that, already, all things, have been finished,—that the Scripture might be fulfilled, saith—I thirst!
text.KJV: After this, Jesus knowing that all things were now accomplished, that the scripture might be fulfilled, saith, I thirst.
text.NABRE: After this, aware that everything was now finished, in order that the scripture might be fulfilled, Jesus said, “I thirst.”
text.DRB: Afterward Jesus knowing that all things were now consummate, that the scripture might be fulfilled, he saith, I thirst.

text.NRSV: A jar full of sour wine was standing there. So they put a sponge full of the wine on a branch of hyssop and held it to his mouth.
text.Rotherham: A vessel, was standing, full of vinegar. A sponge, therefore, full of the vinegar, put about, hyssop, brought they unto his mouth.
text.KJV: Now there was set a vessel full of vinegar: and they filled a spunge with vinegar, and put it upon hyssop, and put it to his mouth.
text.NABRE: There was a vessel filled with common wine. So they put a sponge soaked in wine on a sprig of hyssop and put it up to his mouth.
text.DRB: Avessel therefore stood there full of vinegar. And they putting a sponge full of vinegar about hyssop, offered it to his mouth.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had received the wine, he said, “It is finished.” Then he bowed his head and gave up his spirit.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he had received the vinegar, Jesus said—It is finished! And, bowing his head, delivered up his spirit.
text.KJV: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, It is finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost.
text.NABRE: When Jesus had taken the wine, he said, “It is finished.” And bowing his head, he handed over the spirit.
text.DRB: Jesus therefore when he had taken the vinegar, said: It is consummate. And bowing his head, he gave up the ghost.

text.NRSV: Since it was the day of Preparation, the Jews did not want the bodies left on the cross during the sabbath, especially because that sabbath was a day of great solemnity. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of the crucified men broken and the bodies removed.
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, since it was, a preparation, that the bodies might not remain upon the cross during the Sabbath,—for that Sabbath day was, great, requested Pilate that their legs might be broken, and they be taken away.
text.KJV: The Jews therefore, because it was the preparation, that the bodies should not remain upon the cross on the sabbath day, (for that sabbath day was an high day,) besought Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away.
text.NABRE: The Blood and Water. Now since it was preparation day, in order that the bodies might not remain on the cross on the sabbath, for the sabbath day of that week was a solemn one, the Jews asked Pilate that their legs be broken and they be taken down.
text.DRB: The Jews therefore (because it was the Parasceve) that the bodies might not remain upon the cross on the Sabboth (for that was a great Sabboth day) they desired Pilate that their legs might be broken, and they might be taken away.

text.NRSV: Then the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first and of the other who had been crucified with him.
text.Rotherham: The soldiers, therefore, came; and, of the first, indeed, brake the legs, and of the other who was crucified with him,—
text.KJV: Then came the soldiers, and brake the legs of the first, and of the other which was crucified with him.
text.NABRE: So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first and then of the other one who was crucified with Jesus.
text.DRB: The soldiers therefore came: and of the first indeed they brake the legs, and of the other that was crucified with him.

text.NRSV: But when they came to Jesus and saw that he was already dead, they did not break his legs.
text.Rotherham: but coming, unto Jesus, when they saw that, already, he was dead, they brake not his legs;—
text.KJV: But when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was dead already, they brake not his legs:
text.NABRE: But when they came to Jesus and saw that he was already dead, they did not break his legs,
text.DRB: But after they were come to Jesus, when they saw that he was dead, they did not break his legs,

text.NRSV: Instead, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and at once blood and water came out.
text.Rotherham: but, one of the soldiers, with a spear, pierced, his side, and there came out, straightway, blood and water.
text.KJV: But one of the soldiers with a spear pierced his side, and forthwith came there out blood and water.
text.NABRE: but one soldier thrust his lance into his side, and immediately blood and water flowed out.
text.DRB: but one of the soldiers with a spear opened his side, & incontinent there came forth blood and water.

text.NRSV: (He who saw this has testified so that you also may believe. His testimony is true, and he knows that he tells the truth.)
text.Rotherham: And, he that hath seen, hath borne witness; and, genuine, is his testimony, and, he, knoweth that he saith, what is true, that, ye also, may believe.
text.KJV: And he that saw it bare record, and his record is true: and he knoweth that he saith true, that ye might believe.
text.NABRE: An eyewitness has testified, and his testimony is true; he knows that he is speaking the truth, so that you also may [come to] believe.
text.DRB: And he that saw it, hath given testimony: and his testimony is true. And he knoweth that he saith true, that you also may believe.

text.NRSV: These things occurred so that the scripture might be fulfilled, “None of his bones shall be broken.”
text.Rotherham: For these things came to pass, that, the Scripture, might be fulfilled—A bone thereof, shall not be crushed;
text.KJV: For these things were done, that the scripture should be fulfilled, A bone of him shall not be broken.
text.NABRE: For this happened so that the scripture passage might be fulfilled: “Not a bone of it will be broken.”
text.DRB: For these things were done that the scripture might be fulfilled: You shall not break a bone of him.

text.NRSV: And again another passage of scripture says, “They will look on the one whom they have pierced.”
text.Rotherham: and, again, a different Scripture, saith—They shall look unto him whom they pierced.
text.KJV: And again another scripture saith, They shall look on him whom they pierced.
text.NABRE: And again another passage says: “They will look upon him whom they have pierced.” The Burial of Jesus.
text.DRB: And again another scripture saith: They shall look on him whom they pierced.

text.NRSV: After these things, Joseph of Arimathea, who was a disciple of Jesus, though a secret one because of his fear of the Jews, asked Pilate to let him take away the body of Jesus. Pilate gave him permission; so he came and removed his body.
text.Rotherham: But, after these things, Joseph from Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but having kept it secret for fear of the Jews, requested Pilate, that he might take away the body of Jesus; and Pilate gave permission. He came, therefore, and took away his body.
text.KJV: And after this Joseph of Arimathea, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, besought Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus: and Pilate gave him leave. He came therefore, and took the body of Jesus.
text.NABRE: After this, Joseph of Arimathea, secretly a disciple of Jesus for fear of the Jews, asked Pilate if he could remove the body of Jesus. And Pilate permitted it. So he came and took his body.
text.DRB: And after these things Joseph of Arimathæa (because he was a Disciple of Jesus, but secret for fear of the Jews) desired Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus. And Pilate permitted. He came therefore, & took away the body of Jesus.

text.NRSV: Nicodemus, who had at first come to Jesus by night, also came, bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, weighing about a hundred pounds.
text.Rotherham: There came, moreover, Nicodemus also,—he that came unto him by night at the first,—bearing a roll of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred pounds’ weight.
text.KJV: And there came also Nicodemus, which at the first came to Jesus by night, and brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight.
text.NABRE: Nicodemus, the one who had first come to him at night, also came bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes weighing about one hundred pounds.
text.DRB: Nicodemus also came, he that at the first came to Jesus by night, bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pounds.

text.NRSV: They took the body of Jesus and wrapped it with the spices in linen cloths, according to the burial custom of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: So they received the body of Jesus, and bound it in linen-bandages with the spices,—just as it is, a custom, with the Jews to prepare for burial.
text.KJV: Then took they the body of Jesus, and wound it in linen clothes with the spices, as the manner of the Jews is to bury.
text.NABRE: They took the body of Jesus and bound it with burial cloths along with the spices, according to the Jewish burial custom.
text.DRB: They took therefore the body of Jesus, and bound it in linen cloths with the spices, as the manner is with the Jews to bury.

text.NRSV: Now there was a garden in the place where he was crucified, and in the garden there was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
text.Rotherham: Now there was, in the place where he was crucified, a garden; and, in the garden, an unused tomb, wherein, as yet, no one had been laid.
text.KJV: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden; and in the garden a new sepulchre, wherein was never man yet laid.
text.NABRE: Now in the place where he had been crucified there was a garden, and in the garden a new tomb, in which no one had yet been buried.
text.DRB: And there was in the place where he was crucified, a garden: and in the garden a new monument, wherein no man yet had been laid.

text.NRSV: And so, because it was the Jewish day of Preparation, and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus there.
text.Rotherham: So, there, by reason of the preparation of the Jews, because, near, was the tomb, laid they Jesus.
text.KJV: There laid they Jesus therefore because of the Jews’ preparation day; for the sepulchre was nigh at hand.
text.NABRE: So they laid Jesus there because of the Jewish preparation day; for the tomb was close by.
text.DRB: There therefore because of the Parasceve of the Jews, they laid Jesus, because the monument was hard by.

text.NRSV: Early on the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been removed from the tomb.
text.Rotherham: But, on the first day of the week, Mary the Magdalene, cometh early, while it is yet, dark, unto the tomb,—and beholdeth the stone, already taken away out of the tomb.
text.KJV: The first day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre.
text.NABRE: On the first day of the week, Mary of Magdala came to the tomb early in the morning, while it was still dark, and saw the stone removed from the tomb.
text.DRB: And the first of the Sabboth, Mary Magdalene cometh early, when it was yet dark, unto the monument: and she saw the stone taken away from the monument.

text.NRSV: So she ran and went to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid him.”
text.Rotherham: She runneth, therefore, and cometh unto Simon Peter, and unto the other disciple whom Jesus dearly loved, and saith unto them—They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we know not where they have laid him.
text.KJV: Then she runneth, and cometh to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple, whom Jesus loved, and saith unto them, They have taken away the Lord out of the sepulchre, and we know not where they have laid him.
text.NABRE: So she ran and went to Simon Peter and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and told them, “They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we don’t know where they put him.”
text.DRB: She ran therefore and cometh to Simon Peter, and to the other Disciple whom Jesus loved, and saith to them: They have taken our Lord out of the monument, and we know not where they have laid him.

text.NRSV: Then Peter and the other disciple set out and went toward the tomb.
text.Rotherham: Peter, therefore, went out, and the other disciple,—and they were going unto the tomb;
text.KJV: Peter therefore went forth, and that other disciple, and came to the sepulchre.
text.NABRE: So Peter and the other disciple went out and came to the tomb.
text.DRB: Peter therefore went forth and that other Disciple, and they came to the monument.

text.NRSV: The two were running together, but the other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first.
text.Rotherham: and the two were running together, and, the other disciple, outran Peter, and came first unto the tomb;
text.KJV: So they ran both together: and the other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the sepulchre.
text.NABRE: They both ran, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and arrived at the tomb first;
text.DRB: And both ran together, and that other Disciple did out-run Peter, and came first to the monument.

text.NRSV: He bent down to look in and saw the linen wrappings lying there, but he did not go in.
text.Rotherham: and, stooping aside, beholdeth, lying, the linen-bandages, nevertheless, he entered not.
text.KJV: And he stooping down, and looking in, saw the linen clothes lying; yet went he not in.
text.NABRE: he bent down and saw the burial cloths there, but did not go in.
text.DRB: And when he had stooped down, he saw the linen cloths lying: but yet he went not in.

text.NRSV: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and went into the tomb. He saw the linen wrappings lying there,
text.Rotherham: So Simon Peter also cometh, following him, and entered into the tomb, and vieweth the linen-bandages lying,—
text.KJV: Then cometh Simon Peter following him, and went into the sepulchre, and seeth the linen clothes lie,
text.NABRE: When Simon Peter arrived after him, he went into the tomb and saw the burial cloths there,
text.DRB: Simon Peter therefore cometh, following him, and went into the monument and saw the linen cloths lying,

text.NRSV: and the cloth that had been on Jesus’ head, not lying with the linen wrappings but rolled up in a place by itself.
text.Rotherham: and the napkin, which was upon his head, not, with the linen-bandages, lying, but apart, folded up into one place.
text.KJV: And the napkin, that was about his head, not lying with the linen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by itself.
text.NABRE: and the cloth that had covered his head, not with the burial cloths but rolled up in a separate place.
text.DRB: and the napkin that had been upon his head, not lying with the linen cloths, but apart, wrapped up into one place.

text.NRSV: Then the other disciple, who reached the tomb first, also went in, and he saw and believed;
text.Rotherham: Then, entered, therefore, the other disciple also, who had come first unto the tomb, and he saw and believed.
text.KJV: Then went in also that other disciple, which came first to the sepulchre, and he saw, and believed.
text.NABRE: Then the other disciple also went in, the one who had arrived at the tomb first, and he saw and believed.
text.DRB: Then therefore went in that other Disciple also which came first to the monument: and he saw, and believed.

text.NRSV: for as yet they did not understand the scripture, that he must rise from the dead.
text.Rotherham: For, not yet, knew they the Scripture, that he must needs, from among the dead, arise.
text.KJV: For as yet they knew not the scripture, that he must rise again from the dead.
text.NABRE: For they did not yet understand the scripture that he had to rise from the dead.
text.DRB: For as yet they knew not the scripture, that he should rise again from the dead.

text.NRSV: Then the disciples returned to their homes.
text.Rotherham: The disciples, therefore, departed again, by themselves.
text.KJV: Then the disciples went away again unto their own home.
text.NABRE: Then the disciples returned home. The Appearance to Mary of Magdala.
text.DRB: The Disciples therefore departed again to themselves.

text.NRSV: But Mary stood weeping outside the tomb. As she wept, she bent over to look into the tomb;
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, Mary, remained standing against the tomb, outside, weeping. So then, as she wept, she stooped aside into the tomb,
text.KJV: But Mary stood without at the sepulchre weeping: and as she wept, she stooped down, and looked into the sepulchre,
text.NABRE: But Mary stayed outside the tomb weeping. And as she wept, she bent over into the tomb
text.DRB: But Mary stood at the monument without, weeping. Therefore as she was weeping, she stooped down, & looked into the monument:

text.NRSV: and she saw two angels in white, sitting where the body of Jesus had been lying, one at the head and the other at the feet.
text.Rotherham: and beholdeth two messengers, in white garments, sitting, one at the head, and the other at the feet, where had been lying the body of Jesus.
text.KJV: And seeth two angels in white sitting, the one at the head, and the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
text.NABRE: and saw two angels in white sitting there, one at the head and one at the feet where the body of Jesus had been.
text.DRB: and she saw two Angels in white, sitting, one at the head, and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had been laid.

text.NRSV: They said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “They have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid him.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, say unto her—Woman! why weepest thou? She saith unto them—They have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.
text.KJV: And they say unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? She saith unto them, Because they have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.
text.NABRE: And they said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “They have taken my Lord, and I don’t know where they laid him.”
text.DRB: They say to her: Woman, why weepest thou? She saith to them: Because they have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have put him.

text.NRSV: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she did not know that it was Jesus.
text.Rotherham: These things saying, she turned round, and seeth Jesus standing, and knew not that it was, Jesus.
text.KJV: And when she had thus said, she turned herself back, and saw Jesus standing, and knew not that it was Jesus.
text.NABRE: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus there, but did not know it was Jesus.
text.DRB: When she had said thus, she turned backward, and saw Jesus standing; and she knew not that it is Jesus.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you looking for?” Supposing him to be the gardener, she said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Woman! why weepest thou? Whom seekest thou? She, supposing he was, the gardener, saith unto him—Sir! If, thou, hast borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him; and, I, will take him away.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? She, supposing him to be the gardener, saith unto him, Sir, if thou have borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you looking for?” She thought it was the gardener and said to him, “Sir, if you carried him away, tell me where you laid him, and I will take him.”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to her: Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? She thinking that it was the gardener, saith to him: Sir, if thou hast carried him away, tell me where thou hast laid him; and I will take him away.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Mary!” She turned and said to him in Hebrew, “Rabbouni!” (which means Teacher).
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Mary! She, turning saith unto him, in Hebrew—Rabboni! which meaneth, Teacher.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto her, Mary. She turned herself, and saith unto him, Rabboni; which is to say, Master.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to her, “Mary!” She turned and said to him in Hebrew, “Rabbouni,” which means Teacher.
text.DRB: Jesus saith to her: Mary. She turning saith to him: Rabboni (which is to say, Master.)

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Do not hold on to me, because I have not yet ascended to the Father. But go to my brothers and say to them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Be not detaining me, for, not yet, have I ascended unto the Father; but be going unto my disciples, and say unto them—I am ascending unto my Father and your Father, and my God and your God.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto her, Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended to my Father: but go to my brethren, and say unto them, I ascend unto my Father, and your Father; and to my God, and your God.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to her, “Stop holding on to me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father. But go to my brothers and tell them, ‘I am going to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
text.DRB: Jesus said to her: Do not touch me, for I am not yet ascended to my Father: but go to my Brethren, and say to them, I ascend to my Father and your Father, my God and your God.

text.NRSV: Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord”; and she told them that he had said these things to her.
text.Rotherham: Mary the Magdalene cometh, bringing tidings unto the disciples—I have seen the Lord! and that these things he had said unto her.
text.KJV: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had spoken these things unto her.
text.NABRE: Mary of Magdala went and announced to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord,” and what he told her. Appearance to the Disciples.
text.DRB: Mary Magdalene cometh and telleth the Disciples, That I have seen our Lord, and thus he said unto me.

text.NRSV: When it was evening on that day, the first day of the week, and the doors of the house where the disciples had met were locked for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”
text.Rotherham: It being late, therefore, on that day, the first of the week,—and, the doors, having been made fast where the disciples were, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came, and stood in the midst, and saith unto them—Peace be unto you!
text.KJV: Then the same day at evening, being the first day of the week, when the doors were shut where the disciples were assembled for fear of the Jews, came Jesus and stood in the midst, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
text.NABRE: On the evening of that first day of the week, when the doors were locked, where the disciples were, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in their midst and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
text.DRB: Therefore when it was late that day, the first of the Sabboths, and the doors were shut, where the Disciples were gathered together for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the midst, and saith to them: Peace be to you.

text.NRSV: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. Then the disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and, this, saying he pointed out both his hands and his side unto them. The disciples, therefore, rejoiced, seeing the Lord.
text.KJV: And when he had so said, he shewed unto them his hands and his side. Then were the disciples glad, when they saw the Lord.
text.NABRE: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. The disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
text.DRB: And when he had said this, he shewed them his hands and side. The Disciples therefore were glad when they saw our Lord.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them again, “Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, so I send you.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto them, again,—Peace be unto you! Just as, my Father, sent me forth, I, also, send you.
text.KJV: Then said Jesus to them again, Peace be unto you: as my Father hath sent me, even so send I you.
text.NABRE: [Jesus] said to them again, “Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, so I send you.”
text.DRB: He said therefore to them again: Peace be to you. As my Father hath sent me, I also do send you.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, he breathed on them and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: And, this, saying, he breathed strongly, and saith unto them—Receive ye Holy Spirit:—
text.KJV: And when he had said this, he breathed on them, and saith unto them, Receive ye the Holy Ghost:
text.NABRE: And when he had said this, he breathed on them and said to them, “Receive the holy Spirit.
text.DRB: When he had said this: he breathed upon them; and he said to them: Receive ye the Holy Ghost:

text.NRSV: If you forgive the sins of any, they are forgiven them; if you retain the sins of any, they are retained.”
text.Rotherham: Whosesoever sins ye shall remit, they are remitted unto them, whosesoever ye shall retain, they are retained.
text.KJV: Whose soever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained.
text.NABRE: Whose sins you forgive are forgiven them, and whose sins you retain are retained.”
text.DRB: Whose Sins You Shall Forgive, They Are Forgiven Them: And Whose You Shall Retain, They Are Retained.

text.NRSV: But Thomas (who was called the Twin), one of the twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.
text.Rotherham: But, Thomas, one of the twelve, the one called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.
text.KJV: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.
text.NABRE: Thomas. Thomas, called Didymus, one of the Twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.
text.DRB: But Thomas one of the Twelve, who is called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.

text.NRSV: So the other disciples told him, “We have seen the Lord.” But he said to them, “Unless I see the mark of the nails in his hands, and put my finger in the mark of the nails and my hand in his side, I will not believe.”
text.Rotherham: The other disciples, therefore, were saying unto him—We have seen the Lord! But, he, said unto them—Except I see, in his hands, the print of the nails, and press my finger into the print of the nails, and press my hand into his side, in nowise will I believe.
text.KJV: The other disciples therefore said unto him, We have seen the Lord. But he said unto them, Except I shall see in his hands the print of the nails, and put my finger into the print of the nails, and thrust my hand into his side, I will not believe.
text.NABRE: So the other disciples said to him, “We have seen the Lord.” But he said to them, “Unless I see the mark of the nails in his hands and put my finger into the nailmarks and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.”
text.DRB: The other Disciples therefore said to him: We have seen our Lord. But he said to them: unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, and put my finger into the place of the nails, and put my hand into his side: I will not believe.

text.NRSV: A week later his disciples were again in the house, and Thomas was with them. Although the doors were shut, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”
text.Rotherham: And, eight days after, his disciples again were within, and Thomas with them. Jesus cometh—the doors having been made fast—and stood in the midst, and said—Peace be unto you!
text.KJV: And after eight days again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them: then came Jesus, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst, and said, Peace be unto you.
text.NABRE: Now a week later his disciples were again inside and Thomas was with them. Jesus came, although the doors were locked, and stood in their midst and said, “Peace be with you.”
text.DRB: And after eight days, again his Disciples were within; and Thomas with them. Jesus cometh the doors being shut, and stood in the midst, and said: Peace be to you.

text.NRSV: Then he said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and see my hands. Reach out your hand and put it in my side. Do not doubt but believe.”
text.Rotherham: Then, saith he unto Thomas—Reach thy finger hither, and see my hands, and reach thy hand, and press into my side,—and become not disbelieving, but believing.
text.KJV: Then saith he to Thomas, Reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into my side: and be not faithless, but believing.
text.NABRE: Then he said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and see my hands, and bring your hand and put it into my side, and do not be unbelieving, but believe.”
text.DRB: Then he saith to Thomas: Put in thy finger hither, and see my hands, and bring hither thy hand, and put it into my side; & be not incredulous but faithful.

text.NRSV: Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!”
text.Rotherham: Thomas answered, and said unto him—My Lord, and my God!
text.KJV: And Thomas answered and said unto him, My Lord and my God.
text.NABRE: Thomas answered and said to him, “My Lord and my God!”
text.DRB: Thomas answered, & said to him: My Lord, & my God.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Have you believed because you have seen me? Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have come to believe.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—Because thou hast seen me, hast thou believed? Happy, they who have not seen, and yet have believed!
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “Have you come to believe because you have seen me? Blessed are those who have not seen and have believed.” Conclusion.
text.DRB: Jesus saith to him: Because thou hast seen me, Thomas, thou hast believed: Blessed are they that have not seen & have believed.

text.NRSV: Now Jesus did many other signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book.
text.Rotherham: Many other signs, doubtless, did Jesus, in presence of the disciples, which are not written in this book;
text.KJV: And many other signs truly did Jesus in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book:
text.NABRE: Now Jesus did many other signs in the presence of [his] disciples that are not written in this book.
text.DRB: Many other signs also did Jesus in the sight of his Disciples, which are not written in this Book.

text.NRSV: But these are written so that you may come to believe that Jesus is the Messiah, the Son of God, and that through believing you may have life in his name.
text.Rotherham: but, these, are written, that ye may believe that, Jesus, is, the Christ, the Son of God; and that, believing, ye may have life in his name.
text.KJV: But these are written, that ye might believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; and that believing ye might have life through his name.
text.NABRE: But these are written that you may [come to] believe that Jesus is the Messiah, the Son of God, and that through this belief you may have life in his name.
text.DRB: And these are written, that you may believe that Jesus is Christ the Son of God: and that believing, you may have life in his name.

text.NRSV: After these things Jesus showed himself again to the disciples by the Sea of Tiberias; and he showed himself in this way.
text.Rotherham: After these things, Jesus manifested himself again, unto the disciples, by the sea of Tiberias; and manifested himself, thus:—
text.KJV: After these things Jesus shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias; and on this wise shewed he himself.
text.NABRE: After this, Jesus revealed himself again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. He revealed himself in this way.
text.DRB: After Jesus manifested himself again to the Disciples at the sea of Tiberias. And he manifested thus.

text.NRSV: Gathered there together were Simon Peter, Thomas called the Twin, Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples.
text.Rotherham: There were together, Simon Peter, and Thomas, who was called Didymus, and Nathanael, who was from Cana of Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and, two other, of his disciples.
text.KJV: There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two other of his disciples.
text.NABRE: Together were Simon Peter, Thomas called Didymus, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedee’s sons, and two others of his disciples.
text.DRB: There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas who is called Didymus, and Nathanael which was of Cana in Galilee, & the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his Disciples.

text.NRSV: Simon Peter said to them, “I am going fishing.” They said to him, “We will go with you.” They went out and got into the boat, but that night they caught nothing.
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter saith unto them—I go a fishing! They say unto him—We also, go with thee! They went out, and got up into the boat, and during that night, they caught nothing.
text.KJV: Simon Peter saith unto them, I go a fishing. They say unto him, We also go with thee. They went forth, and entered into a ship immediately; and that night they caught nothing.
text.NABRE: Simon Peter said to them, “I am going fishing.” They said to him, “We also will come with you.” So they went out and got into the boat, but that night they caught nothing.
text.DRB: Simon Peter saith to them: I go to fish. They say to him: We also come with thee. And they went forth and got up into the boat: and that night they took nothing.

text.NRSV: Just after daybreak, Jesus stood on the beach; but the disciples did not know that it was Jesus.
text.Rotherham: But, morning by this time drawing on, Jesus stood upon the beach; nevertheless, the disciples knew not that it was, Jesus.
text.KJV: But when the morning was now come, Jesus stood on the shore: but the disciples knew not that it was Jesus.
text.NABRE: When it was already dawn, Jesus was standing on the shore; but the disciples did not realize that it was Jesus.
text.DRB: But when morning was now come, Jesus stood on the shore: yet the Disciples knew not that it was Jesus.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Children, you have no fish, have you?” They answered him, “No.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, saith unto them—Children! perhaps ye have nothing to eat? They answered him—No.
text.KJV: Then Jesus saith unto them, Children, have ye any meat? They answered him, No.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Children, have you caught anything to eat?” They answered him, “No.”
text.DRB: Jesus therefore saith to them: Children, have you any meat? They answered him, No.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Cast the net to the right side of the boat, and you will find some.” So they cast it, and now they were not able to haul it in because there were so many fish.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Cast the net, on the right side of the boat,—and ye shall find. They cast, therefore; and, no longer, were they able, to draw, it, for the multitude of the fishes.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find. They cast therefore, and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes.
text.NABRE: So he said to them, “Cast the net over the right side of the boat and you will find something.” So they cast it, and were not able to pull it in because of the number of fish.
text.DRB: He saith to them: Cast the net on the right side of the boat; and you shall find. They therefore did cast it: and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes.

text.NRSV: That disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It is the Lord!” When Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he put on some clothes, for he was naked, and jumped into the sea.
text.Rotherham: That disciple, therefore, whom Jesus loved, saith unto Peter—It is, the Lord! Simon Peter, therefore, hearing that it was the Lord, girded about him, his upper garment,—for he was naked; and cast himself into the sea;
text.KJV: Therefore that disciple whom Jesus loved saith unto Peter, It is the Lord. Now when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he girt his fisher’s coat unto him, (for he was naked,) and did cast himself into the sea.
text.NABRE: So the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It is the Lord.” When Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he tucked in his garment, for he was lightly clad, and jumped into the sea.
text.DRB: That Disciples therefore whom Jesus loved, saith to Peter: It is our Lord. Simon Peter when he had heard that it is our Lord, girded his coat unto him (for he was naked) & cast himself into the sea.

text.NRSV: But the other disciples came in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they were not far from the land, only about a hundred yards off.
text.Rotherham: but, the other disciples, came, by the little boat,—for they were not farther from the land than about two hundred cubits off,—dragging the net of fishes.
text.KJV: And the other disciples came in a little ship; (for they were not far from land, but as it were two hundred cubits,) dragging the net with fishes.
text.NABRE: The other disciples came in the boat, for they were not far from shore, only about a hundred yards, dragging the net with the fish.
text.DRB: But the other Disciples came in the boat (for they were not far from the land, but as it were two hundred cubits) drawing the net of fishes.

text.NRSV: When they had gone ashore, they saw a charcoal fire there, with fish on it, and bread.
text.Rotherham: So, when they got out upon the land, they behold a coal fire lying, and fish lying over, and bread.
text.KJV: As soon then as they were come to land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid thereon, and bread.
text.NABRE: When they climbed out on shore, they saw a charcoal fire with fish on it and bread.
text.DRB: Therefore after they came down to land, they saw hot coals lying, and fish laid thereon, and bread.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish that you have just caught.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto them—Bring of the fish which ye caught just now.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto them, Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish you just caught.”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to them: Bring hither of the fishes that you took now.

text.NRSV: So Simon Peter went aboard and hauled the net ashore, full of large fish, a hundred fifty-three of them; and though there were so many, the net was not torn.
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter, therefore, went on board, and drew the net on to the land,—full of large fishes, a hundred and fifty-three; and, though they were so many, the net was not rent.
text.KJV: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land full of great fishes, an hundred and fifty and three: and for all there were so many, yet was not the net broken.
text.NABRE: So Simon Peter went over and dragged the net ashore full of one hundred fifty-three large fish. Even though there were so many, the net was not torn.
text.DRB: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to the land, full of great fishes, an hundred fifty three. And although they were so many the net was not broken.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Come and have breakfast.” Now none of the disciples dared to ask him, “Who are you?” because they knew it was the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto them—Come! break your fast. Not one, of the disciples was venturing to ask him, Who art, thou? knowing that it was, the Lord.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto them, Come and dine. And none of the disciples durst ask him, Who art thou? knowing that it was the Lord.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Come, have breakfast.” And none of the disciples dared to ask him, “Who are you?” because they realized it was the Lord.
text.DRB: Jesus saith to them: Come, dine. And none of them that sat at meat, durst ask him: Who art thou? knowing that it is our Lord.

text.NRSV: Jesus came and took the bread and gave it to them, and did the same with the fish.
text.Rotherham: Jesus cometh, and taketh the bread, and giveth unto them; and the fish, in like manner.
text.KJV: Jesus then cometh, and taketh bread, and giveth them, and fish likewise.
text.NABRE: Jesus came over and took the bread and gave it to them, and in like manner the fish.
text.DRB: And Jesus cometh & taketh the bread and giveth them, & the fish in like manner.

text.NRSV: This was now the third time that Jesus appeared to the disciples after he was raised from the dead.
text.Rotherham: This, already, is the third time Jesus was manifested unto the disciples, after he was raised from among the dead.
text.KJV: This is now the third time that Jesus shewed himself to his disciples, after that he was risen from the dead.
text.NABRE: This was now the third time Jesus was revealed to his disciples after being raised from the dead. Jesus and Peter.
text.DRB: This now the third time Jesus was manifested to his Disciples, after he was risen from the dead.

text.NRSV: When they had finished breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon son of John, do you love me more than these?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I love you.” Jesus said to him, “Feed my lambs.”
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, they had broken their fast, Jesus saith unto Simon Peter—Simon, son of John! lovest thou me more than these? He saith unto him—Yea, Lord! thou, knowest that I am fond of thee, he saith unto him—Be feeding my lambs.
text.KJV: So when they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me more than these? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my lambs.
text.NABRE: When they had finished breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon, son of John, do you love me more than these?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord, you know that I love you.” He said to him, “Feed my lambs.”
text.DRB: Therefore when they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter: Simon of John, lovest thou me more than these? He saith to him: Yea Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith to him: Feed my Lambs.

text.NRSV: A second time he said to him, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I love you.” Jesus said to him, “Tend my sheep.”
text.Rotherham: He saith unto him, again, the second time—Simon, son of John! lovest thou me? He saith unto him—Yea, Lord! thou, knowest that I am fond of thee. He saith unto him—Be shepherding my sheep.
text.KJV: He saith to him again the second time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my sheep.
text.NABRE: He then said to him a second time, “Simon, son of John, do you love me?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord, you know that I love you.” He said to him, “Tend my sheep.”
text.DRB: He saith to him again: Simon of John, lovest thou me? He saith to him: Yea Lord, thou knowest that I love thee. He saith to him: Feed my Lambs.

text.NRSV: He said to him the third time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter felt hurt because he said to him the third time, “Do you love me?” And he said to him, “Lord, you know everything; you know that I love you.” Jesus said to him, “Feed my sheep.
text.Rotherham: He saith unto him, the third time—Simon, son of John! art thou fond of me? Peter was grieved, that he said unto him, the third time, Art thou fond of me? and he said unto him—Lord! all things, thou, knowest: thou perceivest that I am fond of thee. Jesus saith unto him—Be feeding my sheep.
text.KJV: He saith unto him the third time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? Peter was grieved because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said unto him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I love thee. Jesus saith unto him, Feed my sheep.
text.NABRE: He said to him the third time, “Simon, son of John, do you love me?” Peter was distressed that he had said to him a third time, “Do you love me?” and he said to him, “Lord, you know everything; you know that I love you.” [Jesus] said to him, “Feed my sheep.
text.DRB: He saith to him the third time: Simon of John, lovest thou me? Peter was stroken sad because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said to him: Lord, thou knowest all things: thou knowest that I love thee. He said to him: Feed my Sheep.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, when you were younger, you used to fasten your own belt and to go wherever you wished. But when you grow old, you will stretch out your hands, and someone else will fasten a belt around you and take you where you do not wish to go.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto thee—When thou wast younger, thou usedst to gird thyself, and to walk whither thou didst choose; but, when thou shalt become aged, thou shalt stretch out thy hands, and, another, shall gird thee, and bear thee, whither thou dost not choose.
text.KJV: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.
text.NABRE: Amen, amen, I say to you, when you were younger, you used to dress yourself and go where you wanted; but when you grow old, you will stretch out your hands, and someone else will dress you and lead you where you do not want to go.”
text.DRB: Amen, amen I say to thee, when thou wast younger, thou didst gird thyself, and didst walk where thou wouldest. But when thou shalt be old thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and lead thee whither thou wilt not.

text.NRSV: (He said this to indicate the kind of death by which he would glorify God.) After this he said to him, “Follow me.”
text.Rotherham: Now, this, he said, signifying, by what manner of death, he should glorify God. And, having said this, he saith unto him—Be following me.
text.KJV: This spake he, signifying by what death he should glorify God. And when he had spoken this, he saith unto him, Follow me.
text.NABRE: He said this signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God. And when he had said this, he said to him, “Follow me.”
text.DRB: And this he said, signifying by what death he should glorify God. And when he had said this, he saith to him: Follow me.

text.NRSV: Peter turned and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them; he was the one who had reclined next to Jesus at the supper and had said, “Lord, who is it that is going to betray you?”
text.Rotherham: Peter, turning about, beholdeth the disciple whom Jesus loved, following,—who also reclined during the supper upon his breast, and said—Lord, who is it that is delivering thee up?
text.KJV: Then Peter, turning about, seeth the disciple whom Jesus loved following; which also leaned on his breast at supper, and said, Lord, which is he that betrayeth thee?
text.NABRE: The Beloved Disciple. Peter turned and saw the disciple following whom Jesus loved, the one who had also reclined upon his chest during the supper and had said, “Master, who is the one who will betray you?”
text.DRB: Peter turning, saw that Disciple whom Jesus loved, following, who also leaned at the supper upon his breast, and said, Lord who is he that shall betray thee?

text.NRSV: When Peter saw him, he said to Jesus, “Lord, what about him?”
text.Rotherham: Peter, therefore, seeing, this one, saith unto Jesus—Lord! and, this one, what?
text.KJV: Peter seeing him saith to Jesus, Lord, and what shall this man do?
text.NABRE: When Peter saw him, he said to Jesus, “Lord, what about him?”
text.DRB: Him therefore when Peter had seen, he saith to Jesus: Lord, and this man what?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “If it is my will that he remain until I come, what is that to you? Follow me!”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—If I will that, he, remain until I come, what is that to thee? Thou, be following me.
text.KJV: Jesus saith unto him, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee? follow thou me.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “What if I want him to remain until I come? What concern is it of yours? You follow me.”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to him: So I will have him to remain till I come, what to thee? follow thou me.

text.NRSV: So the rumor spread in the community that this disciple would not die. Yet Jesus did not say to him that he would not die, but, “If it is my will that he remain until I come, what is that to you?”
text.Rotherham: This word, therefore, went forth unto the brethren, that, that disciple, should not die. Howbeit, Jesus did not tell him, he should not die; but, If I will that, he, remain until I come, what is that to thee?
text.KJV: Then went this saying abroad among the brethren, that that disciple should not die: yet Jesus said not unto him, He shall not die; but, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee?
text.NABRE: So the word spread among the brothers that that disciple would not die. But Jesus had not told him that he would not die, just “What if I want him to remain until I come? [What concern is it of yours?]”
text.DRB: This saying therefore went abroad among the brethren, that that Disciple dieth not. And Jesus did not say to him, he dieth not; but, So I will have him to remain till I come, what to thee?

text.NRSV: This is the disciple who is testifying to these things and has written them, and we know that his testimony is true.
text.Rotherham: This, is the disciple who beareth witness concerning these things, and who hath written these things; and we know that, true, is, his witness.
text.KJV: This is the disciple which testifieth of these things, and wrote these things: and we know that his testimony is true.
text.NABRE: Conclusion. It is this disciple who testifies to these things and has written them, and we know that his testimony is true.
text.DRB: This is that Disciple which giveth testimony of these things, and hath written these things: and we know that his testimony is true.

text.NRSV: But there are also many other things that Jesus did; if every one of them were written down, I suppose that the world itself could not contain the books that would be written.
text.Rotherham: Now there are many other things also, which Jesus did, which, indeed, if they were to be written one by one, not even the world, itself, I suppose, would contain, the books which must be written.
text.KJV: And there are also many other things which Jesus did, the which, if they should be written every one, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written. Amen.
text.NABRE: There are also many other things that Jesus did, but if these were to be described individually, I do not think the whole world would contain the books that would be written.
text.DRB: But there are many other things also which Jesus did: which if they were written in particular, neither the world it-self I think were able to contain those books that should be written.
